properties.property_name_en: Berlin Modernism Housing Estates [Extension to include Waldsiedlung Zehlendorf]
properties.property_name_zh: 柏林现代主义住宅区（扩展纳入瓦尔德西德隆·策伦多夫）
properties.category_of_property_source_note: Extension of an already inscribed serial property; one added component part.
properties.brief_synthesis_en: The set of housing estates in the Berlin Modern Style provides outstanding testimony to the implementation of housing policies during the period 1910-1933 and especially during the Weimar Republic, when the city of Berlin was characterized by its political, social, cultural and technical progressiveness. The housing estates reflect, with the highest degree of quality, the combination of urbanism, architecture, garden design and aesthetic research typical of early 20th century modernism, as well as the application of new hygienic and social standards. Some of the most prominent leading architects of German modernism were involved in the design and construction of the properties; they developed innovative urban, building and flat-roof typologies, technical solutions and aesthetic achievements.

properties.property_name_en: Beaches of the D-Day Landings, Normandy, 1944
properties.property_name_zh: 诺曼底登陆海滩，1944年
properties.category_of_property_source_note: category revised during evaluation; cultural landscape proposal withdrawn; Guiding Principles: site of memory; serial: 6 sites
properties.brief_synthesis_en: On 6 June 1944, Operation Neptune, the first phase of Operation Overlord, conceived to open a new Western European Front during the Second World War against the forces of the Third Reich, was launched from beaches on the Normandy coast that have come to be known as the Beaches of the D-Day Landings. Along with the military action and human sacrifice on multiple fronts in Eastern Europe, Northern Africa and the Pacific, the Normandy Landings was one of the decisive moments in the Second World War, when after years of planning, seventeen allied nations collaborated in military, logistical, political, and moral resources as part of a concerted plan to overcome a regime of conquest and genocide. The serial property, comprising six component parts, commemorates the way years of planning and alliancebuilding culminated in collective, multinational, logistical and military efforts that marked the beginning of the end of the Second World War in Western Europe, ultimately contributing to the toppling of a totalitarian regime and paving the way for reconstruction, reconciliation, and a renewed commitment to collective security. The Beaches of the D-Day Landings also embody, symbolise, and evoke memories of the immense human sacrifice made to begin to break the entrenched authoritarian regime of the Third Reich that occupied most of Europe in the mid-20th century. The landing operations, launched on 6 June 1944, took place on five beaches (from west to east): Utah Beach, Omaha Beach, Gold Beach, Juno Beach and Sword Beach, as well as at Pointe du Hoc, the sixth amphibious assault site, constituting altogether the component parts of the serial property. Each site still bears the traces of the event. The serial property contains many tangible attributes: the beaches themselves, the five landing sites of 6 June 1944, and an amphibious assault site; remains of the most remarkable structures illustrating the logistical efforts made to ensure the success of Operation Neptune, particularly the artificial harbour known as Mulberry B; remains of the defensive system or “Atlantic Wall” that the Allies faced; traces left by the confrontation in the landscape, deliberately preserved as a reminder of the event; memorial sites created since 1944; memorial monuments, and finally, the American Military Cemetery at Colleville-sur-Mer, built in the immediate vicinity of Omaha Beach. They also contain intangible attributes, memories of the event to which they are intrinsically linked, having acquired strong symbolic significance. In paying tribute to the victims of the confrontation, the Beaches of the D-Day Landings have also become a place of reconciliation between former belligerents.
properties.brief_synthesis_zh: ICOMOS推荐OUV声明中的Brief synthesis原文已抽取；中文页面可在此基础上进行工作译文和审校。

properties.property_name_en: Aalto Works
properties.property_name_zh: 阿尔托作品
properties.brief_synthesis_en: The Aalto Works comprise thirteen modern buildings and building ensembles designed between 1928 and 1988 by Alvar Aalto in collaboration with his first spouse Aino Marsio-Aalto and second spouse Elissa Aalto, as well as the employees of the Aalto studio (1923-1994) and Artek (founded 1935), their affiliated interior design and furnishings company. Located in both urban and rural areas of Finland, the serial property bears witness to the application of global principles of the 20th-century Modern Movement, as a response to new societal and universal needs. Strongly rooted in local building traditions, the Aalto Works are characterised by a comprehensive and individualistic design approach driven by an empathetic, site-specific, and human-centred vision, where each project is unique and responds to specific programmatic requirements. The serial property showcases the Aaltos’ experiments in construction technology, architectural aesthetics, spatial sequences, lighting, and the use of both natural and industrial materials. Each building is carefully integrated into its surroundings. This synthetic design approach also encompassed interior design, merging industrial elements with local traditional materials. Most of the buildings still feature their original collections of furniture and lighting designed by the Aalto studio. 195

properties.property_name_en: Bulwarked Fortresses of the Raia
properties.property_name_zh: 拉亚棱堡防御工事
properties.brief_synthesis_en:
Located along the Raia, the historic boundary between Portugal and Spain, the Bulwarked Fortresses of the Raia form a defence system created during the Portuguese Restoration War (1640-1668), comprising 143 recorded fortifications spread over roughly 1,300 kilometres. The nominated property is a series constituted of three fortifications: the Stronghold of Almeida and the Fortresses of Marvão and Valença. Each includes its fortified perimeter, military buildings and the urban area enclosed by its defensive lines.

The Stronghold of Almeida is a star-shaped bulwarked fort on a strategic hill above the River Côa, distinguished by its six bulwarks, advanced outer works and the fortified town enclosed within. The Fortress of Marvão combines a commanding mountaintop setting with medieval and modern bulwarked structures, illustrating the integration of natural topography and evolving defensive architecture. The Fortress of Valença is a bulwarked stronghold, with two interconnected enclosures, overlooking the River Minho, notable for its scale and integration of modern military architecture with the topography of the site.
properties.brief_synthesis_zh:
该项目位于 Raia，即葡萄牙与西班牙之间的历史边界沿线。Raia 堡垒体系形成于葡萄牙复国战争时期（1640-1668），由约 143 处有记录的防御工事组成，分布范围约 1,300 公里。申报遗产为由三处防御工事组成的系列遗产：Almeida 要塞、Marvão 要塞和 Valença 要塞；每个组成部分包括其防御边界、军事建筑以及防线围合的城区。

Almeida 要塞是位于 Côa 河上方战略丘陵上的星形棱堡，具有六个棱堡、外部防御工程和被防御体系围合的城镇。Marvão 要塞结合了山顶控制性地形、中世纪和近代棱堡结构，体现自然地形与防御建筑演变的结合。Valença 要塞由两个相互连接的防御围合区构成，俯瞰 Minho 河，以其规模以及现代军事建筑与地形的整合为特征。
properties.state_party_proposed_name: Bulwarked Fortresses of the Raia

properties.property_name_en: Zerzevan Castle and Mithraeum
properties.property_name_zh: 泽尔泽万城堡和密特拉神庙
properties.brief_synthesis_en: Zerzevan Castle and Mithraeum is an archaeological hilltop complex located between Diyarbakır and Mardin in Southeastern Anatolia, Türkiye. The nominated property presents an archaeological ensemble reflecting multiple phases of occupation and adaptation over a long span of time, extending from the Assyrian Period (882-611 BCE) through the Roman and Eastern Roman periods until the conquest of the region by Islamic armies in 639 CE. It comprises the remains of a fortified complex, including defensive walls, towers, gates, domestic quarters, a large cistern system, a church, administrative buildings, rock-cut tombs in associated necropolises, quarries, and other service structures. At the northern end of the fort, excavations have revealed a rock-cut underground space referred to as a Mithraeum, (a temple dedicated to the Roman god Mithras), together with an adjacent underground structure carved into the bedrock. The nominated property extends over a rocky ridge rising above the surrounding plain and incorporates both built and rock-cut elements. The wider setting includes remains associated with water management and circulation routes, including the strategic Late Antiquity Amida-Dara road.
properties.brief_synthesis_zh: Zerzevan Castle and Mithraeum 位于 Türkiye 东南安纳托利亚 Diyarbakır 与 Mardin 之间，是一处山顶考古遗址群。申报遗产呈现从亚述时期（公元前 882-611 年）经罗马、东罗马时期至公元 639 年伊斯兰军队征服该地区之前的长期占用与适应过程。遗存包括防御墙、塔楼、城门、居住区、大型蓄水系统、教堂、行政建筑、相关墓地中的岩凿墓、采石场和其他服务性结构。堡垒北端发现了被称为 Mithraeum 的岩凿地下空间（供奉罗马神 Mithras 的神庙）及相邻岩凿地下结构。遗产位于高出周边平原的岩质山脊上，包含建筑遗存和岩凿要素；更广阔的环境还包括与水管理和交通路线相关的遗存，如 Late Antiquity Amida-Dara road。
properties.state_party_proposed_name: Zerzevan Castle and Mithraeum

properties.property_name_en: The System of Fortresses of the Seneschalty of Carcassonne (13th-14th centuries)
properties.property_name_zh: 卡尔卡松总管辖区防御工事体系（13-14世纪）
properties.brief_synthesis_en:
The regional defensive fortification system established by the Capetian kings in the 13th-14th centuries in the former seneschalty of Carcassonne, in current south-west France, consisted of twenty-two fortresses built to protect the seneschal's headquarters at Carcassonne, secure newly annexed lands, safeguard economic resources and control key communication routes, as well as assert royal power in the region through monumental architecture. This serial property, composed of eight component parts, comprises a representative sample of this system and includes the Carcassonne Castle with its double concentric ramparts, which served as the administrative and military centre of the seneschalty, as well as an ensemble of watchmen fortresses built in a relatively short span of time under royal supervision in a rugged topography.

The system of the fortresses of the seneschalty of Carcassonne, stylistically consistent, was designed based on the Capetian military architecture models, developed for the plains of Ile-de-France and adapted to the rocky topography of the Montagne Noire and the Pyrenean foothills. The serial property represents a compelling example of this defensive architecture, which served as a political tool to project the growing, centralised and divinely sanctioned power of the French monarchy, part of a larger movement towards assertion of sovereign powers that marked 13th-century Europe.
properties.brief_synthesis_zh: 报告第 7 部分推荐 OUV 声明中的 Brief synthesis 原文已补齐为两段。中文可在此基础上继续工作译校。
properties.state_party_proposed_name: The System of Fortresses of the Seneschalty of Carcassonne (13th-14th centuries)

properties.property_name_en: Pátzcuaro, Site of Humanistic Memory and Cultural Confluence
Ancient Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara
properties.property_name_zh: 飞鸟和藤原古都
properties.brief_synthesis_en: The Ancient Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara are a serial property of nineteen component parts that are associated with two consecutive early capitals on the Japanese archipelago established through interchanges with kingdoms in China and the Korean Peninsula. Located in the Nara Basin, these sites attest to the formative processes of the emergence and establishment of 102 centralised state systems in East Asia, modelled on the Chinese ritsuryo system. The capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara influenced the construction and evolution of subsequent capitals in Japan, including the Nara Capital in the 8th century and the Heian Capital in Kyoto in the 9th century. The layout, design, technology, and planning of the two capitals were a result of a fusion between local traditions and adoption of culture and technology from other East Asian powers. The nineteen component parts have been selected to represent three tangible and spatial dimensions of these capitals: palace/government office sites, Buddhist temple sites, and mounded tombs. The sites associated with the Asuka capital (588-694 CE) reflect the initial stages of the implementation of the centralised state and feature the concentration of political power in the palace and the gradual addition of governing facilities. The Asuka-dera Temple (component part 005) was the first Buddhist temple in the Japanese archipelago and marks the introduction of Buddhism. Private temples and new types of mounded tombs began to appear in areas under the influence of powerful clans. The sites of the subsequent capital at Fujiwara (694-710 CE) represent the completion of this stage of central power development. The palace, including government offices, was positioned at the centre of the capital, with state temples and the systematic arrangement of tombs.
properties.brief_synthesis_zh: ICOMOS推荐OUV声明中的Brief synthesis原文已抽取；中文页面可在此基础上进行工作译文和审校。
properties.state_party_proposed_name: Ancient Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara

properties.property_name_en: The Cemetery Complexes of the Xiongnu Nobility
properties.property_name_zh: 匈奴贵族墓葬群
properties.brief_synthesis_en: This serial property comprises eight funerary complexes of the Xiongnu nobility. The Xiongnu, ancient predecessors of a confederation of Eurasian nomadic peoples, established a powerful empire between the 3rd century BCE and the 2nd century CE that occupied a vast territory centred on what is now the modern state of Mongolia. They interacted with the Han Chinese and controlled nodes along the main parts of the Steppe Route of the Silk Roads. Among the numerous archaeological traces throughout the area occupied by the empire are their cemeteries, which provide insights into their burial culture. Of substantial size and depth, the main burials are accompanied by satellite graves (with small ring-shaped mounds) as well as sacrificial structures (lines of stone pairs). A wide range of artefacts found in the tombs, including horse tack and chariots as well as weapons, tools and food for the afterlife, provides a window into the Xiongnu way of life and social structure. The tombs of their nobility highlight the engineering and organisational skills of this mainly nomadic culture.

properties.property_name_en: Rock Mosques and Associated Sacred Sites of Mangystau
properties.property_name_zh: 曼格斯套岩石清真寺及相关圣地
properties.brief_synthesis_en: The Rock Mosques and Associated Sacred Sites of Mangystau in Kazakhstan comprise four sacred complexes carved into the landscape alongside extensive burial grounds. Dating from the 12th to the 14th centuries, Shopan-Ata, Karaman-Ata, Shakpak-Ata, and Sultan-Epe are each associated with a Sufi master. Emerging from the nomadic traditions of the Mangystau and Ustyurt plateaus, these sites represent a culture where Islamic Sufi spirituality evolved from ancient practices of ancestor worship, landscape sacralisation, and reverence for spiritual leaders. The complexes include rock-hewn mosques, courtyards, water sources, sacred trees, pathways, and cemeteries spanning from the 10th to the 20th centuries. Locally known as “underground mosques,” these modest and architecturally diverse rock carved spaces are formed within natural caves, steep rock faces, or excavated earth across the semi-desert Mangystau Peninsula bordering the Caspian Sea. These unique spaces range from simple structures to intricate spaces featuring prayer halls, mihrabs, contemplation rooms, multifunctional areas, and tombs of saints. Central to the property is the veneration of Sufi saints within the Yasawi Order, a tradition sustained over centuries by shirakshi (grave guardians) and ongoing pilgrimage rituals.

properties.property_name_en: Wat Phra Mahathat Woramahawihan, Nakhon Si Thammarat
properties.property_name_zh: 帕玛哈泰沃拉玛哈维汉寺，洛坤府
properties.brief_synthesis_en: Wat Phra Mahathat Woramahawihan (or Phra Mahathat monastery) is a Theravada Buddhist monastery located in the Nakhon Si Thammarat Province in Peninsular Thailand, a crucial trade crossroad between the Indian and Pacific Oceans. Founded in the 8th century, where a Hindu shrine once stood (5th-8th centuries), the monastery has been a regional religious centre ever since. The complex is centred on the Great Reliquary of Buddha in the form of a mandala (Buddhavasa), flanked by the residential quarters of the monks (Sanghavasa) to the north and south according to the Tipiṭaka (Pali Canon). It comprises religious buildings, including the Great Reliquary with satellite stupas, various functional structures, two Bodhi tree shrines and the sacred Crystal Beach, on which the monastery is built, as well as residential buildings in the monastery. 164 Bearing witness to the fusion of Brahmanism, Hinduism and the Mahayana and Theravada Buddhism of Central and Southeast Asia, the combined artistic and architectural expressions found at Wat Phra Mahathat, imbued with local and religious traditions, had a lasting influence on building construction in the region.

properties.property_name_en: The system of Italian-style condominio theatres of the 18th and 19th centuries in Central Italy
properties.property_name_zh: 意大利中部18至19世纪意大利式公寓剧院体系
properties.brief_synthesis_en: The condominio theatre represents a major evolution in the Italian-style theatre model around the mid-18th and late 19th centuries, marking the transition from being a place for aristocratic entertainment to becoming a cultural institution driven by an emerging bourgeoisie. Based on a unique partnership between public bodies and groups of wealthy citizens, known as società condominiali, this economic model is founded on collective ownership and the sharing of costs. This management structure enabled the wide spread of theatres, particularly in small and medium-sized towns in Italy. By making the financers of theatre projects partners in their design, the condominio model turned these theatres into spaces for architectural experimentation and as 240 economic driving forces for these small and mediumsized towns. The condominio model thus fostered the development of specific architectural features manifesting themselves in precise adjustments to social spaces (entrance halls, foyer, ridotto), the standardisation of the tiered boxes, the optimisation of sightlines, the development of separate circulation systems, transforming the theatre into a tool of prestige precisely tailored for its users. This model fostered the emergence of spaces for social life where culture belonged to an emerging bourgeoisie. In addition to their artistic purpose, these theatres have also played a role as centres of political awareness, transforming small and medium-sized towns into dynamic social and cultural centres; a function they still retain today.

properties.property_name_en: Jingdezhen Handicraft Porcelain Industry Sites
properties.property_name_zh: 景德镇手工瓷业遗存
properties.brief_synthesis_en:
The Jingdezhen Handicraft Porcelain Industry Sites are located in an area with rich reserves of porcelain stone, kaolin clay, firewood forests and water transportation. Five component parts represent the handicraft porcelain industry system of Jingdezhen from the 10th to the 19th century. Together they exhibit the innovative development of Chinese porcelain making technology and porcelain art that had a profound impact on the development of the ceramic industry of the world.

From the 10th to the 13th centuries integration of the porcelain-making technologies of northern and southern China achieved the stable production of bluish-white porcelain and laid the foundation for Jingdezhen to develop into a Chinese porcelain production centre. In the 13th-15th centuries, innovations included the “binary formula” (mixing porcelain stone with kaolin), new kiln forms, and the use of imported cobalt pigments to produce the blue-and-white porcelain. From the 16th century, Jingdezhen was the handicraft porcelain production centre of the world, supported by a complex industrial structure that connected porcelain manufacturing and sales centres with raw material and firewood production areas via a waterway transportation system.

The Jingdezhen Handicraft Porcelain Industry Sites bear witness to the formation of a large-scale intensive production model characterised by an industrial chain with fine divisions of labour, standardisation and specialisation, leading to a capacity for high quality porcelain. Through the advancement of production technologies and industrial organisation, Jingdezhen maintained its position as the porcelain production centre of the world from the 16th century, promoting the widespread dissemination of Chinese porcelain making technology, art and products around the world.
properties.brief_synthesis_zh:
景德镇手工瓷业遗存位于瓷石、高岭土、柴薪林和水运资源丰富的地区。五个组成部分共同呈现 10 至 19 世纪景德镇手工瓷业体系，展现了中国制瓷技术和陶瓷艺术的创新发展，并对世界陶瓷工业产生深远影响。

10 至 13 世纪，南北方制瓷技术在此融合，实现青白瓷的稳定生产，奠定景德镇发展为中国瓷业中心的基础。13 至 15 世纪，景德镇出现“二元配方”（瓷石与高岭土混合）、新窑型以及使用进口钴料烧造青花瓷等创新。16 世纪起，景德镇成为世界手工瓷业生产中心，以水路交通系统连接瓷器制造与销售中心、原料产地和柴薪产区，形成复杂的产业结构。

遗存见证了以精细分工、标准化和专业化为特征的大规模集约生产模式，并凭借生产技术和产业组织的推进，自 16 世纪起保持世界瓷业生产中心地位，推动中国制瓷技术、艺术和产品在全球传播。
properties.state_party_proposed_name: Jingdezhen Handicraft Porcelain Industry Sites

properties.property_name_en: Tashkent Modernist Architecture. Modernity and Tradition in Central Asia
properties.property_name_zh: 塔什干现代主义建筑：中亚的现代性与传统
properties.brief_synthesis_en: Tashkent Modernist Architecture. Modernity and Tradition in Central Asia outstandingly reflects the confluence of socialist urban planning, modernist architectural innovation, and the craftsmanship of the region in the 1960s-1990s. The nine component parts in Tashkent demonstrate how international modernist principles were adapted to incorporate traditional spatial practices and artistic culture of Uzbekistan. The 1966 earthquake accelerated the modernisation of Tashkent, which had been initiated under the Russian Administration and continued after its designation as the capital of the Uzbek Soviet Socialist Republic in 1930. A General Plan emphasizing socialist urbanism, green spaces, and modern housing fostered the widespread adoption of innovative materials and technologies, including seismic-resistant designs and climateresponsive solutions, which blended prefabrication with artisanal craftsmanship. Architects from both Moscow and Tashkent design institutes embraced elements of Central Asian architecture, including courtyards, loggias, and traditional geometric motifs, blending them with the focus of Soviet modernism on functionality. The serial property exceptionally reflects this hybrid approach to design, where international and local influences converged to create powerful architectural foci that shaped the urban form of modernist Tashkent, incorporating functional zoning and typologies, large public spaces, and monumental art that conveyed socialist narratives whilst celebrating the heritage of Uzbekistan. Criterion (iv): Tashkent Modernist Architecture. Modernity and Tradition in Central Asia outstandingly illustrates developments in architecture and urban planning during the post-Thaw period in a Soviet socialist society. Designed as prominent nodes within the city, the component parts, consciously combining modernist themes and aspirations with reinterpreted local motifs and building patterns, formed cohesive ensembles that shaped the form and identity of the city, integrated with the urban fabric, served as landmarks, and enhanced urban perspectives. The serial property exemplifies the combination of Soviet standardisation and site-specific designs. Monumental art further enriched its expression, blending abstract aesthetics with ideological themes of progress and cultural identity. The serial property is one of the finest examples of architectural and urban design, reflecting, in an exceptional and specific manner, the broader movement of “localised modernism” that spread across the Soviet realm, developed within the cultural and climatic environment of Central Asia.
properties.brief_synthesis_zh: ICOMOS推荐OUV声明中的Brief synthesis原文已抽取；中文页面可在此基础上进行工作译文和审校。
properties.state_party_proposed_name: Tashkent Modernist Architecture. Modernity and Tradition in Central Asia

properties.property_name_en: The Medinas of the Historic Sultanates of the Comoros
properties.property_name_zh: 科摩罗历史苏丹国麦地那
properties.brief_synthesis_en: The serial property comprises the best-preserved parts of six historic cities located on the islands of Ngazidja and Ndzuwani in the Comoros. They developed from urban nuclei that emerged around at least the 11th century and evolved into city-states around the 14th-15th century, becoming eventually the principal urban centres of the sultanates established in the archipelago of Comoros within the framework of the formation of a Muslim elite linked to the Swahili world. This heritage ensemble, encompassing urban, architectural, and intangible elements, reflects the richness and specificity of Swahili cultural tradition within the Comorian island context. The former sultanic medinas have preserved their old urban morphology and fabric, and an exceptional set of traditions and social practices still alive today. Compact fabrics, with centrally organised palaces and public squares (bangwe, mpangahari), stone houses and defensive structures, the medinas showcase insular urban forms and architectural expressions that resulted from cultural exchanges related to migration and Indian Ocean trade networks, while displaying a spatial logic dictated by a hierarchical system of social organisation based on matrilineal and matrilocal customs, age-group systems, and patrilineal Islamic traditions. The Comorian historic cities, though founded upon the same fundamental cultural and social basis, evolved along distinct trajectories shaped by their geographic, political, social and historical contexts.

properties.property_name_en: Village of Sidi Bou Saïd: Hub of Cultural and Spiritual Inspiration in the Mediterranean
properties.property_name_zh: 西迪布赛义德村：地中海的文化与精神灵感之源
properties.category_of_property_source_note: ICOMOS considers that the application of the cultural landscape category to the nominated property is not justified. Article I category: site.
properties.brief_synthesis_en: The Village of Sidi Bou Saïd is located on top of a hill overlooking the Mediterranean Sea, in northern Tunisia. It developed as a sanctuary-village as a result of a process of aggregation around the mausoleum of a Sufi saint – Abou Saïd Khalaf Ibn Yahya El Tamimi El Beji, known as Sidi Bou Saïd – supported by successive ruling dynasties, which, from the 16th century onwards, fostered the sacralisation of the place by associating their political legitimacy with the baraka (blessing) of the saint. The 54 Husainid beys (18th-20th century) consolidated this dynamic by building holiday residences in Sidi Bou Saïd and turning it into an elite resort. The property embodies the encounter between the spiritual power of the saint and the temporal political power. With the religious complex of Sidi Bou Saïd at its heart, the village reflects the traditional Arab-Islamic urban planning around a central place, enriched by the 19th-20th century urban-type residential architecture of the notables.

properties.property_name_en: Alamūt Castle and Related Fortifications
properties.property_name_zh: 阿拉穆特城堡及相关防御工事
properties.brief_synthesis_en: The Alamūt Castle and Related Fortifications are located in the Alborz Mountains of northern Iran, south of the Caspian Sea, within the historic region of Daylamān. The serial property comprises seven castles and fortifications distributed across the Alamūt Valley in Qazvin Province. At its centre stands Alamūt Castle, positioned on a steep rocky outcrop and serving as the headquarters of the Nezāri Ismaʿili state founded by Ḥasan Ṣabbāḥ in 1090. The other component parts – Lambesar Castle, Navizaršāh Castle, Šams Kelāyeh Fortification, Qosṭinlār Fortification, Širkūh Fortification, and Ilān Fortification – are strategically located across the valley and together form a coordinated defensive system. From the late 11th to the mid-13th centuries, Alamūt served as the political, religious, administrative and intellectual centre of the Nezāri Ismaʿili state, which flourished in this mountainous region. The fortifications enabled the organisation, governance, and defence of this community within a naturally protected setting. The Outstanding Universal Value of the serial property is conveyed through the remains of the fortresses and their defensive structures, rock-cut architectural features, water storage and supply systems, archaeological remains associated with the fortified sites, and the surrounding mountainous landscape that structured the defensive network.

properties.property_name_en: Amazonia Theaters
properties.property_name_zh: 亚马孙剧院
properties.brief_synthesis_en: Manaus, founded in 1669 as an isolated fort on the Rio Negro, underwent a spectacular transformation in the 19th century, thanks to the rise of rubber. From a simple village, by around 1900 it had become a prosperous town with 50,000 inhabitants, nicknamed the "Paris of the Tropics". Fortunes amassed from rubber exploitation financed Haussmann-style urban modernisation. The Teatro Amazonas, with its eclectic Neo-Renaissance style, is a prime symbol of this ambition. The extensive use of European materials (Glasgow iron, Carrara marble, Murano chandeliers, French furniture) reflects a desire to position Manaus within the international circuits of luxury and culture. The interior, decorated by European and Brazilian artists, reinforces this cosmopolitan aesthetic. The Largo de São Sebastião, with its paving evoking the "meeting of the waters" and its monument celebrating the opening of the ports, showcases the economic power of the city and serves as a social showcase for the rubber elites.

properties.property_name_en: Sebastia
properties.icomos_recommendation_note: Emergency inscription and simultaneous inclusion on the List of World Heritage in Danger; Reactive Monitoring mission recommended.
properties.brief_synthesis_en: Sebastia, located near Nablus in the West Bank, in Palestine, preserves the archaeological record of the successive occupation of the site by different civilisations from at least the 9th century BCE through the Hellenistic, Roman, and Byzantine periods, with later architectural remains dating to the Crusader era, as well as the Ayyubid/Mamluk and Ottoman periods.
properties.state_party_proposed_name: Sebastia

properties.property_name_en: Ancient Buddhist Site, Sarnath, Varanasi
properties.property_name_zh: 鹿野苑古代佛教遗址，瓦拉纳西
properties.brief_synthesis_en: Located within Varanasi city, state of Uttar Pradesh, India, this serial property composed of two component parts (Chaukhandi Stupa, and the Archaeological Remains of Sarnath) comprises all the known and preserved archaeological and architectural vestiges of the ancient site of Sarnath, believed to be the place that Gautama Buddha visited in the 6th century BCE. Defined by the Buddha as one of the four key sites of Buddhist pilgrimage, Sarnath developed over centuries through royal, monastic and lay patronage. Numerous stupas, temples and viharas were constructed in different historical periods, with evidence from the pre-Mauryan period, through the Kushana and Gupta periods, until the decline of the place in the 12th century CE. Re-discovered in the 18th century, the property is being revived by the Buddhist community as one of their holy places. In the Buddhist tradition, Sarnath is known as Rishipatana, Ishipatana, Mrigadava or the Deer Park, which refer to a sacred geography, where the Buddha is believed to have delivered his first sermon and met his first disciples.

property_assessments.rationale_zh: Key management elements exist but are not operationally clear; National Management Committee must be formally established with mandate and responsibilities.
property_assessments.reviewer_note:
同事版补充摘录：ICOMOS considers that legal protection is in placebut needs to be strengthened by including, in Ministerial Decree No 74 of 2024, mapswith refined boundaries of the nominated component parts and their respective buffer zones. 
Clear protection mechanisms should be in place for both the nominated component parts and their buffer zones until the designations are perfected. Key elements of the management system are in place, but they need to be integrated through coordination and collegial decision-making mechanisms, including a governance/management entity that brings togetherall relevant decision-makers and key actors.Such an entity should ensure that legitimate economic interests of concessionaires do not prevail over the long-term preservation of the proposedOutstanding Universal Value of the nominated property, and that sustainable, compatible opportunities for local communities are created.ICOMOSnotesthatitisunclearwhetherthisbodyhasbeen established and,if so,whatits mandate and responsibilities are. 
The management plan needs to be turned into a more realistic and implementable document through operational programmes, with clear priorities and actions to promptly address the most urgent needs, including emergency conservation and stabilisation measures, and with indicators to monitor progress in improving the state of conservation. Including Heritage Impact Assessment mechanismsin the decision-making processes and programmes, based on existing World Heritage instruments, is indispensableto enhancingthe effectiveness of protection and managementof the nominated property. This also concerns any project on Roça Agostinho Neto, to ensure that its capacity to support the proposedOutstanding Universal Valueis retained until it is added to the series.
Participation in the management of local communities, particularly of the descendants of roças’ labourers, is essential, given the nature of the nominated property and its memorial dimension, to guarantee that the proposed Outstanding Universal Value is transmitted to future generations, thereby supporting the re-appropriation process and socio-economic well-beingof local communities.

property_assessments.conclusion_en: In summary, ICOMOS considers that the current measures of protection of the nominated property are adequate and the development of a PSMV will further strengthen them. The proposed management system requires a good coordination between the many rightsholders and stakeholders, including the residents and the religious communities, who should be included in the decision-making. Tourism should be firmly regulated through relevant tourism strategy and tourism management plan, and the tourism model must shift to cultural and spiritual tourism, in line with the values of the nominated property. These should also be reflected in an interpretation strategy. Development of a disaster risk management plan accompanied by an emergency plan is an urgent necessity and should be completed in priority and within a shorter timeframe than expected.

property_assessments.reviewer_note:
同事版补充摘录：ICOMOS considers thatthe nominated property benefits from substantial documentation, research, and conservation work. Archaeological investigations, photogrammetric documentation, and conservation activities, particularly at Alamūt Castle and Lambesar Castle(component parts 1 and 2),have contributed to stabilising key structures while respecting traditional materials and techniques. However, several component parts require preservation and stabilisation, and ICOMOS considers that a more consistent, long-term conservation strategy across the serial property would be desirable. The existing monitoring framework,coordinated by IMCHTH and implemented by ACHB through regular inspections, defined indicators, and collaboration with scientific institutions, provides a sound basis for safeguarding the nominated property. ICOMOS recommends strengthening preventive conservation measures, ensuring sustained resources and staffing for monitoring, and further developing systematic monitoring of cumulative impacts from tourism, development, and environmental factors across the nominated serial property.ICOMOS considers that it would be advisable that the monitoring systemis adaptedfor easy integration of its outcomes into the Periodic Reporting questionnaire.

同事版影响因素：保护措施
监测

property_assessments.conclusion_zh: A structured management plan with action plan, budget projections, indicators, implementation roadmaps and local management base provides an adequate basis for long-term conservation.
property_assessments.conclusion_en: In summary, ICOMOS considers that the nominated serial property benefits from a solid legal and administrative protection framework supported by national heritage legislation, environmental regulations, and its registration on the National Monuments List of Iran. Protection and management are coordinated by the Iranian Ministry of Cultural Heritage, Tourism and Handicrafts and implemented locally through the Alamūt Cultural Heritage Base, which oversees research, conservation, monitoring, and local community engagement. The management plan, supported by a defined action plan, budget projections, monitoring indicators, and implementation roadmaps, provides a structured framework for the long-term conservation of the property. Risk preparedness strategies and thematic plans for conservation, tourism, and interpretation have also been developed. ICOMOS recommends using as reference the principles and methodology of the Guidance and Toolkit for Impact Assessments in a World Heritage Context under national mechanisms to better assess potential impacts on the attributes of Outstanding Universal Value. Visitor management arrangements are in place and supported by ongoing tourism planning, although access to several component parts remains challenging and will require careful management once the Sustainable Tourism Master Plan is finalised. Local community participation is evident and contributes positively to the safeguarding and management of the nominated property. Overall, ICOMOS considers that the protection and management system provides an adequate basis for the conservation of the nominated property and its values.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: A structured management plan with action plan, budget projections, indicators, implementation roadmaps and local management base provides an adequate basis for long-term conservation.

property_assessments.conclusion_zh: State of conservation is generally good but uneven; conservation and maintenance guidance/protocols are needed to avoid inappropriate interventions.
property_assessments.conclusion_en:
ICOMOS considers that the nominated serial property is well documented, and documentation will continue to grow with the implementation of the planned conservation strategy. Guidance and protocols for maintenance shall ensure the functionality of the buildings and the ability of their attributes to convey the proposed Outstanding Universal Value.
ICOMOS observes that it would be advisable to further develop the monitoring system to explicitly encompass all the attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. The system would benefit from further clarification on the implementation of the proposed third-level monitoring and how data will be used to inform management adjustments. Finally, consideration should be given to designing it to facilitate the easy integration of its outcomes into the Periodic Reporting questionnaire.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: State of conservation is generally good but uneven; conservation and maintenance guidance/protocols are needed to avoid inappropriate interventions.

property_assessments.conclusion_zh: Governance is clear, but institutional cooperation should be formalised; monitoring, risk management, HIA and tourism management require further development.
property_assessments.conclusion_en:
In summary, ICOMOS considers that the protection and management system will be fully adequate for effective safeguarding of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value in the long term once the boundaries of the revised buffer zones and associated draft Regulation on the Management and Protection Zone Regime are approved and enforced, and the provisions for visually sensitive areas are integrated into the 2024 master plan.
Both instruments are crucial and should therefore be adopted and made operational urgently. Consideration should be given to a moratorium on approving new construction within the designated buffer zones and visually sensitive areas until the buffer zones and their regulations enter into force and provisions for visual sensitivities are integrated into the master plan.
The governance system is clear. However, the commitment among the relevant institutional actors to cooperate for the effective, long-term safeguarding of the nominated property, and the roles of each actor in the joint management of the nominated serial property, would need to be formalised through a jointly signed agreement.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: Governance is clear, but institutional cooperation should be formalised; monitoring, risk management, HIA and tourism management require further development.

同事版补充摘录：ICOMOS considers that the vestiges of the Normandy Landings are well documented, including the main attributes.However,furtheranalysisoflandscapeattributes and their role in sustaining the memorial and associative dimensions of proposed Outstanding Universal Value is advisable. Conservation approaches vary, and a coherent overall strategy should be developed that encompasses different forms of conservation, including physical interventions. Itshould be guided by clear principles and priorities that reflect the significanceof each attribute, their rarity, and contribution to proposed Outstanding Universal Value. 
Systematic monitoring of the state of conservation is urgently needed, particularly for fragile attributes at risk of rapid deterioration, and should integrate monitoring of coastal dynamics within the management system. The monitoring system should also be adapted to facilitate integration of its outcomes into the Periodic Reporting framework.

property_assessments.conclusion_zh: Governance is complex and requires strong coordination across instruments and stakeholders; interpretation also needs a coherent OUV-based strategy.
property_assessments.conclusion_en:
In summary, ICOMOS considers that the nominated serial property benefits from adequate legal protection, although such protection does not always stem from heritage-specific provisions, and that some parts of the expanded buffer zone are primarily governed by spatial and urban planning frameworks. It is therefore recommended that the protection regime be strengthened, in particular by extending heritage protection to component parts not currently covered by these mechanisms and by reinforcing protection mechanisms within the buffer zone. Clear objectives for safeguarding attributes supporting the justification for inscription and their landscape setting, should be promptly integrated into the management system and relevant planning instruments. 

The governance of this extensive and densely populated area is inherently complex, requiring strong coordination across multiple instruments and stakeholders; however, municipalities' commitment to the buffer zone is a positive indication as to the future effectiveness of this aspect. Vigilance over development is essential, particularly regarding renewable energy projects, including the offshore wind farm. According to ICOMOS, this should be dismantled once its concession expires and the site should be restored to its original condition. 

A coherent interpretation strategy based on the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property remains necessary and should be fully developed. Continued dialogue with stakeholders, including those expressing dissenting views, and sustained awareness-raising efforts are essential to ensure consensus and that promotional initiatives do not detract from the values and significance of the nominated property.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: Governance is complex and requires strong coordination across instruments and stakeholders; interpretation also needs a coherent OUV-based strategy.

property_assessments.conclusion_en:
In summary, ICOMOS considers that the nominated serial property benefits from an appropriate level of legal protection, as well as regional and local protection mechanisms that safeguard the attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value.
The effectiveness of the management body created as part of the agreement between the regions involved in the nomination and the MiC will be demonstrated by the development of operational procedures and the long-term availability of resources allocated to the body responsible for coordinating and implementing the management plan. The latter provides a framework capable of ensuring the conservation of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value, provided the funding will be sustained beyond the first period from 2025 to 2028.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: A management framework exists, but the effectiveness of the new management body still depends on operational procedures and sustained long-term resources.
property_assessments.reviewer_note:
同事版分档待确认：√（check）

同事版摘录待确认：ICOMOS considers that the management system has been developed adequately.
ICOMOS considers that the plannedmanagement of tourism is consistent withthe findings and projections taken into account in the regional plans.
ICOMOS considers that the local communities havebeen suitably involved in the nomination process via cultural associations,schoolmediationandeducationalinitiatives. Subsequent participation remains unspecified, beyond the affirmation of the role of the actors responsible for the cultural activities of the theatres.ICOMOS considers that participatory processes involving local communitiesshould be an integral part of the day-to-day management of the nominated serial property. A framework of this kind should be developed and integrated into the draft management plan.
The effectiveness of the management body created as part of the agreement between the regions involved in the nomination and the MiC will be demonstrated by the development of operational procedures and the long-term availability ofresources allocated to the body responsible for coordinating and implementing the management plan.【没有明确说哪方面现存明确的管理问题】

ICOMOS considers that the nominated extension contributes to and complements the World Heritage series; the integrity assessment reflects whether the added component part contains attributes that support the Outstanding Universal Value.

property_assessments.conclusion_en: The integrity of the nominated serial property is based on the inclusion of a sufficient area of each historic town to understand their urban morphology, and key elements of the urban fabric, including public and private spaces whose meaning is linked to the traditional social organisation and which host cultural practices. Associations between the built environment and its natural setting are functionally important to support the protection of the nominated property. As a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole, and how each component part contributes to that value. Integrity is furthermore a measure of the intactness of the attributes. Only the best-preserved parts of each medina have been included within the nominated serial property, with the 22 remaining intra-muros areas and some immediate setting included within the buffer zones. ICOMOS considers that this approach is in principle adequate, as it takes into consideration the state of conservation of the historic cities, leaving out elements that have lost their integrity.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据Integrity章节是否明确认为条件已满足/已证明初判。
property_assessments.reviewer_note:
同事版补充摘录：The nominated component parts include sufficient extent of each medina and enough attributes to reflect the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. Moreover, the nominated property retains high integrity in terms of urban morphology, the visual connection between the sea and the mountains, and the system ofreligious and socio-cultural spaces and centres. However, theoldest built structures (palaces, city walls, historic mosques)are highly vulnerable, with several cases in serious disrepair (in Domoni, Mutsamudu, Ikoni, Ntsudjini).
ICOMOS considers thatthe integrity of the nominated seriesas a whole,as well as the integrity of each of the component parts have been demonstrated. 
ICOMOS further considers that the conditions of integrity and authenticity of the whole series andof the individual component parts have been met, but they are both greatly vulnerable.

property_assessments.conclusion_en: The authenticity of the nominated serial property is based on the form and design, materials, and function, as well as location and setting of the historical vestiges of the selected medinas, and the associated intangible heritage. Authenticity depends on the degree of credibility and truthfulness of information sources on the value of the nominated property. These sources of information must be considered and judged primarily within the cultural contexts to which they belong. The urban form, spatial organisation, and the "spirit of place" remain strong in all six medinas, and the authenticity of their use and function is also very high. However, material authenticity is more heterogeneous, particularly where concrete reconstructions have been carried out without clear conservation principles. The relationship with the sea is not fully preserved today due to modern developments.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据Authenticity章节是否明确认为条件已满足/已证明初判。
property_assessments.reviewer_note: 同事版补充摘录：ICOMOS considers that the authenticity of the whole nominated series as well as the authenticity of each of the component parts have been demonstrated.

property_assessments.conclusion_en: An estimated 20,000 inhabitants (2024) live in six component parts, with the same number of residents projected in the buffer zones. The delimitation of boundaries follows a common logic. The component parts comprise compact areas, centred on the intra-muros part of each medina, encompassing only those sections of each historic city where integrity and legibility remain the strongest, while maintaining a sufficiently large buffer zone to act as a visual, functional, and regulatory filter. Depending on the medina, from thirty to seventy percent of intra-muros areas have been included within each component part. The boundaries are designed to follow existing routes.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据Boundaries章节对遗产区/缓冲区边界是否充分、是否需修订的表述初判。

property_assessments.conclusion_en: Despite the shortcomings of the comparative analysis presented in the nomination dossier, ICOMOS considers that the nominated property stands out among its comparators for the comparative completeness of the plantation system and the preserved spatial logic of the coercion and surveillance system over often forcibly contracted labourers. ICOMOS considers that, despite some weaknesses of the comparative analysis, the additional comparative elements gathered by ICOMOS during the evaluation justify consideration of this property for the World Heritage List.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据比较分析章节结论初判。
依据ICOMOS是否认可至少一项申报标准初判；具体每条标准的认可/不认可见criteria_assessments。

property_assessments.conclusion_en: According to the nomination dossier, the nominated series includes all the necessary attributes to convey the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. Most architectural elements within each component part have survived, and their historic layout, spatial logic, and reciprocal relationships remain legible because little development has obliterated them. Changes to the immediate and wider setting have been limited and contribute to the understanding of the historic and cultural significance of the nominated serial property. Functional structures and their machinery have been retained at some roças, allowing an appreciation of how the agro-industrial system functioned. In some cases, the technological equipment remains in use, illustrating the production cycle. Although the forced labour system has ceased, the nominated roças on Sao Tome remain inhabited and provide a sense of social cohesion.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据Integrity章节是否明确认为条件已满足/已证明初判。
property_assessments.reviewer_note: 同事版补充摘录：ICOMOS considers that, at this stage, the attributes included in the nominated component parts suffice to convey the proposed Outstanding Universal Value, and therefore, the integrity ... can be considered sufficient.

property_assessments.conclusion_en: The authenticity of the nominated series is based on the form and design, material and substance, use and function, traditions, techniques and management systems, location and setting, and spirit and feeling. Authenticity depends on the degree of credibility and truthfulness of information sources on the value of the nominated property. These sources of information must be considered and judged primarily within the cultural contexts to which they belong. For each aspect, a brief discussion is provided on how the specific attributes of the nominated property convey the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. The nomination dossier also highlights which attributes have maintained a higher capacity to express the proposed Outstanding Universal Value, and which ones have been subject to changes that have altered this capacity. The overall layout and distribution of the buildings, facilities and structures within the nominated component parts, as well as the industrial and public service buildings, have been preserved.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据Authenticity章节是否明确认为条件已满足/已证明初判。

property_assessments.conclusion_en: The number of permanent inhabitants varies across the nominated component parts. At Monte Café (component part 1), there are 1,100 inhabitants in the nominated component part and an additional 400 inhabitants in the buffer zone. At Agua Izé (component part 2), 2,300 inhabitants are numbered and an additional 700 people live in the buffer zone. At Diogo Vaz (component part 3), 600 inhabitants live in the component part. At Sao Joao (component part 4), only five people live in the component part.

property_assessments.conclusion_en:
ICOMOS considers that the reconversion, whether complete or partial, cannot be a permanent process. This approach must be considered an exception that will not be likely to occur again in the future, so that the marked persistence of the residential function on a large scale is maintained and the authenticity of the original function of the place, namely a place of residence and vacation, is preserved.
ICOMOS considers that the authenticity of the nominated property has been demonstrated but remains vulnerable due to risks associated with mass tourism.

property_assessments.conclusion_en: There are approximately 1,500 inhabitants in the nominated property (2023 census), and 6,500 in the buffer zone. The boundaries include the historic centre of the village together with a large part of the promontory. It largely follows the boundary of the historical zone of Sidi Bou Saïd designated by the 1915 Beylical decree but skirts the later buildings which break with its traditional architectural style on the north-western edges of the historical zone and excludes the marina. In response to the recommendations of ICOMOS included in the interim report, the State Party adjusted the boundaries of the nominated property to the south, by including the El Marassi hotel and a residential complex adjacent to Korsi Essoleh, both of which were built in the aesthetic resembling the structures of the nominated property. The immediate surroundings of the village have been included in the buffer zone to ensure control of the developments near the nominated property and protect the panoramas.

property_assessments.conclusion_en: The integrity of the nominated series is based on the inclusion of all the preserved architectural and archaeological vestiges that reflect the spiritual significance of Sarnath. As a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole, and how each component part contributes to that value. Integrity is furthermore a measure of the intactness of the attributes. The two component parts contain all the known preserved structures erected between the 6th century BCE and the 12th century CE, at what used to be a sacred site of Sarnath. The proposed buffer zone indicates the relationship between the component parts which existed in the past, all the monuments having belonged to one monastic complex. Archaeological and textual evidence suggests that the site underwent historically phases of appropriation and transformation, as well as internal decay, that gradually eroded the integrity of the monastic complex.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据Integrity章节是否明确认为条件已满足/已证明初判。
property_assessments.reviewer_note: 同事版补充摘录：Despite these factors, ICOMOS considers that the integrity of the nominated series as a whole... as well as the integrity of each of the component parts, have been demonstrated.

property_assessments.conclusion_en: The authenticity of the nominated property is based on the form, materials and location of the architectural and archaeological ensemble at Sarnath, presented through a series of two component parts, as well as the spirit and intangible practices associated with the meaning of Sarnath for the Buddhist community worldwide. Authenticity depends on the degree of credibility and truthfulness of the information sources on the value of the nominated serial property. These sources of information must be considered and judged primarily within the cultural contexts to which they belong. The excavations exposed monuments that demonstrate a historical layering spanning over a millennium. Despite past destruction, the surviving structures retain their forms. Conservation efforts undertaken since the early 20th century have ensured that elevation and levels have been maintained with minimal intervention.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据Authenticity章节是否明确认为条件已满足/已证明初判。
property_assessments.reviewer_note: 同事版补充摘录：ICOMOS considers that the authenticity of the nominated series as a whole, as well as the authenticity of each of the component parts, have been demonstrated.

property_assessments.conclusion_en: Excavated architectural and archaeological vestiges of the monastic complex of Sarnath are located within the proposed boundaries, constituting two separate archaeological sites, demarcated physically by compound walls and metal railings. ICOMOS notes that the Shri Digambar Jain Temple, which is part of the archaeological park, is not included in component part 02. There are no inhabitants in the nominated property. The buffer zone, with approximately 6,000 inhabitants (2020 estimate), includes the area of the All-India Radio Station, the garden of Spiritual Wisdom, an extensive area between the two component parts where several temples and monasteries of different Buddhist communities have been constructed in modern times and where the Archaeological Museum is located, as well as the Sarnath Mini Zoo, and the Buddha theme park. To the west, the buffer zone includes some low-rise developments.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据Boundaries章节对遗产区/缓冲区边界是否充分、是否需修订的表述初判。

property_assessments.conclusion_en: ICOMOS considers that the comparative analysis justifies consideration of this property for the World Heritage List.

property_assessments.conclusion_en: According to the State Party, the integrity of the nominated serial property is based on the inclusion of the fortifications as a strategic defensive system ingeniously integrated within the landscape and territory, which contributed to make Alamūt Castle and Related Fortifications a political, religious and cultural centre for the Nezāri Ismaʿili. As a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole, and how each component part contributes to that value. Integrity is furthermore a measure of the intactness of the attributes. Recent research on the Ismaʿili fortifications has identified thirty-three large and small castles, forts, and watchtowers in the Alamūt region. However, as further explained by the State Party in the October 2025 additional information, besides being the only ones in the valley with considerable in situ architectural remains, the seven component parts have been selected because, altogether, they illustrate the strategic functions, intervisibility and interconnectivity of the fortifications within the entire defensive system, with each fortification fulfilling a specific role contributing to the effective operation of the overall system. ICOMOS considers that the rationale for the selection of the component parts is justified by the comparative analysis, and that they collectively represent the defensive system as well as the cultural, religious…
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据Integrity章节是否明确认为条件已满足/已证明初判。

property_assessments.conclusion_en: The State Party argues that authenticity of the nominated property is based on the form and design, materials and techniques, location and setting of the nominated fortifications, as well as spirit and feeling, all articulated through a series of seven component parts. Authenticity depends on the degree of credibility and truthfulness of information sources on the value of the nominated property. These sources of information must be considered and judged primarily within the cultural contexts to which they belong. Archaeological and conservation work have contributed to preserving the form, design, materials and traditional techniques of Alamūt Castle and Related Fortifications. Despite their current ruined state, the surviving elements of all the nominated component parts still retain their original design and form. The material is still the same as in the original constructions, and conservation and restoration have been carried out using suitable and appropriate materials, as well as traditional methods and techniques informed by contemporary scientific approaches.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据Authenticity章节是否明确认为条件已满足/已证明初判。
property_assessments.reviewer_note: 同事版补充摘录：ICOMOS considers that the authenticity of the nominated series as a whole, as well as the authenticity of each of the component parts, have been demonstrated.

property_assessments.conclusion_en: According to the additional information provided by the State Party in October 2025, nine villages (Gāzorkhān, Tavān, Zardchāl, Māshā kelāyeh, Garmārūd, Šams Kelāyeh, Qosṭinlār, Āghāgīr and Ilān) are located within the buffer zones of the nominated component parts, with a total population numbering 1,936 inhabitants. The seven nominated component parts and their buffer zones are located within the Alamūt region which serves as a Natural defensive territory. The State Party argues that the most important consideration in defining the boundaries of the nominated property has been to consider the physical remains of each nominated fortification, with surrounding areas and related key features included within the boundaries where necessary. According to the October 2025 additional information, these features include ancient water-supply systems, historical access routes and parts of traditional orchards, and medicinal plant habitat areas. The buffer zones, on the other hand, are usually delimited by ridge lines, or by mountain slopes and non-natural boundaries such as roads.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据Boundaries章节对遗产区/缓冲区边界是否充分、是否需修订的表述初判。

property_assessments.conclusion_en: While some aspects of the comparative analysis could be further refined to recognise aspects such as the longerterm influence of Baekje and the degree of uniqueness of Asuka and Fujiwara, ICOMOS considers that the comparative analysis is appropriately framed and that the selection of sites of ancient capitals in similar periods within the geocultural region of East Asia for comparison is valid. 97 ICOMOS considers that the comparative analysis justifies consideration of this property for the World Heritage List.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据比较分析章节结论初判。
依据ICOMOS是否认可至少一项申报标准初判；具体每条标准的认可/不认可见criteria_assessments。

property_assessments.conclusion_en: The integrity of the nominated serial property is based on the state of conservation and the way major pressures are managed, as well as the intactness of the material evidence of the selected component parts. As a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts contain all the attributes necessary to express the proposed Outstanding Universal Value, and how each component part contributes to that value. ICOMOS considers that there has been a sound rationale for the selection of the component parts, and that the boundaries of the nineteen component parts contain all the attributes necessary to support the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. The nominated property does not suffer from neglect or adverse effects. The state of conservation of the nominated component parts is generally good and the management system has adequately identified key pressures. ICOMOS considers that the integrity of the nominated series as a whole, as well as the integrity of each of the component parts, have been demonstrated. 98
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据Integrity章节是否明确认为条件已满足/已证明初判。

property_assessments.conclusion_en: The authenticity of the nominated property is based on the ways in which the attributes can be understood as conveying the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. The nominated property is comprised of above and below ground archaeological sites and materials and their landscape contexts. Authenticity depends on the degree of credibility and truthfulness of information sources on the value of the nominated property. These sources of information must be considered and judged primarily within the cultural contexts to which they belong. ICOMOS considers that the authenticity is supported by a strong blend of archaeological finds and historical texts and literature. The research and documentation of the archaeological evidence support the authenticity of the nominated property.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据Authenticity章节是否明确认为条件已满足/已证明初判。
property_assessments.reviewer_note: 同事版补充摘录：ICOMOS considers that the authenticity of the nominated series as a whole, as well as the authenticity of each of the component parts have been demonstrated.

property_assessments.conclusion_en: There are 496 inhabitants in the nominated component parts, and 12,489 inhabitants in the buffer zone. Most of the component parts have no inhabitants, other than the Fujiwara Palace site (component part 013, 440 inhabitants), the Asuka-dera Temple Site (component part 005, 36 inhabitants), and very small numbers at the sites of the Asuka Palace Site, Tachibana-dera Temple Site, Yamada-dera Temple Site, Kawara-dera Temple Site, and the Motoyakushiji Temple Site (component parts 001, 006, 007, 008 and 015). There is some private land within the nominated component parts. Additional information received in February 2026 outlined the processes for the acquisition of private land. The boundaries of the component parts are based on their designations as Historic Site, Special Historic Site, and Place of Scenic Beauty under the Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties or imperial property under the Imperial House Law.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据Boundaries章节对遗产区/缓冲区边界是否充分、是否需修订的表述初判。

property_assessments.conclusion_en: ICOMOS considers that there are several additional properties on the World Heritage List and the Tentative Lists of States Parties that express some similarities to the nominated property and therefore could be compared. ICOMOS considers that the comparative analysis justifies consideration of this property for the World Heritage List.

property_assessments.conclusion_zh: 依据Authenticity章节是否明确认为条件已满足/已证明初判。
property_assessments.conclusion_en:
ICOMOS notes that various treatments for stabilising, conserving and presenting the sites can be observed across the forty-eight component parts, such as walls, soil covering and fences; and shelters of various types and sizes have been erected at many of the nominated component parts to protect the archaeological materials. In some component parts, key elements were rebuilt after suffering damage and loss in 19th-century wars (e.g. TJ-03, TJ-04 and TJ-07), retaining their original characteristics.
ICOMOS also notes that past conservation interventions and reconstructions have impacted on the authenticity of some sites, but these are not systematically detailed in the nomination dossier. ICOMOS considers that, in some cases, the restoration works appear conjectural and obscure the authentic fabric of the monuments. For example, ICOMOS considers that reconstructions at component parts KZ-02 and KZ-03 (Yassy-Kultobe and Sauran Settlement) obscure the spatial layout and original fabric, despite prior studies. The remains of the mosque at component part KZ-04 (Syganak Settlement) also seem to be reconstructed. These past reconstructions do not demonstrate best practice. At component part TJ-03 (Site of Khujand), the citadel has largely been rebuilt around the fortress, although some of the original walls survive. The fortress itself is a ruin with a high degree of authenticity. Any future plans to reconstruct elements of this structure should be very cautiously considered, and subject to Heritage Impact Assessment (HIA).
Some of the individual component parts have been impacted by past restorations, reconstructions, and landscaping works. Others remain vulnerable due to the absence of detailed technical guidance, and inadequate site-level supervision. Special care must be taken in relation to future restoration and tourism-oriented changes, all of which should be subject to HIA.
Since the criteria for justifying the proposed Outstanding Universal Value have not been demonstrated at this stage and there are issues with the selection of component parts and the identification of attributes that might support the proposed Outstanding Universal Value, authenticity, as defined by the Operational Guidelines for the Implementation of the World Heritage Convention, cannot be confirmed at this stage.

property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据ICOMOS是否认可至少一项申报标准初判；具体每条标准的认可/不认可见criteria_assessments。

property_assessments.conclusion_en: Integrity is a measure of wholeness, intactness, and absence of threats to the nominated serial property and its attributes. As a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole, and how each component parts contribute to that value. ICOMOS considers that each of the eight component parts contributes to the nominated series in a substantial way. Together, they represent the regional characteristics of Xiongnu tombs across the administrative and territorial divisions of the Xiongnu empire. They also cover the chronological span of the Xiongnu empire, and include examples of different expressions of the Xiongnu funerary culture, thus constituting a representational sample of Xiongnu cemetery complexes. The integrity of the individual tombs is sufficient, though it has been affected by acts of looting that caused some destruction.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据Integrity章节是否明确认为条件已满足/已证明初判。

property_assessments.conclusion_en: Authenticity is the ability to understand the values attributed to the nominated property as conveyed by its attributes. The authenticity of the nominated serial property is based on the forms and designs of the noble tombs, satellite graves and sacrificial structures that form the Xiongnu cemetery complexes, as well as their locations. Authenticity depends on the degree of credibility and truthfulness of information sources on the value of the nominated property. 148 These sources of information must be considered and judged primarily within the cultural contexts to which they belong. The nominated component parts preserve the original forms and layouts of the different funerary structures, as well as their original locations. The original wider settings of the tombs have not been studied, as confirmed by the additional information supplied by the State Party in February 2026.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据Authenticity章节是否明确认为条件已满足/已证明初判。

property_assessments.conclusion_en: Neither the component parts nor their buffer zones are reported to have any inhabitants. Nevertheless, the management plan mentions the need to implement natural disaster prevention measures, including the relocation of two herder households from the buffer zones at Duurlig Nars (component part 2) and Gol Mod II (component part 4). The initial delimitation of the boundaries of the nominated component parts and buffer zones were corrected and updated by the State Party in November 2025, in response to questions posed by ICOMOS. Revised maps were also submitted at that time. Each component part is delimited with a boundary that accords with national legislation, with a minimum distance of 200 metres from the outermost tombs.

property_assessments.conclusion_en:
In its interim report, ICOMOS requested that the State Party provide additional information on the process of extending the buffer zone, including a map showing the planned revised buffer zone and the key view lines that the extension will cover, as well as the timeframe for completing it.

In February 2026, the State Party responded with a proposal for a revised buffer zone, covering the historic community areas of Mueang Phra Wiang and the old town from the Ayutthaya period. Furthermore, a height-control zone with a radius of one kilometre from the Great Reliquary is demarcated. The revision process for the buffer zone is expected to be completed by 2029. ICOMOS welcomes this initiative and recommends that a revised map be submitted to ICOMOS and the World Heritage Centre upon its completion.

property_assessments.conclusion_en: ICOMOS considers that the Solar Furnace reflects a different theme from that of the overall nomination and would merit an ad hoc comparative analysis with its direct comparator, the Solar Furnace in Odeillo (France), constructed between 1962 and 1968, following experiments begun in 1949. ICOMOS considers that, despite its weaknesses, the comparative analysis justifies consideration of this property for the World Heritage List, with the exclusion of the Big Solar Furnace (component part 2).

ICOMOS considers that the conditions of integrity can be demonstrated by the ensemble formed by the nine component parts located in Tashkent, excluding the Big Solar Furnace (component part 2), which does not reflect the proposed Outstanding Universal Value.

property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据ICOMOS是否认可至少一项申报标准初判；具体每条标准的认可/不认可见criteria_assessments。

property_assessments.conclusion_en: Integrity is a measure of the wholeness and intactness of the attributes that convey the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. For a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole and how each component part contributes to that value. The integrity of the Aalto Works is based on the influential structures, features and spaces created by the Aalto studio that best illustrate the integration of a new international architectural language with traditional local ideas during the modernisation of society in the 20th century by using sensitivity and a human-centred approach to architecture and design. Overall, the component parts are intact and retain their relationship with their associated landscapes and settings. Most retain interiors with significant fittings and furniture. Many sustain their original use, or sympathetic functions. The Paimio Sanatorium (component part 002) is the most vulnerable of the nominated component parts in terms of sustaining an appropriate use and needing significant conservation interventions, though there are mechanisms in place to mitigate potential losses. ICOMOS considers that the integrity of the nominated series as a whole as well as the integrity of each of the component parts have been demonstrated.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据Integrity章节是否明确认为条件已满足/已证明初判。

property_assessments.conclusion_en: The authenticity of the nominated property is based on the form and design, materials and substance, use and function, location and setting as well as spirit and feeling, articulated through a series of thirteen component parts. Authenticity depends on the degree of credibility and truthfulness of information sources on the value of the nominated property. These sources of information must be considered and judged primarily within the cultural contexts to which they belong. ICOMOS considers that all the nominated component parts have preserved their forms and designs. Careful conservation and management of each component part have ensured that the authenticity of these attributes is sustained. In some cases, previous work that had an impact on design authenticity has been reversed in recent conservation projects.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据Authenticity章节是否明确认为条件已满足/已证明初判。

property_assessments.conclusion_en: The estimated population within the nominated property totals 384 people residing in four of the thirteen component parts. There are 4,615 people living within the proposed buffer zones (2023-2024 figures). The boundaries of the component parts are clearly identified and delineated. Most refer to the historical layout, adopting the current legal or administrative divisions, and follow the boundaries of the plots where buildings and groups of buildings are located. Most of the proposed boundaries are consistent with national heritage or other protective designations of the land register.
property_assessments.rationale_zh: 依据Boundaries章节对遗产区/缓冲区边界是否充分、是否需修订的表述初判。

ICOMOS considers that, despite its shortcomings, the comparative analysis justifies consideration of this property for the World Heritage List.
