criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the nominated property serves as an exceptional testament to the power and influence of the Muslim faith in the Middle Ages among the nomadic and semi-nomadic tribes of the Aral-Caspian region. It is linked to the spread of the Sufi interpretation of Islam and the founder of the Yasawi Sufi Order, Khoja Ahmed Yasawi. Over time, the Sufi preachers gained the status of auliye (saints), and the rock-carved complexes where they were teaching and some are buried became destinations for pilgrims and tourists. ICOMOS observes that the events, living traditions, ideas, beliefs or artistic or literary works that form the basis for this criterion must themselves be of outstanding universal significance, and must be demonstrated directly or tangibly in the nominated property. It is not clear from the description of the nominated p roperty and justification provided what the State Party consider to be of outstanding universal significance and in what tangible way it is specifically associated with the nominated property. In the February 2026 response to the interim report the State Party clearly links the justification of the criterion (vi) to the living pilgrimage tradition to the places associated with Sufi saints and masters at the same time admitting that the contribution of pilgrimage practices to the evolution of sacred practices requires f urther research. The assertion that the nominated property uniquely testifies to the strength of the medieval Muslim faith and the spread of Sufi Islam among the nomadic and semi - nomadic tribes of the Aral-Caspian region does not fit with the definition of this criterion with the selected criterion. Instead, this argument and the tradition of pilgrimage as a vital component of the living cultural tradition of the nominated property are appropriately addressed under the justification for criterion (iii). ICOMOS considers that this criterion is not demonstrated.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 vi 未得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the States Parties on the grounds that the FerganaSyrdarya Corridor was an extensive transport network that facilitated cultural exchanges across a central area of Eurasia over a very long period. The east-west interactions were initiated in the 2 nd century BCE by the Han general Zhang Qian, but the States Parties also emphasise the interactions between the nomadic steppe region to the north and north-east, and the agricultural-oasis-pastoral region to the south and south-west. Overall, the Silk Roads enabled close diplomatic, economic and cultural ties across Asia and the Mediterranean. According to the States Parties, t he evidence for interchange can be seen in written records and archaeological artefacts with diverse origins, such as coins, gems, silk, mirrors, porcelain and glass. The river section in this Corridor also facilitated certain types of trade, such as livestock, and also slaves. The Corridor also demonstrates the spread of Islam, and various phases of state formation in Central Asia. The diversity of religious sites reflects the introduction of faiths from west and south Asia and their eastward movement along the routes. The inclusion of Zoroastrian, Buddhist, Christian and Islamic sites is distinctive. ICOMOS considers that the Fergana-Syrdarya Corridor was of strategic significance and involved both east-west and north-south interactions. The movements of people, trade, exchanges of goods, crafts and technologies are potentially relevant but specific evidence of interchange has not been provided, and the chronologies in relation to the exchanges are not clearly set out. At this stage, while it is evident that the Fergana-Syrdarya Corridor facilitated interchanges that could meet this criterion, the nominated component parts and evidence presented do not yet present a coherent network. At this stage, evidence for the intensity, specificities and long-term impacts of the cultural exchanges is generalised. The geology of this region gave access to rich resources such as metals and semi-precious minerals. These were important for trade as raw materials and for the production of objects which are found locally and across the Silk 128 Roads. This aspect is not well -addressed by the nomination and should be incorporated into the recommended review of the selection of the component parts. Regarding the empire systems that vied for control of the area in different periods, these could be more fully elaborated. The political and strategic significance of the nominated Corridor should be reflected in component parts that reflect the impact s of these different polities. There are a range of component parts that could achieve this, but they are not clearly described in this way, and there could be additional component parts that should have been selected for these reasons. It would have been useful to discuss specifically how “ close diplomatic, economic, and cultural ties with Eastern Turkestan, China, Western Asia, Rome, and Southern Africa ” are attested by the specific component parts selected. The States Parties advised that a chronological synthesis framework is being developed correlating the principal political phases including Kushan, Turkic Khaganates, Samanid, Karakhanid, Mongol and Timurid periods, although this was not provided to ICOMOS during the evaluation of the nominated propert y. Addressing how these periods and functions interact will be useful in enhancing the justification of this criterion. ICOMOS also notes that the points made about the symbiosis between nomadic and sedentary peoples in the Fergana-Syrdarya Corridor is potentially interesting but not yet well demonstrated by tangible evidence or included in the comparative analysis. A more nuanced or complex portrayal of these relations could be developed. While c ooperative exchange is highlighted, conflict, competition, and shifting power relations were also part of these interactions. It should be demonstrated how nomadic and sedentary societies were mutually transformed. ICOMOS considers that there is a potential for this criterion to be justified for the Fergana-Syrdarya Corridor through improvements to the narrative for the potential Outstanding Universal Value, and the revised selection and justification of component parts. The Corridor is likely to be able to represent histories of cultural interactions and interchanges, demonstrating cultural and religious shifts over a prolonged period, and the forms of cultural production that emerge from the confluence of empires and polities. It should be possible to present additional evidence of multidirectional exchanges (both imports and exports). There is a potential to demonstrate specific cultural interchanges between nomadic and agricultural peoples through the northsouth connections with the steppe route, including historic tensions related to land uses and access to water. To demonstrate this criterion, it will be necessary to directly link site-specific evidence of cultural exchanges such as artefacts, inscriptions, or architectural remains at nominated component parts. In the additional information of February 2026, the States Parties advised that further revisions would strengthen the justification for criterion (ii) through clearer demonstration of technological transfers (hydraulic engineering, glazed ceramics, metallurgy). ICOMOS considers that this criterion is not demonstrated at this stage.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 ii 未得到证明。
criteria_assessments.analyst_note:
同事版四级分档待确认：○；ICOMOS does not consider that any of the criteria have been justified at this stage due to issues with the justification for theproposed Outstanding Universal Value and the description and selection of component parts. At this stage, criteria (ii) and (iii), and possibly also criterion (v), are considered to be the most promising bases for revision and further development of this nomination. 【现阶段没有认可，但是认为几条价值标准具有进一步发展潜力。】

人工确认：采用同事版○的四级语义；保留原 accepted_by_icomos 布尔判断。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the States Parties on the grounds that the component parts demonstrate cultural traditions associated with water management for agriculture, fortification and the coexistence of different religions within urban settlements. Craft traditions are also represented in many of the nominated component parts, including artisanal production of pottery and glass, and mining. These distinctive cultural traditions of this Corridor are understood as arising from, and sustained by, the exchanges between nomadic pastoralists and sedentary agricultural communities, visible in material culture and settlement patterns. ICOMOS considers that the arguments outlined for this criterion are potentially relevant, but that the presented evidence does not yet demonstrat e with precision which attributes of the selected component parts directly embody the claimed traditions. The specific nomadic and agricultural cultures (and cultural traditions) of these areas need to be more systematically described. Those that are mentioned are presented unevenly across the serial nomination. For example, water management is described for the sites of Sauran (KZ-03), Sardoba (UZ-14) and Zhetyasar (KZ05 a-k), but detail is lacking for the other component parts, suggesting an over-reliance on a few component parts to demonstrate this aspect. Because karez and qanat systems are found in other parts of Central Asia and the Middle East, their importance should be explained in terms of the cultural traditions of the nominated Corridor, and how they enabled key developments throughout the Fergana-Syrdarya Corridor. More consideration could be given to the breeding and trade in horses, which is a potential area of distinctiveness of this C orridor. This was central to cultural and military life in this part of Central Asia. Horses from the Fergana Valley became renowned, especially to the elites ruling the north China plains. The trade in horses also supported the links and interchanges between the settled and steppe communities that are central to this criterion. Regarding the claims about religious diversity and coexistence, ICOMOS notes that few areas within Central Asia have witnessed the successive emergence and evolution of these diverse religious traditions. However, there is little evidence presented to demonstrate this at the nominated component parts. I n general, a range of different traditions are mentioned by the States Parties, but there is little explanation of how 129 they are inter -related or how they demonstrate the specificities and outstanding attributes of these parts of the Silk Roads over time. For this criterion, the cultural traditions need to be elaborated beyond a description of different functions and land uses. In the additional information of February 2026, the States Parties advised that further revisions would strengthen the justification for criterion (iii), in part by linking component parts to historically attested polities such as Kangju and Dayuan whose political power was grounded in control of the nominated Corridor. ICOMOS considers that the interactions between nomadic and settled communities are a potentially important focus for the consideration of criterion (iii). How this is specifically expressed in the nominated component parts should be demonstrated. To strengthen the justification for this criterion, it is necessary to link the cultural traditions and selected sites more explicitly. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is potentially relevant for this nomination, but that there is insufficient detail about the layering of outstanding cultural traditions to demonstrate it at this stage. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is not demonstrated at this stage.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 iii 未得到证明。
criteria_assessments.analyst_note:
同事版四级分档待确认：○；

人工确认：采用同事版○的四级语义；保留原 accepted_by_icomos 布尔判断。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the States Parties on the grounds that the selected sites demonstrate an architectural typology. Information about building styles and wall paintings has been provided as evidence. In the additional information of October 2025, the States Parties further explained that this criterion is considered to represent a typological sequence, and that taken together, the serial component parts represent an outstanding type of landscape termed a “city-water-agrosystem-defense-pilgrimage” continuum that is characteristic of the Syrdarya River axis. This is distinct from the desert-oasis or purely mountain-passage models found in other corridors of the Silk Roads. The States Parties also consider that the chronological diversity of these elements is evidence of a long developmental process, and adaptations to changing hydrological and political contexts. In the nomination dossier, only six of the fortyeight component parts were indicated as contributing to the proposed justification for criterion (iv): the Historical and Architectural Complex of Uzgen (KG-01), Hazrati Shoh Mausoleum (TJ -01), Bunjikat (Kal a-I Kahkaha I -III settlements TJ -06), Abdullatif Sultan Madrassa (Kok Gumbaz, TJ-07), Otrar Settlement (KZ-01) and Zhankent Settlement (KZ-06). In response to questions about this from ICOMOS, the State s Parties provided additional information in October 2025 that included a table that identifies the intended contribution of each of the fortyeight component parts to the justification of criterion (iv); and in the additional information of February 2026, the States Parties advised that further revisions would clarify the applicability of criterion (iv) through the demonstration of complex irrigationbased urban system in semi -arid riverine landscapes. ICOMOS appreciates these clarifications but considers that th e selection of the component parts does not sufficiently support the argument s put forward for this criterion. While it is possible to include multiple types and/or periods, the diversity of the selected component parts is a poor basis to support this criterion. While every case is individually assessed, it is also noted that neither of the two previously inscribed Silk Roads corridors has been recognised on the basis of criterion (iv). ICOMOS acknowledges that some of the component parts identified for this criterion are individually significant examples of architectural ensemble types and decoration. However, it is not clear how the nominated Corridor as a whole is an outstanding example of a type of building, architectural or technological ensemble or landscape that illustrates a significant stage in the history of the Silk Roads network; and the explanation that it is an outstanding type of landscape that is a “city-water-agrosystem-defense-pilgrimage” continuum is not clear to ICOMOS. ICOMOS considers that the argument supporting this criterion is not robust for these reasons. Although the additional information expanded the justification to include all the component parts, this has been added to the existing selection of the component parts, rather than guiding their selection from the outset. ICOMOS considers that criterion (iv) is not an ideal choice for this serial nomination that aims to demonstrate such a high degree of typological diversity and chronological span, and that the arguments put forward by the States Parties for criterion (iv) are potentially more relevant for the justification of other criteria. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is not demonstrated.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 iv 未得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the States Parties on the grounds that, over centuries, local communities adapted their agriculture and water management to the shifting courses of the rivers in this Corridor. The Syrdarya basin potentially illustrates the utili sation of the river as a waterway for trade along the Silk Roads. Additional information was provided in October 2025, including a table that outlines the specific contribution of each component part to this criterion. The States Parties argue that this criterion is expressed as an extended e vidential continuum where locally diverse types of hydro130 engineering form a single integrated system: the mastery of shifting channels of the Syrdarya, the transformation of natural branches into irrigation arteries, the development and operation of subsurface galleries and karez systems, the construction of sardobas and other storage facilities, the protection of distribution nodes through fortification, and agricultural adaptations to changing hydrodynamics. The course of the Syrdarya River changed significantly across the long timeframe covered by this nomination. In the additional information received in February 2026, the States Parties refer to the archaeological evidence of repeated reorganisation of settlement layouts in response to shifts in the Syrdarya River channels. Irrigation canals, flood-control structures and stratigraphic evidence document long-term hydraulic management as a structuring force. The States Parties consider river-based adaptation to be a defining characteristic of this Corridor, and its riverine character to be central to the application of this criterion. The States Parties also emphasise the importance of the mix of terrestrial caravan routes with a fluvial communication axis. The Syrdarya River functioned as an economic artery linking settlements longitudinally. In the additional information received in February 2026, the States Parties advised that monumental port installations have not survived, but that the distribution patter ns of imported ceramics and metal goods, and settlement positioning relative to navigable sections of the river support the interpretation of functional fluvial trade during specific historical phases. The river-based axis facilitated connectivity between steppe polities to the north and urban centres to the south, reinforcing the Corridor’s role as a longitudinal integrative system rather than merely a transversal route. ICOMOS notes that a focus on water management and irrigation systems is also a strong element of the Chang’an-Tianshan and the ZarafshanKarakum Corridors. The additional information provided by the States Parties is potentially interesting and could be further developed to strengthen the justification for this criterion. What needs to be better articulated in this case is the specificity of these aspects in the Fergana-Syrdarya Corridor, particularly the uses of the rivers, the water management systems, and the adaptations to changing water courses over time. These need to be more visibly described for the nominated property and better linked to the selection of the component parts. ICOMOS notes with concern that the States Parties identified the relevance of criterion (v) in relation to a portion of the overall nominated serial property. Only one component part was initially indicated as demonstrating irrigation (UZ-14 Sardoba (Agachli)). This was addressed in part in the additional information received in February 2026. Other component parts listed as contributing to this criterion include : the karez system of the Sauran Settlement (KZ-03), which represents the northernmost known application of this sophisticated underground irrigation technology; the Syganak Settlement (KZ-04) which demonstrates relocation of the settlement in response to the river’s migration; the Zhankent Settlement (KZ-06) which exemplifies the importance of irrigation networks; and the Zhetyasar Archaeological Landscape (component parts KZ-05 a-k) which illustrates adaptation to the changing hydrology of the delta. A nother six component parts were indicated as relevant to criterion (v) in the additional information provided in October 2025, all located in Kyrgyzstan. In the additional information of February 2026, the States Parties advised that further revisions would clarify the applicability of criterion (v) through the demonstration of complex irrigationbased urban systems in semi-arid riverine landscapes. Despite the presence of individually important sites, at this stage, the nomination presents more as a collection of individual examples than an interconnected series that demonstrate patterns of land use or settlement in an outstanding way. This poses a difficulty for applying this criterion to the nominated serial property as a whole and suggests the need to review and reconsider the selection of component parts and the way they are described. ICOMOS considers that the additional arguments provided for this criterion offer the potential for the revision of this nomination and the review of the selection of component parts. While the nominated Corridor could demonstrate distinctive forms of settlement and land use, at this stage, criterion (v) is too weakly justified on the basis of the selected component parts and evidence provided to be sustained. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is not demonstrated at this stage.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 v 未得到证明。
criteria_assessments.analyst_note:
同事版四级分档待确认：○；

人工确认：采用同事版○的四级语义；保留原 accepted_by_icomos 布尔判断。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the States Parties on the grounds that the nominated component parts are linked to numerous significant events associated with religion, living traditions and notable people, as well as exchanges in culture, science, technology and the arts. The nomination dossier highlights evidence for the spread of Zoroastrianism and Islam through extant fire temples, shrines, and sacred complexes, as well as the enduring continuity of traditions such as silk weaving which together provide material testimony to the role of the nominated Corridor in shaping spiritual and cultural practices of universal resonance. ICOMOS requested additional information about this justification, which was received in October 2025, including a table of how each component part contributes to this criterion. ICOMOS considers that the justification and evidence presented is fragmented at the level of the nominated series as a whole; and that there is a heavy reliance on the associations with notable people without direct and tangible links with their legacies. The spread of religions and the evidence in monuments, archaeological 131 remains, rituals and traditions are of potential interest but are better demonstrated in relation to criteria (ii) and (iii). ICOMOS considers that this criterion is not demonstrated. ICOMOS does not consider that any of the criteria have been justified at this stage due to issues with the justification for the proposed Outstanding Universal Value and the description and selection of component parts. At this stage, criteria (ii) and (ii i), and possibly also criterion (v), are considered to be the most promising bases for revision and further development of this nomination.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 vi 未得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the nominated serial property exhibits an important interchange of human values in the creation and construction of Xiongnu noble tombs that reflect influences from the funeral cultures of the Han Dynasty, the Nannang (Nangnang), and the Altai. The archaeological artefacts found in the burials, including lacquered cups and utensils created for the royal court of the Han Dynasty in China, embroidered textiles from Persia, stone and glass beads crafted in Central Asia, Greco-Roman silverware, and Roman glassware, show that the Xiongnu were directly involved in trade and cultural relations between the Eastern and Western worlds from the 3 rd century BCE to the 2nd century CE. ICOMOS considers that the tomb construction as well as the foreign artefacts that accompanied the dead indicate important external contacts of the Xiongnu. However, participation in information and trade networ ks is very common among cultures and cannot be considered an outstanding trait. The role the Xiongnu played in fostering trade along the Steppe Route of the Silk Roads is highlighted, connecting the Eastern and Western worlds, but the artefacts in the tombs only establish the participation of the Xiongnu in this trade; they do not define the role they played and the influence they had on other cultures. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is not demonstrated.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 ii 未得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that The Cemetery Complexes of the Xiongnu Nobility testify in an exceptional way to the extinct Xiongnu funerary culture. It provides insights into the burial 147 customs, rituals, established traditions and tomb construction of the nomadic Xiongnu nobles. Even though there exists already more than a century of research on these tombs, much about the Xiongnu funerary culture, with its burial customs and rituals, is still unknown or not well understood. Nevertheless, ICOMOS considers that the tombs with their deep burial chambers, satellite graves and sacrificial structures, as well as with their rich offerings, make the nominated serial property an exceptional testimony to the extinct Xiongnu funerary culture. ICOMOS considers that The Cemetery Complexes of the Xiongnu Nobility are an exceptional expression of, and the richest source of insight into, the functioning of this culture of the Central Asian Steppe. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is demonstrated.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 iii 已得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the nominated serial property is the best extant example of a specific type of Central Asian funerary structure from the rd century BCE to the 2nd century CE. ICOMOS considers that the size and especially the depth (over twenty metres) of some of the tombs are proof of the technical and organisational capabilities of Xiongnu society. The use terraces and the dromoi made construction in sandy soils possible. However, the details and the evolution of the construction process require further study, and ICOMOS considers that the construction process remains not documented enough for the tombs to be considered an outstanding example of a typology of building or technological ensemble. Furthermore, no significant stage in human history that would be illustrated by the nominated property has been clearly defined. Finally, the description of the tomb construction as a cultural tradition justifies better criterion (iii). ICOMOS considers that this criterion is not demonstrated. ICOMOS considers that the Xiongnu nobility burials, which include rich grave offerings as well as satellite graves and sacrificial structures, include attributes that document the Xiongnu burial culture well. Many of the known graves have not yet been excavated and represent a significant opportunity for future investigation. The selection of component parts testifies to the extension of the Xiongnu funerary culture throughout the empire and to the economic power of the nomadic Xiongnu society. ICOMOS con siders that the nominated property meets criterion (iii), but that criterion (ii) and (iv) have not been demonstrated.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 iv 未得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that Wat Phra Mahathat remarkably exhibits religious diversity, spiritual continuity and vital elements that capture the waves, art forms and essence of important religions such as Hinduism, Mahayana Buddhism and Theravada Buddhism, which have been transmitted across the south of maritime Asia for about 1,500 years. The architectural forms, Buddhist practices and living traditions of Wat Phra Mahathat also had a significant impact on other places in Thailand, Malaysia and beyond. ICOMOS considers that to justify this criterion, the fusion of the ideas or values should be deemed exceptional and influential over time or within a specific cultural area. While the Great Reliquary has demonstrated the exceptionality of the fusion of ideas, its lasting inf luence has not been adequately established. In the additional information of November 2025, the State Party provided examples of stupas constructed in the subsequent dynasties in other regions of Thailand, Myanmar and Malaysia that were influenced by the Great Reliquary. Based on the evidence provided by the State Party, ICOMOS considers that this criterion is demonstrated.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 ii 已得到证明。
criteria_assessments.analyst_note: 同事版汇总结论补充：ICOMOS considers that the nominated property meets criteria(ii) and (vi).

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that Wat Phra Mahathat is the spiritual centre of the region, with the living traditions of diverse belief systems as witnessed in daily practices, merit makings and annual ceremonies, including the annual clothwrapping ceremony for the Great Reliquary, the shamanistic Nora dance, ancestor worship, Brahminic rituals, rare artworks and local chronicles. These traditions vividly represent the 159 relationships between this sacred site and its surrounding communities and inspire other monasteries and communities in Thailand and beyond. ICOMOS considers that to justify this criterion, the associated traditions should be of outstanding universal significance. ICOMOS acknowledges the association of the numerous living traditions, and notes that the Nora dance and the chronicles are of outstanding universal significance. ICOMOS therefore considers that this criterion has been demonstrated. ICOMOS considers that, in addition to the attributes identified by the State Party, the overall layout of the monastery, the spatial arrangement of the religious buildings, and the views to and from the Great Reliquary are also attributes that convey the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. ICOMOS considers that the nominated property meets criteria (ii) and (vi).
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 vi 已得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the Modernist architecture of Tashkent represents a significant interchange of human values in technology, planning, and design. The city acted as a crossroads where global modernist trends, such as prefabrication and curtain walls, were reinterpreted through the lens of local cultural, social, and environmental contexts. This exchange was facilitated by the movement of architects and engineers from across the USSR, particularly during post-earthquake reconstruction. The resulting “Tashkent model” created a regional variant of modernism that was later shared trans -nationally, influencing urban development and socialist international cooperation in other non-Western nations, particularly in Africa and nonaligned countries. ICOMOS considers that, whil st the urban and architectural reconstruction experiment in Tashkent may have influenced other cities and capitals across the Soviet republics, it is much less evident how, and to what extent, Tashkent could be seen as a model that directly influenced urban and architectural developments in nonaligned countries. Substantial further research would be needed to justify this criterion from this perspective. The fusion of modernist architectural language with local traditional motifs and craftsmanship is not specific to Tashkent; it can be found elsewhere in the USSR and in specific manifestations of modernism globally. Therefore, this interchange would not suffice to justify this criterion. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is not demonstrated.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 ii 未得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the nominated serial property is an outstanding example of a modernist urban landscape representing a significant stage in history: the era of post -war socialist modernisation. It illustrates a nonWestern model of development designed to demonstrate progress and equality outside of capitalist frameworks. The “ensembles” of the city integrate monumental public buildings, infrastructure, and green networks into a unified system that structured daily life according to socialist values. These structures represent the technological and ideological peak of lateSoviet architecture, particularly through advancements in seismic -resistant design and industrialised prefabrication within a regional context. ICOMOS considers that Tashkent Modernist Architecture outstandingly illustrates developments of architecture and urban planning during the post-Thaw period in a Soviet socialist society. The series of the nominated urban component parts 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, and represents an outstanding ensemble of urban foci that, consciously combining modernist themes and aspirations with reinterpreted local motifs and building patterns, has contributed to shaping the modernist urban form of Tashkent. Tashkent Modernist Architecture is one of the finest examples of architectural and urban design, reflecting, in an exceptional and specific manner, the broader movement of “localised modernism ” spread across the Soviet realm, developed within the cultural and climatic environment of Central Asia. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is demonstrated. ICOMOS considers that, in addition to the attributes identified in the nomination dossier, urban, visual and functional links among the component parts are also essential in conveying the proposed Outstanding Universal Value and criteria. Landscaping and urban vegetation in the broader urban setting also contribute to understanding and appreciation of Central Asian urban and architectural modernism of Tashkent. ICOMOS considers it important to identify specific tangible attributes for each component part to ensure they are taken into account in management and conservation actions. In the October 2025 additional information, the State Party also provided a table that explicitly shows how each component part contributes to illustrating each attribute. ICOMOS considers that the nominated property meets criterion (iv) but that criterion (ii) has not been demonstrated.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 iv 已得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the nominated property contributes in an exceptional way to the th-century modernisation of society by integrating a new international language and traditional national ideas in both architecture and design to meet the needs of individuals, families, communities and society, serving as a symbol of Finland’s independence while integrating scientific progress with a distinct human dimension. Aalto’s approach prioriti ses the sensory human experience through tactile materials (wood, brick, copper), unique forms, and thoughtful lighting. The designs deeply integrate locality, climate, and seasonal cycles, often dissolving boundaries between indoor and outdoor spaces to enhance well -being. Rooted in individual experience, this method combines art and technology to create functional, high-quality environments that foster citizenship, community and solidarity. The Aalto Works, as a manifestation of a collective design, stand out as a living legacy where innovative mass production meets traditional craftsmanship, establishing an enduring tradition of empathetic and sustainable architecture. To justify this criterion, the tangible attributes of a nominated property must demonstrate that ideas or values about developments in architecture, technology, monumental ar ts, town -planning or landscape design were exported or imported and transformed exceptionally, or that they reflect a local fusion of ideas/values that could be deemed exceptional and influential over time or within a specific cultural area. ICOMOS considers that the nominated serial property comprises representative modern buildings, ensembles, and sites that significantly influenced the formation and development of modernism, and which continue to impact contemporary architecture worldwide. Through an organic functionalist approach, the Aalto Works adapted global Modern Movement ideas to local contexts, seamlessly integrating exteriors with interiors and harmoni sing with their settings. This architecture offers an outstanding response to universal societal needs, prioriti sing citizen well-being throughout the moderni sation process. Bringing together modern concepts and building traditions, this collective design approach represents outstanding responses to various universal societal needs of individuals and communities, through the introduction of a new language of form, materials, and production methods, executed with exceptional sensitivity, simplicity, and integrity. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is demonstrated. 189 ICOMOS considers that the nominated property meets criterion (ii).
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 ii 已得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the nominated property offers an outstanding example of the confrontation between two systems, a defensive one represented by the Atlantic Wall, and an offensive one through the maritime assault prepared by the Allies; a confrontation whose scale and intensity are exceptional in history. The landscape of the DDay Landing Beaches also illustrates, through preserved vestiges, military cemeteries, and commemorative monuments, the immediate desire to convey the historical significance of this event. ICOMOS considers that the proposed justification for this criterion raises two fundamental issues. The first concerns the insufficient number of tangible and immovable attributes. The proposed attributes included within the nominated property that reflect the defensive system are very limited in comparison with those that remain along the Normandy coast. Elements reflecting the offensive system are mostly mobile and were not intended to be permanent. Furthermore, they are not subject to conservation measures. Natural elements have also undergone significant changes in certain areas of the nominated property due to ongoing processes, such as erosion and debris accumulation. The exceptional scale and intensity of the confrontation would not necessarily suggest Outstanding Universal Value. The second issue is linked to the fact the proposed justification for this criterion is more associative than typological, which does not align with the definition underpinning the narrative for this criterion. Consequently, the justification put forward by the State Party fails to demonstrate that the nominated property offers “an outstanding example of a type of building, architectural or technological ensemble or landscape”. 203 These concerns were raised in the ICOMOS interim report. In the additional information of February 2026, the State Party decided to withdrew this criterion, to focus only on the associative and memorial dimensions of the nominated property.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 iv 已得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the nominated property stands as a symbol of immense hope and progress towards freedom. Long awaited by those oppressed under the Nazi yoke across Europe, the D-Day Landings marked a turning point in this struggle; they illustrate how a collective alliance of nations committed to these values established a lasting peace, whose universal significance remains embedded in this landscape. ICOMOS considers that this memorial construct raises significant questions. Firstly, ICOMOS considers that messages of peace cannot constitute the primary argument to justify a potential Outstanding Universal Value, insofar as the aspiration for peace or freedom cannot be measured or compared with that of other sites. While these messages and values are facets of many sites of memory associated with recent conflicts, it is nonetheless impossible to compare them to determine which messages of peace or freedom possess outstanding universal significance and which do not. ICOMOS considers that these messages and values pertain more to the process of reconciliation than to the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. Furthermore, the justification for criterion (vi) must “be directly or tangibly associated with events or living traditions, with ideas, or with beliefs, with artistic and literary works ” expressing outstanding universal significance, which must therefore be established. In this regard, ICOMOS finds it difficult to assess the exceptionality of the D-Day Landings in isolation without situating this event within a broader global context. The Second World War was an event of outstanding universal significance, and Operation Neptune took place within this complex context, as evidenced by the multiplicity of theatres of operation in various countries worldwide, some of which were also decisive for the outcome of this global conflict. However, the nomination dossier appears to establish an implicit equivalence among the liberation of France following the Normandy Landings, that of Europe, and the end of the war itself. This reasoning obscures other operations of crucial importance. For example, the liberation of Berlin by the Red Army occurred almost a year later, in May 1945, as well as the battles on nonEuropean fronts, such as the defeat of Japan in August 1945. While t he Normandy Landings were undoubtedly a decisive turning point in the Second World War, they were nevertheless part of a series of interconnected operations resulting from cooperation between the Allies across several theatres of operation. Recognising this complexity and the plurality of historical experiences within the justification for criterion (vi) appears essential to justify this criterion while valuing the symbolic dimension of the nominated property. Finally, ICOMOS notes that the nomination dossier does not provide an in-depth reflection on the evolution of postwar commemoration and the changes it has undergone over the decades. The challenge also lies in recognising the diversity of memories, thereby avoiding the limits of universalisation, which would in reality amount to applying one reading of the Second World War. On the basis of these complexities ICOMOS requested in its interim report whether a revision to the proposed justification for this criterion could be envisaged. In the additional information of February 2026, the State Party provided further details on the context of the D-Day Landings associated with the nominated property, and complementary reflections on memorialisation, alongside a revised justification focused solely on criterion (vi). ICOMOS considers that this additional information contributes to provide a better contextualised explanation of the memorialisation process, recognising its complexities and multi vocality. However, the proposed revised justification for criterion (vi) shifts away from the association with the historical event itself and its memorialisation, extending well beyond these aspects to assign political meaning and associations to it. The revised justification presents the nominated property as associated not only with the Normandy D -Day Landings but also, by extension, with the foundation of the United Nations. ICOMOS considers that the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property cannot be focused only on abstract notions, such as hope, associated with the D -Day Landings. It further considers that the proposed revised justification for criterion (vi) makes an unwarranted leap in attempting to establish a direct and tangible association between the nominated property, the foundation of the United Nations and the resulting post-war international order, as such links are not credibly expressed through its attributes. Conversely, ICOMOS recognises that, having largely retained their appearance from the Second World War, the Normandy Beaches constitute a natural memorial capable of evoking powerful memories of the D -Day Landings. As part of a sequence of pivotal operations across multiple theatres in Eastern and Southern Europe, North Africa, and the AsiaPacific region, reflecting unprecedented multinational cooperation and deployment of resources, the DDay Landings marked the start of a long and costly campaign to liberate northwestern Europe from the yoke of the Third Reich and ultimately contributed to the end of the war in Europe. 204 ICOMOS considers that this criterion is demonstrated based on the abovementioned revised justification. ICOMOS concurs with the attributes identified by the State Party but also considers that some geographical and landscape features, along with surviving defensive and offensive remains across the wider coastal stretch and buffer zone, further reinforce the memorial associations of the nominated property. ICOMOS considers that the nominated property meets criterion (vi) based on the revised justification proposed in this evaluation text. Criterion (iv) was withdrawn by the State Party in February 2026.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 vi 已得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the nominated serial property represents an exceptional milestone in the process of diffusion of Capetian military architecture in the 13 th-14th centuries, from Western Europe towards the Baltic and the Danube River regions, as well as the Mediterranean Middle East. The ensemble of fortifications, characterised by architectural cohesion and skilful adaptation of technical solutions to the landscape setting, represents an architectural model, which spread beyond the borders of the seneschalty. Its territorial organisation influenced the geopolitical developments in the wider region. ICOMOS considers that the system of fortifications can be seen as an outcome of the diffusion of the Capetian military architecture model, transplanted from northern France plains and adapted to the mountainous landscape of south-west France. However, this transfer of technical and architectural knowledge (adaptation) cannot be seen as an important interchange of human values, as it did not involve any idea or concept imported from another culture. ICOMOS also considers that the nominated serial property cannot be seen itself as a milestone in terms of wider diffusion of the Capetian military architecture to other areas. The nominated fortifications may have been part of an indirect process of cultural diffusion but there is no evidence of their direct influence elsewhere, as confirmed by the State Party through the additional information of October 2025. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is not demonstrated.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 ii 未得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the nominated serial property is an exceptional ly homogenous and well -preserved example of a centrally commanded territorial and border military system, which marked the rapid expansion of the kingdom of France towards the Mediterranean world and the Iberian kingdoms. Illustrative of the strengthening and considerable expansion of Capetian power, it is emblematic of the movement towards assertion of sovereign powers that marked th-century Europe. ICOMOS considers that the nominated serial property forms a defensive ensemble with shared architectural language and military functions. The only coherent fortified syst em built by the Capetian kings, it is an exceptional example of the highly standardised Capetian architecture, which historically was part of the rise of the Capetian Crown and its way to control and secure the territory it dominated. It marked a pivotal historic moment in medieval Europe, namely the political shift from feudal fragmentation to the emergence of strong centralised monarchies. The spatial organisation of the nominated fortresses reveals a unified political purpose and a centrally conceived defensive strategy, while their material fabric and architectural language display the vocabulary of Capetian military design carefully adapted to diverse and often demanding topographies. ICOMOS further considers that while the nominated fortresses were scenes of key historical events related to the conquest of Languedoc, they cannot translate to a full representation of this process. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is demonstrated.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 iv 已得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the nominated serial property bears an exceptional testimony to the living cultural tradition of Italian-style condominio theatres. These theatres represent a fundamental transition, moving from the private domain of the aristocracy and religious institutions to civic institutions accessible to a large paying audience. Their unique financing model, the società condominiale or società di palchettisti (box owners’ society), brought together public bodies and private citizens, particularly from the emerging bourgeoisie, in the construction and management of these spaces. The very architecture of these 18 th and 19th century Italian-style theatres, with their private boxes, stalls and galleries, was deliberately designed to reflect and display the social stratification of the time, embodying the principle of “see and be seen”. Besides from being simple performance venues, these theatres operated as multifunctional and autonomous social and cultural centres, veritable indoor public squares with foyers and ridotti. The longevity and continued use of these theatres, which continue to host a wide variety of cultural events today, confirm their status as a living tradition and as pillars of local civic life. ICOMOS considers that the development of condominio Italian-style theatres in the centres of small and mediumsized towns in Italy bears witness to a phenomenon that saw widespread expansion in the 19 th century in Italy and Europe, followed by Latin America. These theatres, which first appeared in Italian provincial cities from the late 18 th century, were one of the few places where groups of representatives of the emerging bourgeoisie could gather, and where public opinion was formed. These theatres, financed via società condominiali involving public bodies and private individuals in the construction and management of these spaces, embody the transition from the private domain of the aristocracy and religious institutions to civic institutions accessible to a wide audience. With a rare density of still-preserved theatres, the regions of Emilia-Romagna, Marche and Umbria, which form part of the former Papal States, have fostered the development of innovative solutions for theatres, particularly for the distribution of tiered boxes around the stalls, reflectin g the participation of different social classes, seated according to their means, and testifying to the new social hierarchy. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is demonstrated on the basis of a reduced nominated series comprising the following component parts: 02, 03, 04, 05, 06, 07, 09, 12, 16, 17.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 iii 已得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the Italian-style theatres, built in the th and th centuries in Central Italy, constitute a representative group of condominio-type theatres. Central Italy made a vital contribution to the development of the auditorium with tiered rows of boxes, an architectural model that enjoyed lasting success in Europe and beyond. The value of the nominated property lies in its ability to illustrate the evolution of the Italian-style theatre through a range of architectural, acoustic and stage design solutions. The main aspects of this evolution are the transformation of the floor plan/shape, the design of ceilings and other acoustic features, the stage machinery and the layout of the forestage. The theatres of the nominated serial property are still operating. Concentrated in the territory of the former Papal Legations, they have managed, despite periods of interruption to their operation, to preserve the features of their construction and their link with the social and urban fabric. In its interim report, ICOMOS sought clarification concerning the shapes and layout of the auditorium in the Italian-style theatres, and how these reflected the specific nature of condominio theatres. The additional information of February 2026 specifies that the condominio system, which is based on collective ownership and shared financing, enabled these structures to become the centre of local public life, and fa cilitated the historical transition from aristocratic patronage to bourgeoisie entrepreneurship, which made it an exportable model across the world. The spatial configuration of the tieredbox auditoriums reflects a desire to balance participatory decision-making and the requirements for visibility and prestige. The architecture of these theatres materialises a system of collective governance that ensured their sustainability and cultural success within their communities. Whilst ICOMOS acknowledges that the condominio Italian-style theatres built in the th and 19th centuries in Central Italy are part of a significant period in Italian history (the emergence of the bourgeoisie and the Unification of Italy), ICOMOS also considers that t heir specific contribution to the dissemination and expansion of what was known as the “ Italian style” throughout Europe and Latin America has not been demonstrated. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is not demonstrated.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 iv 未得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the nominated property represents a modern defence system centred around innovative military architecture, hybrid urban planning and advanced artillery expertise, creating a model of territorial defence that influenced European military practice. ICOMOS considers that for this criterion to be justified, the nominated series must provide material evidence of military exchanges of knowledge, techniques and models in military architecture, demonstrating the role of the Raia in broader European developments. 247 ICOMOS considers that the fortifications of the Raia could be further considered as part of a wider European exchange of military ideas, linked to the circulation and development of bastioned fortification from the Renaissance through the 16 th and 17 th centuries. This interpretation requires the frontier system to be considered as a whole, including fortifications on both sides of the PortugueseSpanish border, which were developed in a shared strategic context and in direct response to one another. At this stage, t he nomination lacks concrete documentation of transnational exchanges or adoption of Raia solutions elsewhere, and the comparative analysis with other European and global fortifications is insufficient to demonstrate an important interchange. Moreover, the nominated series does not convincingly demonstrate how innovations developed over time within the Raia were disseminated or influenced military practice beyond the Iberian Peninsula. Justification of this criterion will require a more comprehensive approach, including the chronology of construction, understanding of the movement of military engineers, technological transfers and local adaptations, and deepened comparison with European frontier systems, as well as how and to what extent these interchanges are reflected in relevant fortresses and were important for the development of military architecture. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is not demonstrated.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 ii 未得到证明。
criteria_assessments.analyst_note: 同事版汇总结论补充：ICOMOS considers that, at this stage, none of the criteria have been demonstratedandthat the serial approach is notjustified.

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the nominated property represents significant examples of 17th and 18th century bulwarked constructions combining advanced design, scientific innovation and coordinated strategy to form an effective defence system, contributing to technical and practical developments of European military architecture and technology. ICOMOS considers that, while the component parts represent notable examples of bulwarked fortifications from this period, their outstanding character cannot be fully demonstrated. The limited selection of only three fortresses from a larger system of 143 fortifications does not adequately reflect the full range of typologies, design innovations, and strategic approaches characteristic of the Raia. Furthermore, the nomination dossier lacks sufficient comparative analysis with other contemporary European or global fortifications to establish the outstanding significance of the nominated property. As a result, there is no clear evidence that the nominated component parts, taken together, illustrate a stage of human history at an outstanding level. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is not demonstrated.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 iv 未得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the nominated property represents a heritage of urban planning integrating military and civilian functions with associated intangible cultural practices, reflecting the role of national identity in shaping the frontier, and forming part of the process that marked the independence of Portugal within the Iberian context. Criterion (vi) requires that a property be directly or tangibly associated with events, living traditions, ideas, beliefs, or artistic and literary works of o utstanding universal significance. In this case, the State Party links the nominated property to the historical process of Portuguese independence and to the development of a frontier system integrating military and civilian functions. While these references relate to historically important developments in the Iberian context, ICOMOS considers that the nomination dossier does not sufficiently demonstrate that the associated events or ideas possess outstanding universal significance. Furthermore, the nature of the association between these processes and the specific component parts included in the nomination remains insufficiently defined, and the attributes expressing this link are not clearly identified. The comparative analysis also does not demonstrate that this association is outstanding in relation to other fortified border systems. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is not demonstrated.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 vi 未得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that Ribeira Sacra is an exceptional testimony to a living cultural waterscape that evolved over 1,500 years, with a large number of water works and evidence of cultural knowledge about water management. The State Party considers that the complex settlement patterns of the nominated property demonstrate adapt ation to difficult environmental conditions, including agricultural practices on rugged terrain using socalcos (terraces). The State Party also considers that the different hydraulic technologies for generating energy are significant, including traditional water mills and the 19 th and 20 thcentury hydroelectricity dams and power stations found within the nominated property. T he State Party also considers the interdependence between culture and nature to be central to the proposed Outstanding Universal Value and point s to the intangible cultural heritage traditions of the waterscape. ICOMOS notes the long history of interaction between people and nature in this area, its scenic qualities, and the ways in which the remote and steep terrain and watercourses have been adapted to support human settlements and lifeways. A very wide variety of landscape elements representing multiple historical timeframes have been used to argue for the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. However, ICOMOS does not consider that Ribeira Sacra has been established as an “outstanding example” of a traditional settlement and/or land use. While interesting imprints of relict historical periods are readable in the landscape and continue to be used (such as the pattern of microplots and terracing), the integration and inter -relationships of these with the many other proposed attributes have not been demonstrated. Despite the efforts of the State Party to clarify the basis of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value, ICOMOS considers that this has not portrayed a coherent pattern of settlement/land use that could be considered to be outstanding. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is not demonstrated. 258 ICOMOS considers that criterion (v) has not been demonstrated.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 v 未得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: 267 This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the nominated property testifies to the cultural traditions of Roman and Eastern Roman military life, including garrison organisation, water management, religious practices associated with Christianity and the Mithraic cult, and the long-term occupation of a strategic ridge in Southeastern Anatolia. The State Party further presents the fortified complex as an exceptional testimony to a cultural tradition that brings together military and religious practices, stressing that the castrum included not only military and residential structures but also religious buildings, and that an underground rock -cut space provides evidence of Mithraic beliefs and rituals within this military setting. The ensemble of fortifications, internal buildings, hydraulic installations, necropolises and underground rock-cut spaces is presented as reflecting these traditions in a tangible form. ICOMOS considers that, on the basis of the information provided, the testimony of the nominated property to these cultural traditions has not been sufficientl y substantiated in terms of its distinctiveness and evidentiary basis when compared with other known sites of similar type. In particular, the nomination dossier does not clearly demonstrate the distinctiveness of this testimony, nor does it adequately establish its evidentiary basis in comparison with other known sites associated with Roman and Eastern Roman military and settlement systems. ICOMOS further notes that the attributes cited in support of this criterion, namely fortifications, internal buildings, hydraulic installations, necropolises and underground rock-cut spaces, are characteristic elements of Roman and Eastern Roman military and settlement sites more broadly. While their presence at the nominated property is acknowledged, the nomination dossier does not provide sufficient evidence to demonstrate that their configuration, state of preservation or documented use conveys a testimony that is unique or exceptional when assessed against other sites of the same cultural and functional categories considered in the comparative analysis. In particular, the evidentiary link between the physical remains and the specific cultural traditions invoked under this criterion is not demonstrated as being a unique or exceptional testimony in a comparative context. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is not demonstrated.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 iii 未得到证明。
criteria_assessments.analyst_note: 同事版汇总结论补充：ICOMOS considers that since the criteria for justifying the potential Outstanding Universal Value have not been demonstrated, respective supporting attributes cannot be confirmed.

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the fortified complex, with its defensive circuit, internal architectural layout, hydraulic system, necropolises and associated landscape features, constitutes an example of a Roman and Eastern Roman military camp, a castrum, and its supporting systems, illustrating a significant stage in the history of frontier defence and settlement in the region. In this regard, the State Party emphasises the ridgetop setting of the settlement, the organic layout shaped by topography, the 1.2-kilometre-long fortification wall with bastions and towers, the arrangement of barracks and circulation routes, the administrative complex near the main gate, the Grand Church and underground church, and the extensive hydraulic system including numerous cisterns and a main water channel, as together demonstrating the architectural planning and support systems of a Roman and Eastern Roman garrison. ICOMOS considers that the justification for this criterion depends on a clear demonstration of how the fortifications, internal structures, hydraulic installations and associated features collectively represent, in an outstanding and coherent manner, a specific architectural or technological type illustrating a significant stage in human history. On the basis of the information provided, including the current state of excavation and documentation, this has not been adequately demonstrated, as the nomination dossier does not sufficiently establish the degree to which the architectural layout, construction techniques and technological systems presented exceed, or are meaningfully distinguished from, the ones characteristic of comparable Roman and Eastern Roman military ensembles. In particular, the functional elements cited are not shown to form an integrated and representative ensemble that can be clearly identified as outstanding within the broader typological and regional context. ICOMOS further notes that a full understanding of the architectural and technological ensemble remains constrained by the partial exposure of the built fabric and the uneven level of documentation across different attributes of the nominated property. As a result, the relationship between the fortifications, internal structures, hydraulic installations and associated features is not sufficiently demonstrated to support their interpretation as a clearly defined and exemplary type of an Eastern Roman garrison. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is not demonstrated.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 iv 未得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the underground rock -cut space referred to as a Mithraeum is directly associated with the Mithraic cult and, together with other religious structures at the site, reflects beliefs and rituals of particular significance in the context of the Roman eastern frontier. ICOMOS notes that the identification of this underground space as a Mithraeum, and the strength of its association with the Mithraic cult, are central to the proposed justification under this criterion. In November 2025, the State Party provided further explanations of how Mithraism is evidenced and 268 documented at Zerzevan, describing the underground space as having features presented as typical of Mithraea (including a rectangular plan arranged for seating, niches on the eastern wall including a principal niche presented as being associated with the tauroctony scene, a central altar base, and water-related features presented as being linked to purification rites), and situating this interpretation within a regional comparative context along the Roman eastern frontier. ICOMOS notes, however, that the chronological interpretation advanced in relation to the closure and filling-in of this space remains methodologically uncertain, since the reported discovery of a coin within the fill provides, in stratigraphic terms, a terminus post quem for the deposit rather than conclusive evidence for the date of closure of the chamber itself. In its interim report, ICOMOS further noted that the current interpretation of the underground space relies primarily on functional attribution, while the documentation provided does not conclusively establish the presence of diagnostic elements or contextual evidence necessary to support a direct and tangible association with Mithraic beliefs and ritual practices. In the absence of such evidence, the proposed association remains interpretative rather than demonstrative. In this regard, ICOMOS considers that the architectural configuration of the chamber, as currently exposed, does not exhibit the spatial organisation that is characteristically associated with confirmed Mithraic sanctuaries, and that key typological indicators (including flanking benches, axially positioned cult niches, structured entrance sequences, or tauroctony relief panels) are not presently evidenced. ICOMOS also notes that the excavation information cited by the State Party during the nomination evaluation process indicates that the fill of the chamber was described as mixed and without stratified archaeological findings, which further limits the extent to which the sequence of use, closure and later reuse of the space can presently be established with confidence. ICOMOS further observes that certain carved features cited in the nomination dossier require reconsideration, including a “ray crown” motif above the entrance which, through stylistic analysis, could be identified as a Byzantine cross dating to the 6 th to 8th centuries CE, and that inscriptions previously described as “undecipherable” could be recognised as Syriac and Arabic, indicating a possible medieval activity rather than evidence of Late Antique Mithraic practice. ICOMOS further notes that the inscription may be composite and diachronic rather than a homogeneous text of a single period, reinforcing the need for further specialist epigraphic assessment before firm chronological conclusions are drawn from it. ICOMOS also notes that some features interpreted as ritual fixtures could be interpreted differently as constructional or lighting-related, and that the absence of stratified deposits or securely associated artefacts limits the capacity to establish a specific ritual attribution or chronology for primary use. In February 2026, the State Party reiterated the arguments presented in the nomination dossier supporting its interpretation of the underground rock -cut chamber as a Mithraeum, with an additional provisional decipherment of the debated inscriptions at the entrance of the chamber. Nonetheless, ICOMOS considers that the evidentiary basis for this identification as a Mithraeum, as presented in the nomination dossier and the additional information submitted by the State Party in October 2025 and February 2026, is not sufficiently demonstrated, particularly in relation to architectural form, associated features and material culture. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is not demonstrated. ICOMOS considers that since the criteria for justifying the potential Outstanding Universal Value have not been demonstrated, respective supporting attributes cannot be confirmed.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 vi 未得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the six Berlin housing estates already inscribed on the World Heritage List, together with the nominated extension, testify to a broad housing reform movement that made a decisive contribution to improving housing and living conditions in Berlin. Their quality of urban, architectural and garden design, as well as the housing standards developed during this period, served as guidelines for social housing constructed since then, both in and outside Germany. The nominated extension, being one of the most representative estates of the comprehensive Berlin housing reform, enhances the justification of this criterion through its innovative urban planning solution which integrates the estate into a heavily wooded suburban landscape, and through the combination of architecture, modern design, landscaping and the contemporary transport ation infrastructure. ICOMOS considers that the nominated extension contributes to expanding and amplifying the justification of this criterion.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 ii 已得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the six Berlin housing estates already inscribed on the World Heritage List, together with the nominated extension, exemplify new urban and architectural typologies, designed in the search for improved social living conditions. Fresh design solutions, as well as technical and aesthetic innovations, were incorporated by the leading modernist architects who contributed to their construction. T he nominated extension complements the World Heritage series by providing an outstanding example of a mixed urban typology of garden cities combined with large housing estates through the integration of the surrounding natural environment into the urban plan. The technical innovations such as the prefabricated building elements, standardised house types, aesthetic innovations of modern and extensive colourful design solutions, and the infrastructural concept of the underground station combining the transport infrastructure with a social and cultural centre, contribute to the development of the serial typological urban and architectural ensemble of the Berlin Modernism Housing Estates and its significance in the context of early 20 th-century modernist architecture. ICOMOS considers that the nominated extension contributes to expanding and amplifying the justification of this criterion. ICOMOS concurs with the identified attributes proposed by the State Party for the nominated extension, and considers that they complement those of the World Heritage series. ICOMOS considers that the nominated extension of Waldsiedlung Zehlendorf meets criteria (ii) and (iv), and contributes to expanding and amplifying the alreadyapproved justification of these criteria.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 iv 已得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the nominated serial property bears witness to the fusion between European artistic and technological influences in the late 19 th century and the cultural and territorial characteristics of the Amazonian landscape. This synthesis is embodied in the design, form and materials of the buildings and their adjoining public spaces. The theatres served as catalysts for a dynamic exchange between international performances and regional artistic traditions. ICOMOS considers that both component parts of the nominated serial property, built in the late 19 th century, have a chronological link with the rubber boom in the Amazon (1879-1912). ICOMOS notes that despite the additional information of October 2025, highlighting a similar geographical situation, the use of local materials in their construction and their integration into international musical theatre circuits, the notion of an exchange of influences has not been justified. In effect, the existence of one or more places where ideas, technologies and artistic practices intersected and led to innovative and unprecedented forms is not demonstrated here. Artists were commissioned by the authorities of Belém or Manaus to produce monumental works of art; artworks produced in Europe were commissioned by both cities; urban planning and landscaping projects were undertaken for the creation of the city of Manaus and the expansion of Belém, in the spirit of Haussmann’s Paris; and a European workforce was recruited and worked alongside Brazilian artists and artisans to create painted and sculpted works evocative of the vegetation and landscape of the Amazon. However, ICOMOS considers that these imports of art objects, concepts and know -how demonstrate an essentially one-way adoption and reflect a reference to a European lifestyle and culture, to the "Belle Époque", rather than a substantial exchange of influences. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is not demonstrated.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 ii 未得到证明。
criteria_assessments.analyst_note: 同事版汇总结论补充：ICOMOS considers thatthe serial approachis notjustified and that the selection of component parts should be reduced to include only the Teatro Amazonas(component part 002) and the Largo de São Sebastião in Manaus. ICOMOS considers that only the Teatro Amazonas (component part 002) and the Largo de São Sebastião in Manaus meet criterion (iv), and that criterion (ii) has not been demonstrated.

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the exceptional nature of the nominated theatres, as examples of architecture and urban planning, illustrates the profound social, economic and cultural transformations that followed the Industrial Revolution. This achievement can be seen in their architectural eclecticism, which combines industrial materials, such as iron structures, with local resources, such as stone and Amazonian woods. ICOMOS considers that the construction of a theatre and the development of a public space within a ci ty are common phenomena that are not exceptional in themselves, nor is the creation of cultural and leisure facilities in city centres, associated with their expansion. The construction of Italian-style theatres in the th century was a phenomenon that occurred throughout Europe and the Americas, particularly Latin America. The financing of these cultural facilities was then facilitated by the growing wealth of countries participating in the Industrial Revolution, the resulting national cultural policies and the rise of the bourgeoisie, who sought to display their prosperity through these prestigious projects. The nominated serial property participates in this general trend, without exhibiting any exceptional or prototypical characteristics. However, ICOMOS considers that the Teatro Amazonas (component part 002) and the Largo de São Sebastião represent the culmination of the construction of a modern capital in the heart of the Amazon rainforest. The city was built thanks to the rubber boom, a global phenom enon associated with the Industrial Revolution. The Teatro Amazonas was built by European architects, engineers and artists, with contributions from Brazilian artists, using technologies and materials imported from Europe combined with local resources. A true architectural showcase, it reflects the immense wealth generated by rubber exploitation at the end of the th century, as well as the ideals of progress, civilisation and European cosmopolitanism that the local elite wished to promote in the heart of the Amazon. ICOMOS considers that the situation is different for Belém, whose urban history covers a longer timeframe, from the 17th to the 20th century, and is not solely linked to the rubber boom. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is demonstrated by the Teatro Amazonas (component part 002) and the Largo de São Sebastião in Manaus alone.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 iv 已得到证明。

criteria_assessments.summary_en: This criterion is justified by the State Party on two grounds.
