First, the nominated property is presented as the birthplace of and a global reference point for the mythological traditions associated with the Twelve Gods of the ancient Greek religion, while the remains of the temples and sanctuaries at Dion on the lower slopes of the mountain are the most remarkable expression of the cult of the Olympian Gods and its longevity over a millennium. 315 Second, the State Party considers that the exceptional geomorphological and climatic elements have been mythologised throughout the ages to the present day, as reflected in modern toponyms such as the “Pantheon” (Mytikas peak), the “Throne of Zeus” (Stefani peak), and the “Plateau of the Muses”. The legacy of the Twel ve Gods and their dwelling on Mount Olympus have inspired literature, art, popular culture, and science throughout the world and across the centuries to the present day. Furthermore, the mountain is still used as a metaphor for divine order and human aspiration. According to the State Party, Mount Olympus stands as a symbolic topos (name and concept) of the myth, grandeur and excellence, with people bestowing its name on many places, massifs, towns and villages around the globe and even beyond to the planet Mars. ICOMOS considers that Mount Olympus is an outstanding example of a mountain landscape with particular geological characteristics that have inspired, and can clearly be linked to, an ancient religious or mythological expression, which in turn has maintained a centuries-long relevance to the present day and has had an impact far beyond its region of origin. The works of Homer (Iliad and Odyssey) and Hesiod (Theogony) in particular captured the idea of Mount Olympus as the abode of the Twelve Greek Gods, and the dissemination of their literature around the world has given Mount Olympus far -reaching relevance. The existence and importance of the link between the myth and the mountain (component part 1) is strengthened by the presence of the archaeological remains of the ancient city of Dion (component part 2). This archaeological site is closely associated with the cult of Zeus, the ruler of the Twelve Gods, and from where the forbidding mountain peaks can clearly be observed. Further traces of the worship of the gods on Mount Olympus can be found, for example, on St. Antonios peak, where evidence of an open-air sanctuary dedicated to the cult of Zeus has been discovered. However, ICOMOS considers that the documentation of the attributes that support the proposed Outstanding Universal Value is not sufficient at the present stage. The physical aspects of the nominated serial property, including the awe-inspiring mountain landscape and the archaeological remains of the service to the Olympian Gods, need more detailed documentation. The relevance and far -reaching impact of the myth also have to be documented in a more detailed and systematic manner, including, for example, more research on the history of the diffusion of the myth and the relevance it has today. ICOMOS considers that this criterion will be fully justified once the attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value are fully documented. ICOMOS considers that criterion (vi) will be fully justified once the attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value are fully documented.
criteria_assessments.summary_zh: ICOMOS认为标准 vi 已得到证明。

attributes.source_quote:
Based on the nomination dossier, and according to the
State Party, the key attributes of the proposed
Outstanding Universal Value are: the urban planning and
urban morphology; urban fabric; architectural forms and
public and ceremonial spaces of social interaction;
decorative elements of the urban fabric (carved doors,
decorated beams, zidaka-niches, coral and volcanic
stone masonry); as well as the associated intangible
heritage in the form of cultural practices, traditions, social
systems,
celebrations,
vernacular
architectural
techniques based on the use of local materials.
ICOMOS notes that the proposed justification is centred on
the period of sultanates in the Comoros, between the 14th-
15th and 19th centuries, as reflected by the principal urban
centres of two islands of the archipelago. The specific
historic period, however, cannot be considered in itself to
carry an Outstanding Universal Value. Moreover, the
Comorian medinas are more than a result of the
establishment of the sultanates. They started developing
much earlier, under the system of territorial chiefdoms. In
the 14th-15th century, they had not yet reached their full
urban
morphology.
The
period
of
their
greatest
development falls apparently in the 17th-18th century only;
the city walls erected at that time marking the extent the
medinas. The historic trajectory of the Comorian medinas
is therefore best understood on a longer-term historical
timescale, which allows an appreciation of how the historic
cities emerged from nuclei of clan-based communities to
evolve into fully urbanised and fortified centres, embodying
the synthesis of local continuity and external exchanges.
ICOMOS concurs with the reasons for which the State
Party considers the year 1886 and the start of the colonial
period as the end of the sultanate era rather than the formal
abolition of sultanates. ICOMOS considers that the
nominated serial property is more than a reflection of the
existence of the political power of the sultans in the
Comoros; it testifies to a much longer process of
urbanisation, which accompanied Islamisation, and was
initially shaped under the system of territorial chiefdoms.
While the early urban nuclei did not constitute urban
centres, they provided the social, political, and territorial
foundations upon which later city-states would develop.
Arab-Shirazi
migration
and
sultanate
formation
superimposed on this existing system, giving rise to the
distinctive Comorian socio-political and urban…
attributes.summary_zh: ICOMOS/申报文本在价值论证部分集中列明支撑OUV的主要属性；本条保留原文，后续可在页面中继续细分属性组。

attributes.source_quote: The integrity of the nominated serial property is based on the inclusion of a sufficient area of each historic town to understand their urban morphology, and key elements of the urban fabric, including public and private spaces whose meaning is linked to the traditional social organisation and which host cultural practices. Associations between the built environment and its natural setting are functionally important to support the protection of the nominated property. As a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole, and how each component part contributes to that value. Integrity is furthermore a measure of the intactness of the attributes. Only the best-preserved parts of each medina have been included within the nominated serial property, with the 22 remaining intra-muros areas and some immediate setting included within the buffer zones. ICOMOS considers that this approach is in principle adequate, as it takes into consideration the state of conservation of the historic cities, leaving out elements that have lost their integrity.
attributes.summary_zh: 完整性章节涉及支撑OUV的组成要素、属性完整性、系列组成关系及脆弱性。

attributes.source_quote:
Based on the nomination dossier, the key attributes of the
proposed Outstanding Universal Value include the layout
of the roças, with the residences of the administrators
overlooking the plantation to facilitate control, working and
transportation facilities, the barracks of the labourers,
workshops, storage and drying facilities, chapels, schools
and hospitals, bells, towers, all reflecting in their tangible
evidence and associated sources of information, such as
the reciprocal location of the different buildings and
functions, the different quality in construction techniques,
materials and spaces, the coercive and exploitative
nature of the roças.
attributes.summary_zh: ICOMOS/申报文本在价值论证部分集中列明支撑OUV的主要属性；本条保留原文，后续可在页面中继续细分属性组。

attributes.source_quote: According to the nomination dossier, the nominated series includes all the necessary attributes to convey the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. Most architectural elements within each component part have survived, and their historic layout, spatial logic, and reciprocal relationships remain legible because little development has obliterated them. Changes to the immediate and wider setting have been limited and contribute to the understanding of the historic and cultural significance of the nominated serial property. Functional structures and their machinery have been retained at some roças, allowing an appreciation of how the agro-industrial system functioned. In some cases, the technological equipment remains in use, illustrating the production cycle. Although the forced labour system has ceased, the nominated roças on Sao Tome remain inhabited and provide a sense of social cohesion.
attributes.summary_zh: 完整性章节涉及支撑OUV的组成要素、属性完整性、系列组成关系及脆弱性。

attributes.source_quote: through over-renovation and the erasure of historical surfaces. Following an assessment of threats, the State Party has established preventive measures to protect the property from degradation, in collaboration with local authorities, communities, and roça concession holders. However, their implementation depends on the availability of human and financial resources. ICOMOS considers that the state of conservation is uneven, with structures at Monte Café and Agua Izé (component parts 1 and 2) in critical condition, and that the main factor affecting the nominated property is an inappropriate development, driven by a lack of capacity and resources and by vulnerable socio-economic conditions. There is limited capacity to address the largescale conservation and maintenance issues and the structural factors that negatively affect the nominated property. Significant and long-term international assistance would be needed to accompany and support the State Party in meeting its objectives.
attributes.summary_zh: 真实性章节涉及属性的信息来源、物质与非物质真实性、使用功能、环境背景及相关风险。

attributes.source_quote:
Based on the nomination dossier, and according to the
State Party, the key attributes of the proposed
Outstanding Universal Value are: spatial organisation
and urban morphology of the village, urban dynamics
and volume of the buildings, landscape aspect, places
and the related intangible expressions of artistic and
spiritual nature, associated cultural and spiritual
intangible practices and traditions (feasts and religious
ceremonies, legends and popular stories linked to the
origins and development of the village), as well as
tangible artistic products.
In the additional information of October 2025, the State
Party clarified that it perceived the nominated property as
an associative cultural landscape, with the sloping
topography, Mediterranean vegetation, and panoramic
views of the sea serving as material supports for spiritual
and contemplative experiences, and light, colours, and
the serenity of the place inspiring artistic creation.
Moreover, the State Party explained that the significance
of the nominated property resides in the interaction
between the spectacular natural site, the spiritual
arrangement of the village, and the urban planning and
architecture being in symbiosis with the setting.
ICOMOS considers that the definition of a cultural
landscape, as stipulated in paragraph 47 of the
Operational Guidelines for the Implementation of the World
Heritage Convention, implies a clear and structuring
interaction between natural processes and human
interventions. The natural environment is not merely a
backdrop; it actively participates in the genesis and
evolution of a property. In the case of the nominated
village, its location on a hill overlooking the Mediterranean
Sea undeniably adds a landscape dimension to the urban
morphology. However, the adaptation of the settlement to
the topography is rather an outcome of architectural and
urban planning than a historical co-evolution between the
natural and human-made systems. Furthermore, the
nomination dossier tends to conflate the notion of
landscape character, that is the visual quality of a site,
with that of cultural landscape, which would imply a
dynamic interaction between humans and nature. Thus,
ICOMOS considers that the nominated property cannot
be considered a cultural landscape.
The State Party accepted ICOMOS’ position on this
matter in February 2026 and indicated that given the
importance of the landscape dimension for the nominated
property, a landscape approach will be used as…
attributes.summary_zh: ICOMOS/申报文本在价值论证部分集中列明支撑OUV的主要属性；本条保留原文，后续可在页面中继续细分属性组。

attributes.source_quote: of the nominated property, but most importantly to the residents of the village. The recent atmospheric events have clearly highlighted the vulnerability of the site and gaps in the disaster risk preparedness of the State Party to respond effectively to natural disasters, in order to avoid negative outcomes, or reduce their impact. Accordingly, ICOMOS considers that the implementation of the natural disaster risk management plan for the nominated property is an urgent necessity and should be prioritised by the State Party. It should be accompanied by an emergency plan that needs to be developed taking into consideration the specificities of the terrain and the risks that it poses to residents and visitors. ICOMOS considers that the nominated property has not suffered from major forms of neglect but is affected negatively by environmental factors (landslides) and tourism. Both require monitoring and concerted attention.
attributes.summary_zh: 完整性章节涉及支撑OUV的组成要素、属性完整性、系列组成关系及脆弱性。

attributes.source_quote:
Based on the nomination dossier, and according to the
State Party, the attributes of the proposed Outstanding
Universal Value are: the industrial spatial layout of
Jingdezhen;
technological
evidence
of
porcelain
production (kiln sites and workshops); intangible heritage
practices and tangible remains of the social organisation
of porcelain production; evidence of the extraction of raw
materials for porcelain production (kaolin, porcelain stone,
timber); wharves, roads, and bridges associated with the
river and road transportation of raw materials and
porcelain products; accumulations of kiln waste and
unexcavated artefacts and structures; buildings such as
ancestral halls, temples, and guilds; and sites and
excavated artefacts that provide evidence of the evolution
of kiln technologies and porcelain products over ten
centuries.

attributes.source_quote:
Based on the nomination dossier, and according to the
State Party, the key attributes of the proposed
Outstanding Universal Value are: archaeological and
architectural remains, the sacred landscape, the layout
and planning of the complex, inscriptions and ancient
texts that testify to the historic significance of the
71
nominated property, associations, as well as artistic
expressions including monumental art.
In its interim report ICOMOS raised concerns regarding
the
use
of
the
terms
“sacred
landscape”
and
“commemorative
landscape”
in
reference
to
the
nominated serial property. These terms describe rather
the larger area of the sacred geography (Deer Park), the
real extent of which has not been (or cannot be)
archaeologically confirmed at this stage, and which is not
fully reflected through the proposed attributes of the two
nominated component parts. ICOMOS notes that the
serial approach proposed by the State Party results from,
and the proposed boundaries of the component parts are
defined by, the known architectural and archaeological
remains uncovered in the area. The proposed buffer
zone, connecting the two component parts, emphasises
in this case their past relationship to each other and
suggests the broader sacred geography within which the
structures have been historically located. Therefore,
ICOMOS considers that it would be more appropriate to
refer to the nominated serial property simply as an
architectural and archaeological ensemble, to reflect
better the current reality of the two separate component
parts.
Nonetheless, ICOMOS understands the importance of the
sacred landscape dimension in relation to the symbolic
value of the sacred geography, which historically
exceeded the boundaries of the current two nominated
component parts. Accordingly, ICOMOS supports the use
of a landscape approach as a management tool for the
nominated serial property and its buffer zone. ICOMOS
considers that the nominated serial property should be
seen as an architectural and archaeological ensemble,
managed by the State Party as a sacred landscape, i.e.,
taking into consideration the relationship of the two
component parts to each other and to their wider setting,
as indicated by the buffer zone (and potentially beyond),
including the role played today by the monasteries of
different
Buddhist
communities
that
have
been
established in modern times in the vicinity of the site.
ICOMOS considers it important to maintain the spiritual
atmosphere of the buffer zone…

attributes.source_quote: The integrity of the nominated series is based on the inclusion of all the preserved architectural and archaeological vestiges that reflect the spiritual significance of Sarnath. As a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole, and how each component part contributes to that value. Integrity is furthermore a measure of the intactness of the attributes. The two component parts contain all the known preserved structures erected between the 6th century BCE and the 12th century CE, at what used to be a sacred site of Sarnath. The proposed buffer zone indicates the relationship between the component parts which existed in the past, all the monuments having belonged to one monastic complex. Archaeological and textual evidence suggests that the site underwent historically phases of appropriation and transformation, as well as internal decay, that gradually eroded the integrity of the monastic complex.

attributes.source_quote:
Based on the nomination dossier and the additional
information, and according to the State Party, the key
attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value
are: the fortifications, both individually as military
architectural typology and collectively as a strategic
defence system; the integrated landscape formed by the
fortifications within the Alborz Mountains, including
through the ingenious food supply system and water
management underground network; and the cultural and
intellectual legacy of Alamūt Castle.
attributes.summary_zh: ICOMOS/申报文本在价值论证部分集中列明支撑OUV的主要属性；本条保留原文，后续可在页面中继续细分属性组。

attributes.source_quote: According to the State Party, the integrity of the nominated serial property is based on the inclusion of the fortifications as a strategic defensive system ingeniously integrated within the landscape and territory, which contributed to make Alamūt Castle and Related Fortifications a political, religious and cultural centre for the Nezāri Ismaʿili. As a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole, and how each component part contributes to that value. Integrity is furthermore a measure of the intactness of the attributes. Recent research on the Ismaʿili fortifications has identified thirty-three large and small castles, forts, and watchtowers in the Alamūt region. However, as further explained by the State Party in the October 2025 additional information, besides being the only ones in the valley with considerable in situ architectural remains, the seven component parts have been selected because, altogether, they illustrate the strategic functions, intervisibility and interconnectivity of the fortifications within the entire defensive system, with each fortification fulfilling a specific role contributing to the effective operation of the overall system. ICOMOS considers that the rationale for the selection of the component parts is justified by the comparative analysis, and that they collectively represent the defensive system as well as the cultural, religious…

attributes.source_quote: The nominated property is considered by the State Party to be of Outstanding Universal Value as a cultural property for the following reasons: • Asuka and Fujiwara were capitals of the ancient state on the Japanese archipelago from the end of the 6th to the early 8th century that were modelled on the Chinese ritsuryo system and exerted a fundamental influence on the construction and evolution of subsequent capitals in Japan, including Nara (8th century) and Kyoto (9th century). • The nominated serial property provides archaeological evidence of the formative processes of the emergence and establishment of centralised state systems in East Asia, demonstrating important regional interchanges. • The sites of palaces/government offices, temples and tombs associated with the two ancient capitals demonstrate the spatial expressions of political power, ceremonial and administrative functions and new types of mounded tombs. • The Buddhist temple sites represent the introduction of Buddhism to the Japanese archipelago and the coupling of Buddhism with the centralised state. The attributes of the nominated property are archaeological sites (remains of structures and associated artefacts), and aboveground landforms associated with the two ancient capitals.

attributes.source_quote:
Based on the nomination dossier and the additional
information, and according to the State Party, the key
attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value
are the complex multi-chamber plans (rock-cut and
catacomb types), the organic integration into the natural
landscape, the continuity of religious practices, and the
formation of a “sacred geography” of necropolises around
them.
Regarding terminology, ICOMOS noted in its interim
report that the term “mosque” was somewhat misleading
in the context of this nomination, as the component parts
are multifunctional complexes that include prayer halls,
mihrabs,
self-reflection
rooms
(çilehanes),
tombs,
cemeteries, courtyards and water wells or cisterns, and
display a variety of architectural organisation, spaces and
elements. Rather than the term “mosques”, ICOMOS
considered it would be preferable to call the nominated
property by another, more descriptive term that reflects
underground structures and the presence of historic
necropolises.
The State Party, in the additional information provided in
February 2026, gave further justification for the choice of
terminology used in the name of the nominated property,
underlying the fact that the component parts are locally
known as underground mosques. The State Party also
proposed a new name that better reflects character,
composition and location of the nominated property:
“Rock Mosques and Associated Sacred Sites of
Mangystau”, instead of “Rocky Mosques of Mangystau
Peninsula” as initially indicated. ICOMOS considers this
change of name appropriate.

attributes.source_quote: Integrity is a measure of wholeness, intactness and absence of threats to the nominated property and its attributes. As a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated series as a whole, and how each component part contributes to that value. The wholeness and intactness of the nominated property is based on the substances, layouts and spatial relationships of all the rock-cut sacred spaces, nearby necropolises and various associated structures and areas that manifest the evolution of sacred practices in which Islamic Sufi spirituality organically continued and transformed more ancient practices. It is also based on the views to and from the nominated component parts, and on the associated continuing syncretic rituals and traditions that are expressed in the tangible elements. The nominated component parts collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated series as a whole. The complexes together include prayer halls, mihrabs, rooms for contemplation and self-reflection, multifunctional spaces and tombs of saints as well as pilgrimage pathways, courtyards, wells, sacred trees and necropolises.

attributes.source_quote: The nominated property is considered by the States Parties to be of Outstanding Universal Value as a cultural property for the following reasons: • The Fergana-Syrdarya Corridor was a crossroads of Silk Roads routes that includes caravan routes from different directions (east and west, south and north), including both land and water routes. • These sections of the Silk Roads facilitated exchanges between the cultures of Eurasian states, and demonstrate extensive interactions, particularly between the nomadic steppe region in the north and north-east and the agricultural-oasis-pastoral region to the south and south-west. • Cultural traditions are demonstrated through the evidence of systems of agricultural water canals used for irrigation, the construction of fortifications, and the coexistence of different religions over time. • The nominated component parts include outstanding examples of architectural ensemble types and decoration such as wall paintings associated with significant phases of history in Central Asia. • The Fergana-Syrdarya Corridor demonstrates interactions with the natural environment, demonstrated by the network of water management elements and cultivated fields.

attributes.source_quote: Integrity The integrity of the nominated serial property is based on the state of conservation and the way major pressures are managed, as well as the intactness of the material evidence of the selected component parts. For serial nominations, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts contain all the attributes necessary to express the proposed Outstanding Universal Value, and how each component part contributes to that value. As discussed above, ICOMOS has identified some instances where urgent conservation interventions are needed. Most archaeological materials (excavated and buried) are in a stable condition. Where not reburied or sheltered, archaeological remains are vulnerable, and in some cases require urgent intervention. These are recognised by the States Parties. Although there are some conservation issues that need to be addressed, most of the religious monuments meet the requirements of integrity. There are issues for the settings of some component parts due to urbanisation, tourism development and intrusive infrastructure. Based on the nomination dossier and the additional
information, and according to the State Party, the key
attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value
are: the excavated and unexcavated tombs of Xiongnu

attributes.source_quote: Integrity is a measure of wholeness, intactness, and absence of threats to the nominated serial property and its attributes. As a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole, and how each component parts contribute to that value. ICOMOS considers that each of the eight component parts contributes to the nominated series in a substantial way. Together, they represent the regional characteristics of Xiongnu tombs across the administrative and territorial divisions of the Xiongnu empire. They also cover the chronological span of the Xiongnu empire, and include examples of different expressions of the Xiongnu funerary culture, thus constituting a representational sample of Xiongnu cemetery complexes. The integrity of the individual tombs is sufficient, though it has been affected by acts of looting that caused some destruction.

attributes.source_quote: The integrity of the nominated property is based on the wholeness of the Wat Phra Mahathat monastery complex. This includes all the religious buildings and the sacred sand ground on which they stand, their layout and spatial relationships, the views to and from the complex and the intangible rituals and traditions associated with the complex. The intangible aspects find expression in the associated tangible elements. Integrity is also a measure of the intactness of the attributes, and the way major pressures on these are managed. While the conditions of integrity of the nominated property can be considered demonstrated since all the necessary attributes are still present within the nominated property, it faces several current or future pressures, most notably associated with development pressures, natural disasters and human activities. ICOMOS considers that these pressures are currently being managed adequately.

attributes.source_quote:
Based on the nomination dossier and the additional
information, and according to the State Party, the key
attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value
are: Experimental and innovative approach; Modern
interpretation
of
traditional
elements;
Architecture
responding to socialist urban design; Soviet standard
typologies and custom designs; and Soviet monumental
art.
According to the State Party, the five key attributes of the
nominated serial property are described as follows:
The “Experimental and innovative approach” attribute
highlights the role of Tashkent as a laboratory for
technical and social experimentation, with innovations in
anti-seismic architecture, and in the use of pioneering
materials and engineering designed to meet the
challenges of a specific seismic and climatic frontier.
The “Modern interpretation of traditional elements”
attribute
emphasises
the
synthesis
of
modernist
geometric forms with regional Central Asian and Islamic
motifs, ensuring that mass-produced, industrialised
architecture remained culturally and climatically relevant
by respecting local heritage and vernacular spatial logic.
The “Architecture responding to socialist urban design”
attribute is reflected by the nominated component parts
being integral to a socialist planning framework
characterised
by
functional
zoning,
ensemble
composition and an emphasis on public space, creating
an egalitarian and efficient city.
The “Soviet standard typologies and custom designs”
attribute is exemplified by deviations from USSR-wide
standardised typologies for cultural and administrative
functions to address the local needs of Tashkent, in
particular
regional
seismic,
climatic,
and
cultural
conditions.
The “Soviet monumental art” attribute highlights that
monumental artworks are directly integrated into the
architectural fabric to serve both aesthetic and ideological
purposes, merging Soviet themes with local artistic
traditions to reinforce narratives of progress, solidarity
and cultural identity.
ICOMOS notes that the proposed justification for
inscription includes statements that refer to the whole
modernist city of Tashkent and are not applicable to the
nominated serial property, which is only constituted of a
selected ensemble of buildings in the city.

attributes.source_quote: The State Party argues that the nominated serial property meets the conditions of integrity, as the component parts together illustrate technical and formal innovations, a modernist reinterpretation of traditional regional elements, the principles of socialist urban planning, and a functional response to new urban needs. The largely intact urban context preserves spatial and visual relationships among the component parts. The nomination dossier also briefly describes the conditions of integrity for each component part. As a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole, and how 173 each component part contributes to that value. Integrity is furthermore a measure of the intactness of the attributes. Following exchanges with the State Party, ICOMOS has concluded that the nine nominated component parts in Tashkent suffice to convey the proposed Outstanding Universal Value, but the addition, at a later stage, of the Tashkent TV Tower, Hotel Uzbekistan and the Palace of Aviation Constructors, via a significant boundary modification request, would strengthen integrity.

attributes.source_quote:
Based on the nomination dossier and the additional
information, and according to the State Party, the key
attributes (“primary attributes” in the State Party’s
terminology) of the proposed Outstanding Universal
Value are as follows:
1.
Elements that contribute to the modernisation of
society,
2.
The use of urban planning, architecture and
design in creating an identity for the new nation,
3.
A mutually beneficial interaction with the site
context and surrounding natural or urban
environment,
4.
A synthesis of science, art, design and
architecture,
5.
Designed interiors as an integral part of the
Aalto Works,
6.
Collaborative design work,
7.
An innovative approach and international
impact on modern design.
ICOMOS notes that the nomination is built around seven
identified key attributes characterising the Aalto Works
related to the proposed justification for criterion (ii), each
of which is present in one or two of the nominated
component parts. Then, for each nominated component
part, the State Party has identified an additional group of
secondary attributes – technical attributes in the terms of
legislative protection in Finland.
ICOMOS considers that the proposed key attributes do
not convey the distinctive qualities of the nominated
property in a clear and comprehensible way. In addition,
any given attribute of the proposed Outstanding Universal
Value must be reflected not only in one or two component
parts, but in each of the nominated component parts. This
issue was addressed in the ICOMOS interim report. In the
additional information of February 2026, the State Party
provided more detailed description of the proposed
attributes, underlying the specific connections between
the thirteen component parts and their contribution to
each of the seven key attributes, as well as the way each
of the attributes is present in each of the component parts.
The State Party also highlighted the distinctiveness of
each of the component parts and their qualities.
ICOMOS considers that the proposed key attributes are
too general, noting conceptual overlap and cross-cutting
relationships. Consequently, ICOMOS recommends
refining them and suggests combining the attributes 1 and
2 (“contribution to the modernisation of society” and “the
use of urban planning, architecture and design in creating
an identity for the new nation”). Furthermore, ICOMOS
considers that the reference to the creation of an identity
for the new nation cannot be considered as an argument
that…

attributes.source_quote: Integrity is a measure of the wholeness and intactness of the attributes that convey the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. For a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole and how each component part contributes to that value. The integrity of the Aalto Works is based on the influential structures, features and spaces created by the Aalto studio that best illustrate the integration of a new international architectural language with traditional local ideas during the modernisation of society in the 20th century by using sensitivity and a human-centred approach to architecture and design. Overall, the component parts are intact and retain their relationship with their associated landscapes and settings. Most retain interiors with significant fittings and furniture. Many sustain their original use, or sympathetic functions. The Paimio Sanatorium (component part 002) is the most vulnerable of the nominated component parts in terms of sustaining an appropriate use and needing significant conservation interventions, though there are mechanisms in place to mitigate potential losses. ICOMOS considers that the integrity of the nominated series as a whole as well as the integrity of each of the component parts have been demonstrated.

attributes.source_quote: The nominated property was initially considered by the State Party to be of Outstanding Universal Value as a cultural property for the following reasons, before changes made during the evaluation process: • It is the site where, on 6 June 1944, Operation Neptune, the first phase of Operation Overlord, was launched on the Normandy coast to open a new front 201 in Western Europe against the Third Reich's troops; an operation that ultimately led to the liberation of Western Europe and the end of the Second World War in Europe. • The nominated property constituted both a relict cultural landscape, encompassing the physical testimony left by the D-Day Landings, and an associative cultural landscape, due to the exceptional associated symbolic values. • The nominated property encompassed the entire shoreline where the landing operations took place, stretching from Ravenoville in the west to Ouistreham in the east, a vast maritime area with submerged vestiges, and eight terrestrial areas: Utah Beach, Pointe du Hoc, Omaha Beach, the Longues-sur-Mer battery, the Mulberry B artificial harbour, Gold Beach, Juno Beach, and Sword Beach, preserving physical remains of the defensive system and of the offensive event. • The nominated property became a site of global gathering associated with an event widely perceived as having enabled the return of freedom and heralded a lasting peace on the European continent, strengthened by the reconciliation between former belligerents. It serves as a place of universal hope that transcends European borders.

attributes.source_quote: and boundary delineation, protection of the buffer zone, interpretation, as well as participation and consensus. Additional information was received from the State Party on 26 February 2026. All additional information received has been incorporated into the relevant sections of this evaluation report. Date of ICOMOS approval of this report 11 March 2026 198 2 Description of the nominated property Note: The nomination dossier and additional information contain detailed descriptions of this property, its history and its state of conservation. Due to limitations on the length of evaluation reports, this report provides only a short summary of the most relevant aspects. Description and history As originally nominated, the property comprised a terrestrial and maritime ensemble directly associated with the Allied amphibious and airborne operations initiated on 6 June 1944 (D-Day).

attributes.source_quote: Integrity According to the nomination dossier, the nominated property retains integrity through its combined terrestrial and maritime landscape, which preserves key material and associative attributes of the Normandy Landings of 6 June 1944. These include representative elements of the Atlantic Wall in combat zones, remains of the logistical infrastructure of Operation Neptune, preserved wartime vestiges, memorial sites, and commemorative places established from 1944 onwards. The overall landscape integrity is considered satisfactory and continues to convey the operation's scale and unity. However, the condition and legibility of individual attributes vary due to post-war clearance activities, the recovery of materials, and natural coastal processes such as erosion and marine action, although sufficient elements remain to illustrate their historical significance. ICOMOS expressed concerns in its interim report regarding the applicability of the cultural landscape category and the inclusion of the maritime area within the nominated property. In the additional information of February 2026, the State Party decided to drop the cultural landscape category and to reduce the area of the nominated property by excluding the maritime area and treating it as part of the buffer zone. The nominated property was also reconfigured into a series of six component parts, including the five beaches where the D-Day Landings occurred and the Pointe du Hoc. Other attributes, such as Mulberry B artificial harbour, remains within the

attributes.source_quote:
Based on the nomination dossier, and according to the
State Party, the key attributes of the proposed
Outstanding Universal Value can be grouped as follows:
the Castle of Carcassonne and its ramparts as a central
stronghold of military and political power; the newly
created defensive system spread on the territory of the
seneschalty of Carcassonne; a coherent construction
programme following the principles of the 13th-century
Capetian military architecture; watchmen fortresses built
on limestone ridges; strategic position for defence
purposes; ostentatious monumentality; architectural
rationality and military efficiency; vistas and panoramas;
and the surrounding landscape unoccupied by civilian
residences.
ICOMOS considers that the State Party developed the
justification for the proposed Outstanding Universal Value
around two strands. The first one is related to the
consolidation of power by the kings of France in the
Languedoc region, and the creation of the seneschalty of
Carcassonne, of which the nominated serial property is
considered a tangible manifestation. The second strand is
centred around the qualities of the Capetian military
architecture and their adaptation to the distinctive
topographical setting. The Castle of Carcassonne and its
ramparts (component part 01) is slightly different in this
sense, not being a hill/mountain fort but forming an
important part of the defence system, as it served as its
administrative centre.
In the additional information of October 2025, the State
Party explained that from the twenty-two fortresses under
direct administration of the seneschal of Carcassonne, it
selected those that served military and administrative
functions, rather than played a symbolic role. The historical
analysis of the constitution of the fortified system allows to
identify three phases in the history of consolidation of the
territory and link them to three groups of fortresses: (1)
fortresses to the east, in the territory long-time pacified,
which were annexed in the first phase; (2) fortresses in the
south and those that directly protected Carcassonne, linked
to the resistance of the populations against the Church and
the king of France, as well as the border conflicts with the
Kingdom of Aragon; and (3) fortresses later added to the
system. The watchmen fortresses selected for the
nominated serial property belong to the second group.
Stylistic characteristics, architectural similarities, and
integration with and visual dominance over…
attributes.summary_zh: ICOMOS/申报文本在价值论证部分集中列明支撑OUV的主要属性；本条保留原文，后续可在页面中继续细分属性组。

attributes.source_quote: The integrity of the nominated serial property is based on the inclusion of sufficient number of structural elements of the construction programme implemented by the Capetian Crown to control the conquered region of Languedoc in the aftermath of the Albigensian Crusade, and the related landscape aspects that played a role in the spread and consolidation of the royal control over this territory to fully convey the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. As a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole, and how each component part contributes to that value. Integrity is furthermore a measure of the intactness of the attributes. The nominated component parts represent partially the fortification system created for the seneschalty of Carcassonne, which included fortresses of different types, built at different stages of the military campaigns. Moreover, the conquest of Languedoc involved, besides construction of fortresses on the conquered territory, the establishment of “new towns”, which were strategically placed and helped resettle the population to ensure loyalty to the French Crown. In that sense, ICOMOS considers that the nominated series cannot be said to include enough necessary attributes to express the proposed Outstanding Universal Value fully.

attributes.source_quote:
Based on the nomination dossier and the additional
information, and according to the State Party, the key
attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value
are: a specific financing method used for the construction
of these theatres, based on the patronage of the società
condominiali, a partnership between public bodies and
individuals from the emerging bourgeoisie; the presence
of an auditorium (U-shaped, bell-shaped, horseshoeshaped, truncated oval shape or elliptical) with boxes,
spaces reserved for the public (including reception areas
such as ridotti and entrance halls) and stage machinery;
a high density and interrelatedness of large-capacity
Italian-style theatres, forming a regional cultural system
dedicated
to
live
performance;
the
harmonious
incorporation of the theatres into their historical urban
setting through rigorous planning; exchanges between
architects, artists and theatre craftspeople; and the
continuity of use of these theatres in accordance with their
original purpose as venues for performances, meetings
and gatherings. ICOMOS considers that some of these
attributes are not sufficiently specific or relevant to
determining the significance of condominio theatres.

attributes.source_quote: Integrity is a measure of the completeness of the nominated property, its intactness and the absence of threats to its attributes. As a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole, and how each component part contributes to that value. Not all the theatres of the nominated serial property have retained all of their original layout, decor and original furniture. The fully or partially preserved stage equipment and machinery are often no longer in service. Some theatres have also suffered significant damage due to earthquakes, the Second World War, fires or other incidents. The damaged or destroyed parts of these theatres have been restored or rebuilt, while in some cases they are awaiting restoration work. The entrances to the theatres have generally been modified and adapted to make them accessible for all audiences. attributes.source_quote:
Based on the nomination dossier, and according to the
State Party, the key attributes of the proposed
Outstanding Universal Value can be grouped as follows:
unity and conceptual principles underlying a coherent 17th
century system of bulwarked border towns along the Raia;
innovation and complexity shown in the adaptive designs
of the fortresses, advanced artillery integration and largescale construction under limited resources; and the
precocious rehearsal of Vauban’s “pre-carré”, seen in its
dense double-line frontier fortification and adoption of
advances in European defensive concepts.
The nominated property is presented as a series
comprising three component parts, each of which, the
State Party argues, embodies the proposed Outstanding
Universal Value and its associated attributes. Regarding
the serial approach and the selection of these three
fortresses from the 143 forming the Raia, the State Party
clarified in November 2025 that the choice was guided by
a unifying concept, aiming to incorporate the most
representative Portuguese bulwarked fortresses along
the border. In February 2026, it further specified that this
nomination does not advocate for an open-ended strategy
for the inclusion of additional bulwarked fortresses, and that
these fortresses, together with Évora and Elvas, constitute
an exemplary representation of the cultural heritage under
consideration. The State Party also highlighted that the
three selected fortresses are part of the only sixteen fortified
ensembles located on the Portuguese side of the Raia that
are considered to be in a very good or good overall
condition. The three component parts are all located within
contiguous major hydrographic basins. They also represent
three different morphological settings: plateau/plain for
Almeida, mountain for Marvão and riverside for Valença.
However, despite the detailed historical information
provided, ICOMOS considers that the nomination dossier
does not clearly demonstrate how the fortresses, either as
a general system or in relation to the three selected
component parts, function together within the Raia. As a
result, the overall structure and functioning of the
defensive system remain insufficiently articulated. This
raises concerns as to whether the three component parts,
taken together, can adequately represent the proposed
Outstanding Universal Value. It is not demonstrated how
this initial series justifies the proposed Outstanding
Universal Value, nor how potential future…

attributes.source_quote: The nominated property is considered by the State Party to be of Outstanding Universal Value as a cultural property for the following reasons: • Ribeira Sacra is an outstanding cultural waterscape that demonstrates the relationship between humans and water. • The spectacular river canyons and valleys have been sculpted by water, and the harvesting of water over centuries of continuous occupation has ensured the survival and wellbeing of communities. • The eremitic tradition that emerged in the 5th century, and the establishment of monastic communities and traditional settlements have shaped the landscape of small plots and fragmented land holdings, interspersed with churches, monasteries and abbeys that overlook river courses. • The exceptional water heritage includes archaeological sites, systems for draining the socalcos (terraces on steep slopes with crops and 256 vineyards), waterworks of all historical periods (including the 20th century industrialisation of hydropower), fountains, water mills, canals, weirs, crossings, bridges, river routes and waterfalls. • A high density of vineyard and agricultural terraces have adapted to the natural constraints of the slopes.

attributes.source_quote:
Based on the nomination dossier, and according to the
State Party, the key attributes of the potential Outstanding
Universal Value are: the remains of the fortification
system, including ramparts, towers and gates; the
domestic, religious and administrative structures within
the fortified enclosure; the hydraulic installations
consisting of rock-cut cisterns and water channels; the
necropolises with rock-cut tombs; the quarries associated
with construction activities; the underground rock-cut
spaces, including the structure identified as a Mithraeum;
and features associated with historic circulation routes.
ICOMOS notes that the name of the nominated property,
“Zerzevan
Castle
and
Mithraeum”,
reflects
the
interpretative framework adopted by the State Party. This
terminology was discussed during the evaluation process,
with ICOMOS expressing its concern that the functional
and typological identification of certain elements,
particularly the underground rock-cut space interpreted as
a Mithraeum, remains subject to differing interpretations
based on the information currently available. ICOMOS
considers that the way in which attributes are interpreted
has a direct impact on the potential Outstanding Universal
Value.

attributes.source_quote:
Based on the nomination dossier, and
according to the State Party, while the nominated
extension shares the common attributes of the World
Heritage property, it also contributes to the whole series
with the following attributes: the hybrid urban typology that
combines the garden city elements and apartment
buildings through the integration of the buildings into a
wooded landscape; the fast connection to the city centre via
the modernist underground station; the shopping arcades
within the station conceived as a commercial, supply and
community centre; the exclusive use of flat roofs; the
extensive use of colour throughout the buildings and
building elements; and the sculptural design of the façades
as reflected in the windows and jamb storeys.

attributes.source_quote: The nominated extension was originally shortlisted as a potential component part in the 2008 nomination dossier, but ultimately it was not included in the nominated selection due to the process of privatisation of the cooperative buildings and state of conservation issues at the time. Since then, most of the apartment buildings have been acquired by a company with an interest in preserving the historical character of the estate. In addition, the issues relating to the state of conservation have been remedied as a result of the ongoing and comprehensive restoration efforts that have been undertaken. The State Party considers that Waldsiedlung Zehlendorf is now ready to be included in the Berlin Modernism Housing Estates, as a component part to complete the series. The nominated extension is of adequate size, encompassing the complete historical urban area of Waldsiedlung Zehlendorf, including all residential and functional buildings, green spaces, gardens, public areas and streets. All the factors affecting the conditions of the nominated extension are under control. Waldsiedlung Zehlendorf significantly enhances the integrity of the World Heritage serial property through attributes such as the integration of green spaces and a forest with housing, a bespoke modernist transportation infrastructure combined with a community and commercial centre, the exclusive use of flat roofing throughout the estate, and a specific façade colour scheme. ICOMOS considers that the integrity of the nominated extension has been demonstrated.

attributes.source_quote:
Based on the nomination dossier and the additional
information, and according to the State Party, the key
attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value
can be grouped as follows: the form, design and materials
of the architecture, which combine European eclecticism,
late 19th century Industrial Revolution technologies and
materials imported from Europe with Amazonian artistic
expression, techniques and materials; predominance in
the urban landscape and adaptation to the environment,
where the theatres and associated public spaces are
symbolic of the socio-political and cultural transformations
of the “golden age of rubber” and their role in the
structuring and urban development of the cities; and the
multiple uses and functions of the property, which range
from the performing arts to the preservation of intangible
cultural heritage, such as the Boi-Bumbá of the State of
Amazonas and the Carimbó of the State of Pará, thus
acting as a catalyst for artistic education and economic
development.
In its interim report, ICOMOS requested additional
information on the historical narrative presented by the
State Party and the values it embodies.
In the additional information of February 2026, the State
Party clarified that although the Rubber Cycle was
relatively
short,
it
generated
unprecedented
concentrations of wealth, creating the conditions for
ambitious projects that transformed the urban landscape,
cultural life and identity of the region.
Belém, founded in 1616, was already the administrative
and trading centre of the Amazon. With the export of
rubber, it strengthened its position within the international
economic networks. The Theatro da Paz, in Neo-classical
style, symbolises this cosmopolitan openness. Manaus
developed as a centre for the collection and export of
latex, and as the capital of the new province of Amazonas
from 1850. The eclectic-style Teatro Amazonas embodies
the aim of making Manaus a cultural capital in the heart
of the rainforest. Belém and Manaus are both rivals and
interdependent. According to the State Party, the two
theatres function as complementary nodes on the same
artistic circuit and embody the architectural translation of
an ephemeral extractive economy, a modernisation in the
heart of the rainforest and a selective appropriation of
European models.

attributes.source_quote: Integrity According to the State Party, the integrity of the nominated serial property is based on the combination of European eclecticism, industrial metal structures and Amazonian materials. It is also based on the capacity of the theatres to symbolise the "rubber boom" and to shape the urban landscape. Finally, the integrity of the nominated serial property also lies in the continuity of artistic functions combined with the participation of traditional cultural expressions and the creation of economic opportunities for the local population. As a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole, and how each component part contributes to that value. Integrity is furthermore a measure of the intactness of the attributes. ICOMOS considers that the justification proposed by the State Party for the nominated serial property is not demonstrated.

attributes.source_quote:
Based on the nomination dossier and the additional
information, and according to the State Party, the key
attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value
are the geological features and geomorphological
phenomena (“geosites”) to which mythological narratives
are repeatedly anchored.
Further attributes are the traces left by people serving and
worshipping these gods on and around the mountain.
These traces include, for example, the Archaeological site
of Dion (component part 2) and the St. Antonios peak
(part of component part 1), both of which also form part of
this narrative. More physical evidence of the worship of
the gods will probably be identified through future
systematic archaeological surveys in the higher mountain
regions.
An associated intangible attribute is the impact the myth
of Mount Olympus still has today as the abode of the
gods, and Mount Olympus as a topos expressed in works
of art, literature and the use of the name Olympus.
Originally, the State Party had not defined specific
tangible aspects of the mountain as cultural attributes of
the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. In response
to a request for clarification by ICOMOS, the State Party
submitted additional information in November 2025 and
reiterated that the intangible cultural values are linked to
all the natural attributes of the mountain itself rather than
to any particular spot or viewshed of the mountain. In
other words, the mountain is valued as a dominant single
entity within which a multiplicity of peaks, valleys and
other natural features are associated with specific aspects
of the life of the ancient Greek gods.
In the additional information of February 2026, the State
Party defined twenty geosites, several of which are linked
explicitly to a cultural narrative focused on the connection
between the mountain and the myth. They serve as
examples that help to clarify the connection between the
myth and the mountain. However, according to the State
Party, the value rests not in each individual location, but
in the combination of these sites and vistas that together
form the scene or stage for the performance of the
Olympian Gods. Other geosites are viewpoints that allow
one to observe the awe-inspiring peaks of the mountain.
As part of the additional information of November 2025,
the State Party proposed to strengthen the identification
314
of tangible attributes associated with criterion (vi) by
including the archaeological remains of temples and
sanctuaries at…

attributes.source_quote: The integrity of a nominated property is a measure of the completeness or intactness of the attributes that convey its proposed Outstanding Universal Value. For a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole and how each component part contributes to that value. The integrity of the nominated serial property is linked to the mountain (component part 1), particularly its aweinspiring, cloud-shrouded steepness and inaccessibility, as well as to the archaeological city of Dion in the foothills of Mount Olympus (component part 2), and the myth of the mountain as the abode of the Twelve Greek Gods that has become a cultural reference point all over the world. The physical spaces referred to in the Greek mythology as the abode of the Twelve Olympian Gods are included in the nominated property but are not described in detail by the State Party. The two component parts individually and collectively are believed to contain all the attributes necessary to convey the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. However, no detailed plans of the Archaeological site of Dion, the second component part of the nominated property, have been provided, nor has the archaeological landscape surrounding the site been described in adequate detail.

payload.brief_en:
Located along the Raia, the historic boundary between Portugal and Spain, the Bulwarked Fortresses of the Raia form a defence system created during the Portuguese Restoration War (1640-1668), comprising 143 recorded fortifications spread over roughly 1,300 kilometres. The nominated property is a series constituted of three fortifications: the Stronghold of Almeida and the Fortresses of Marvão and Valença. Each includes its fortified perimeter, military buildings and the urban area enclosed by its defensive lines.

The Stronghold of Almeida is a star-shaped bulwarked fort on a strategic hill above the River Côa, distinguished by its six bulwarks, advanced outer works and the fortified town enclosed within. The Fortress of Marvão combines a commanding mountaintop setting with medieval and modern bulwarked structures, illustrating the integration of natural topography and evolving defensive architecture. The Fortress of Valença is a bulwarked stronghold, with two interconnected enclosures, overlooking the River Minho, notable for its scale and integration of modern military architecture with the topography of the site.
payload.brief_zh:
该项目位于 Raia，即葡萄牙与西班牙之间的历史边界沿线。Raia 堡垒体系形成于葡萄牙复国战争时期（1640-1668），由约 143 处有记录的防御工事组成，分布范围约 1,300 公里。申报遗产为由三处防御工事组成的系列遗产：Almeida 要塞、Marvão 要塞和 Valença 要塞；每个组成部分包括其防御边界、军事建筑以及防线围合的城区。

Almeida 要塞是位于 Côa 河上方战略丘陵上的星形棱堡，具有六个棱堡、外部防御工程和被防御体系围合的城镇。Marvão 要塞结合了山顶控制性地形、中世纪和近代棱堡结构，体现自然地形与防御建筑演变的结合。Valença 要塞由两个相互连接的防御围合区构成，俯瞰 Minho 河，以其规模以及现代军事建筑与地形的整合为特征。

a.source_en: a) Reconceptualise the nomination to clearly define a conceptual framework and parameters that identify the essential features of the Raia system and how it is expressed and linked by relevant selected fortresses,
a.summary: 重新构思申报文本，明确 Raia 防御体系的概念框架和判断参数，说明其核心特征，以及这些特征如何通过所选堡垒体现并相互关联。

b.source_en: b) Conduct further research on construction chronology, technological transfers, and cross-border military interactions to clarify historical significance,
b.summary: 进一步研究建设年代、技术传播和跨境军事互动，以澄清其历史意义。

c.source_en: c) Reassess the serial approach and consider a more representative selection of fortresses to reflect typological diversity, geographical distribution, and strategic functions of the Raia system,
c.summary: 重新评估系列遗产申报方法，并考虑选择更具代表性的堡垒，以体现 Raia 体系的类型多样性、地理分布和战略功能。

d.source_en: d) Strengthen the comparative analysis with additional Raia fortifications and relevant European and global examples to substantiate claims relating to the potential outstanding universal value,
d.summary: 通过补充更多 Raia 防御工事及相关欧洲和全球案例，加强比较研究，以支持其潜在突出普遍价值的论证。

e.source_en: e) Document the attributes of each of the component parts that will be selected, in terms of their location, topography, typology, conservation state, authenticity, and integrity,
e.summary: 从位置、地形、类型、保护状况、真实性和完整性等方面，记录未来所选各组成部分的属性。

f.source_en: f) Address management, conservation, risk management, and visitor interpretation issues to demonstrate long-term protection and sustainability.
f.summary: 处理管理、保护、风险管理和游客阐释等问题，以证明遗产能够得到长期保护并具备可持续性。

a.source_en: a) D eveloping an integrated conservation plan for the serial property,
a.summary: 制定系列遗产的综合保护计划。

b.source_en: b) Ensuring that baseline documentation includes data on the landscape aspects of the property,
b.summary: 确保基础档案资料包含遗产景观方面的数据。

c.source_en: c) Equipping all municipalities with the relevant planification tools (PLU or PLUi),
c.summary: 为所有市镇配备相关规划工具（PLU 或 PLUi）。

d.source_en: d) Developing a comprehensive tourism strategy for the serial property and integrating it with the management plan,
d.summary: 为系列遗产制定综合旅游策略，并将其纳入管理计划。

e.source_en: e) Preparing an interpretation strategy for the series as a whole,
e.summary: 为整个系列遗产制定阐释策略。

f.source_en: f) Ensuring that Heritage Impact Assessment is conducted before any development project within the property, its buffer zones, and its immediate setting, and that the procedures for Heritage Impact Assessment are consistent with the Guidance and Toolkit for Impact Assessments in a World Heritage Context;
f.summary: 确保在遗产、缓冲区及其直接环境内开展任何发展项目之前进行遗产影响评估，并确保遗产影响评估程序符合《世界遗产背景下影响评估指南与工具包》。

a.source_en: a) Ensuring the operationalisation of the World Heritage Protection Administration management structure as soon as possible, as well as the direct involvement the local community,
a.summary: 尽快使世界遗产保护管理机构投入运行，并确保当地社区直接参与。

b.source_en: b) Updating, approving and implementing the management plan, and extending it to include a strategic vision centred around the conservation of the Outstanding Universal Value of the serial property,
b.summary: 更新、批准并实施管理计划，并扩展其内容，纳入以保护系列遗产突出普遍价值为核心的战略愿景。

c.source_en: c) Developing a conservation strategy in conformity with international standards, for example for the post -excavation treatment of the tombs or their presentation to visitors,
c.summary: 按国际标准制定保护策略，例如墓葬发掘后的处理及向参观者展示。

d.source_en: d) Examining the plan to displace herder families who live near the cemetery complexes in light of their rights and needs, and the advantages of their presence as collaborators and guardians,
d.summary: 结合牧民家庭的权利和需要，以及其作为协作者和守护者在场的优势，审查迁移居住在墓地群附近牧民家庭的计划。

e.source_en: e) Placing markers on the ground to help identify the boundaries of the component parts and their respective buffer zones,
e.summary: 在地面设置标识，以帮助识别各组成部分及其相应缓冲区的边界。

f.source_en: f) Investigating the relationship of the funerary complexes with their wider setting, in order to explore, among other issues, the need to readjust the boundaries of the component parts through minor boundary modification requests,
f.summary: 研究墓葬群与更广阔环境之间的关系，以探讨是否需要通过小幅边界修改申请调整组成部分边界等问题。

g.source_en: g) Designing indicators to measure and assess the state of conservation of the attributes conveying the Outstanding Universal Value, including environmental factors that could have an impact on these attributes,
g.summary: 制定指标，用以衡量和评估承载突出普遍价值的属性的保护状况，包括可能影响这些属性的环境因素。

h.source_en: h) Improving security at the sites to avoid looting and other anthropogenic impacts,
h.summary: 加强遗址安全，避免盗掘和其他人为影响。

i.source_en: i) Developing the visitor information and infrastructure at the component parts and establishing their carrying capacity before promoting the sites as tourism destinations,
i.summary: 在将遗址作为旅游目的地推广前，发展各组成部分的游客信息和基础设施，并确定其承载能力。

j.source_en: j) Informing the World Heritage Centre of the intention to undertake or authorise all major projects which may affect the Outstanding Universal Value of the serial property, in line with paragraph 172 of Operational Guidelines for the Implementation of the World Heritage Convention.
j.summary: 根据《操作指南》第 172 段，将任何可能影响系列遗产突出普遍价值的重大项目的实施或批准意向告知世界遗产中心。

payload.brief_en: Zerzevan Castle and Mithraeum is an archaeological hilltop complex located between Diyarbakır and Mardin in Southeastern Anatolia, Türkiye. The nominated property presents an archaeological ensemble reflecting multiple phases of occupation and adaptation over a long span of time, extending from the Assyrian Period (882-611 BCE) through the Roman and Eastern Roman periods until the conquest of the region by Islamic armies in 639 CE. It comprises the remains of a fortified complex, including defensive walls, towers, gates, domestic quarters, a large cistern system, a church, administrative buildings, rock-cut tombs in associated necropolises, quarries, and other service structures. At the northern end of the fort, excavations have revealed a rock-cut underground space referred to as a Mithraeum, (a temple dedicated to the Roman god Mithras), together with an adjacent underground structure carved into the bedrock. The nominated property extends over a rocky ridge rising above the surrounding plain and incorporates both built and rock-cut elements. The wider setting includes remains associated with water management and circulation routes, including the strategic Late Antiquity Amida-Dara road.
payload.brief_zh: Zerzevan Castle and Mithraeum 位于 Türkiye 东南安纳托利亚 Diyarbakır 与 Mardin 之间，是一处山顶考古遗址群。申报遗产呈现从亚述时期（公元前 882-611 年）经罗马、东罗马时期至公元 639 年伊斯兰军队征服该地区之前的长期占用与适应过程。遗存包括防御墙、塔楼、城门、居住区、大型蓄水系统、教堂、行政建筑、相关墓地中的岩凿墓、采石场和其他服务性结构。堡垒北端发现了被称为 Mithraeum 的岩凿地下空间（供奉罗马神 Mithras 的神庙）及相邻岩凿地下结构。遗产位于高出周边平原的岩质山脊上，包含建筑遗存和岩凿要素；更广阔的环境还包括与水管理和交通路线相关的遗存，如 Late Antiquity Amida-Dara road。

payload.brief_en:
The regional defensive fortification system established by the Capetian kings in the 13th-14th centuries in the former seneschalty of Carcassonne, in current south-west France, consisted of twenty-two fortresses built to protect the seneschal's headquarters at Carcassonne, secure newly annexed lands, safeguard economic resources and control key communication routes, as well as assert royal power in the region through monumental architecture. This serial property, composed of eight component parts, comprises a representative sample of this system and includes the Carcassonne Castle with its double concentric ramparts, which served as the administrative and military centre of the seneschalty, as well as an ensemble of watchmen fortresses built in a relatively short span of time under royal supervision in a rugged topography.

The system of the fortresses of the seneschalty of Carcassonne, stylistically consistent, was designed based on the Capetian military architecture models, developed for the plains of Ile-de-France and adapted to the rocky topography of the Montagne Noire and the Pyrenean foothills. The serial property represents a compelling example of this defensive architecture, which served as a political tool to project the growing, centralised and divinely sanctioned power of the French monarchy, part of a larger movement towards assertion of sovereign powers that marked 13th-century Europe.
payload.brief_zh: 报告第 7 部分推荐 OUV 声明中的 Brief synthesis 原文已补齐为两段。中文可在此基础上继续工作译校。

payload.intro: 引用 ICOMOS 结论部分对价值标准的判断摘要。

criteria.iii: 景德镇手工瓷业遗存展示了 10 至 19 世纪瓷业连续生产过程，以及包括生产社会组织在内的完整产业格局。五个组成部分共同见证了中国手工瓷业传统。

criteria_en.ii:
This criterion is justified by the State Party based on the ability of the nominated property to demonstrate the integration of the handicraft porcelain industry of Jingdezhen with global porcelain production between the 10th and 19th centuries. The production of the distinctive bluish-white porcelain was based on the development of porcelain-making technologies from north and south China in the 10th-13th centuries. Continuous development in the 13th-19th centuries included the production of blueand-white, enamel and famille-rose porcelain, combining Chinese porcelain-making technologies with Middle Eastern and European pigments, and fusion of foreign cultural elements in decorative techniques. The porcelain technologies and artistic style of Jingdezhen attracted global attention. Jingdezhen was a global porcelain production centre from the 16th to the 18th centuries.

ICOMOS considers that the interchange of cultures is demonstrated for the nominated property, although this changed over the span of time represented by the component parts. The earlier periods demonstrate the interchange of knowledge and experimentation with technologies with other regions in China, progressively developing the production methods for which Jingdezhen is distinctive. Later exchanges through global trading networks brought new pigments, forms and techniques, which in turn underpinned the global popularity of Chinese porcelain, particularly the blue-and-white porcelain which was both highly prized and widely imitated. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is demonstrated.

The Jingdezhen Handicraft Porcelain Industry Sites demonstrate global exchanges in porcelain-making technologies and art, and innovations in porcelain production from the 10th to the 19th centuries. The Hutian Ancient Kiln Site portrays the integration of porcelain-making technologies for bluish-white porcelain from the north and south of China from the 10th-13th centuries. The development of technologies at Jingdezhen during the 13th-19th centuries combined traditional Chinese porcelain-making with Middle Eastern, Chinese and European pigments, and a fusion of Chinese and foreign decorative techniques to produce blue-and-white, enamel and famille-rose porcelain. The “binary formula” technology attracted widespread attention and was an important inspiration and influence on the development of high-temperature hard-paste porcelain technology in Europe during the 18th century. Jingdezhen became a world porcelain production centre from the 16th-18th centuries, with its artistic styles studied and imitated all over the world.
criteria_en.iii:
This criterion is justified by the State Party based on the ability of the selected sites to demonstrate in an exceptional way the continuous process of production of porcelain from the 10th to 19th centuries, including the associated social organisation. In additional information received in October 2025, the State Party further clarified how the nominated property represents the cultural tradition of Chinese handicraft porcelain production based on the traditions of production techniques, social organisation in the porcelain industry and the evolution of aesthetic and artistic traditions.

ICOMOS considers that the nominated property demonstrates the industrial and social organisation of the production of porcelain and the changes between the 10th and 19th centuries. In this context, the production of porcelain was more than a technical process. The nominated property contains evidence of craft traditions and expertise, and the complex social organisation of the production of porcelain that evolved over many centuries. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is demonstrated.

The Jingdezhen Handicraft Porcelain Industry Sites exhibit the continuous production process of the porcelain industry from the 10th to the 19th centuries and a complete industrial pattern including the social organisation of production. The five component parts provide a testimony to the tradition of Chinese handicraft porcelain industry.
criteria_en.iv:
This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that the selected sites demonstrate a technological ensemble that illustrates the development of the porcelain industry. This includes the art and industrial organisation model from the 13th to the 19th century. The State Party asserts that Jingdezhen is a unique and outstanding example of innovation in porcelain technology, including the use of raw materials, modelling and glazing techniques and the “binary formula”. The gourd-shape and egg-shape kilns were innovations in firing technology. The State Party also justifies this criterion on the basis of the division and organisation of space and labour.

The Jingdezhen Handicraft Porcelain Industry Sites are a technological ensemble that illustrates important stages in the development of the handicraft porcelain industry, an outstanding example of innovation in global porcelain technologies, art and industrial organisation from the 13th to the 19th centuries. The uses of raw materials at Jingdezhen, the “binary formula” technology, modelling technologies, kiln designs, and innovations in underglaze and overglaze decoration techniques enriched the diversity of porcelain shapes and decorative styles. The large-scale industrial spatial layout, division of labour, and specialised and standardised production model enabled the efficient and high-quality porcelain production at Jingdezhen.
criteria_en.vi:
This criterion is justified by the State Party on the grounds that traded porcelain from Jingdezhen became a vehicle of Chinese culture and an important symbol of oriental art. The blue-and-white porcelain of Jingdezhen was a key element of the “chinoiserie” popular in European decorative arts in the 17th and 18th centuries, including interior design, furniture, porcelain, textiles and gardens. European nobility became collectors of Chinese porcelain, and many famous palaces feature “Chinese rooms” with various forms of designed objects. The artistic characteristics of Jingdezhen porcelain were widely imitated.

ICOMOS considers that the porcelain of Jingdezhen (especially the blue-and-white porcelain) is a recognisable symbol of Chinese culture and Oriental art and was linked to European decorative arts in the 17th and 18th centuries. As a luxury trade commodity, it became central to the European fashion for “chinoiserie”. Through its designs and motifs, it influenced painting, furniture, architecture and decoration, serving as a medium for the global expression of Chinese civilisation and culture. ICOMOS considers that this criterion is demonstrated. ICOMOS considers that the nominated property meets criteria (ii), (iii), (iv) and (vi), that the serial approach is justified and that the selection of component parts is adequate.

As an important vehicle of Chinese culture and an important symbol of Oriental art, evidence of blue-and-white porcelain of Jingdezhen establishes a direct association with European decorative art from the 17th-18th centuries. As a global high-end trade commodity, the blue-and-white porcelain of Jingdezhen is one of the core elements of “chinoiserie” that was popular in Europe. These artistic elements were widely collected and used in pattern design, painting, furniture, architectural decoration and other fields, with porcelain serving as a medium to promote the global expression of Chinese culture.
