entries_en.integrity:
Following the modifications made to the boundaries of some component parts and the expansion of the buffer zones, ICOMOS considers that the integrity of the urban component parts has been significantly strengthened. ICOMOS considers that the conditions of integrity can be demonstrated by the ensemble formed by the nine component parts located in Tashkent, excluding the Big Solar Furnace (component part 2), which does not reflect the proposed Outstanding Universal Value.
ICOMOS also considers that the conditions of integrity would be significantly enhanced by the inclusion, at a later stage, of the Tashkent TV Tower, the Hotel Uzbekistan, and the Palace of Aviation Constructors.
entries_en.authenticity: ICOMOS considers that the conditions of authenticity have been demonstrated for the nine urban component parts in Tashkent, excluding the Big Solar Furnace (component part 2), as credibly contributing to the reflection of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value.
entries_en.integrity_authenticity_conclusion:
In conclusion, ICOMOS considers that the conditions of integrity and authenticity of the nominated serial property are met, except for the Big Solar Furnace (component part 2). The integrity of a reduced series comprising only the nine urban component parts would be reinforced by extending it to include the Tashkent TV Tower, the Hotel Uzbekistan and the Palace of Aviation Constructors. To this end, ICOMOS advises that a roadmap for their inclusion at a later stage, via a significant boundary modification procedure, be prepared, with a clear timeframe and steps.
ICOMOS considers it essential that the State Party undertakes an individual, detailed assessment of the conditions of integrity and authenticity of each nominated component part, including the identification of specific measures to improve them. The extensive research conducted in recent years provides a solid basis for effective, operational conservation planning and interventions to improve the integrity of the nominated component parts.
Finally, ICOMOS considers that long-term preservation of the integrity and authenticity of the nominated property would require particular attention to the visual interconnections between these buildings as urban foci and to the role played by the vegetation and landscaping in the wider setting, as this was a key dimension of the broader urban design.
entries_en.boundaries: In the February 2026 additional information, the State Party significantly expanded some buffer zones and slightly adjusted the boundaries of some of the component parts, fully addressing ICOMOS’ observations. The rationale for these expanded boundaries is clearly explained and justified. As such, the buffer zones provide an added layer of protection to the urban component parts and support the full expression of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value.

entries.protection_property: Component parts are legally protected, but implementation is weakened by unclear/insufficient regulatory regimes and agency capacity constraints.
entries.protection_buffer_zone: Expanded buffer zones address ICOMOS concerns, but their effectiveness depends on approval, enforcement, cadastre/master-plan integration and temporary moratorium measures.
entries.conservation: State of conservation is generally good but uneven; conservation and maintenance guidance/protocols are needed to avoid inappropriate interventions.
entries.management: Governance is clear, but institutional cooperation should be formalised; monitoring, risk management, HIA and tourism management require further development.
entries.threats_addressed: Heavy urban development pressure and approved nearby projects remain a major risk until HIAs, buffer-zone regulations, visual protections and moratorium are operational.

entries_en.conservation:
ICOMOS considers that the nominated serial property is well documented, and documentation will continue to grow with the implementation of the planned conservation strategy. Guidance and protocols for maintenance shall ensure the functionality of the buildings and the ability of their attributes to convey the proposed Outstanding Universal Value.
ICOMOS observes that it would be advisable to further develop the monitoring system to explicitly encompass all the attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. The system would benefit from further clarification on the implementation of the proposed third-level monitoring and how data will be used to inform management adjustments. Finally, consideration should be given to designing it to facilitate the easy integration of its outcomes into the Periodic Reporting questionnaire.
entries_en.management:
In summary, ICOMOS considers that the protection and management system will be fully adequate for effective safeguarding of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value in the long term once the boundaries of the revised buffer zones and associated draft Regulation on the Management and Protection Zone Regime are approved and enforced, and the provisions for visually sensitive areas are integrated into the 2024 master plan.
Both instruments are crucial and should therefore be adopted and made operational urgently. Consideration should be given to a moratorium on approving new construction within the designated buffer zones and visually sensitive areas until the buffer zones and their regulations enter into force and provisions for visual sensitivities are integrated into the master plan.
The governance system is clear. However, the commitment among the relevant institutional actors to cooperate for the effective, long-term safeguarding of the nominated property, and the roles of each actor in the joint management of the nominated serial property, would need to be formalised through a jointly signed agreement.

a.source_en: a) Approving the enlarged buffer zones with the associated regulations as soon as possible, based on the implementation calendar provided by the State Party, including their boundaries in the State Cadastre, and earmarking them also in the cartographic documentation of the master plan for Tashkent City,
a.summary: ICOMOS正式建议条目；已按同事版主题体系完成逐条分类。

b.source_en: b) Integrating the outcomes of the visual study into the 2024 master plan as soon as possible, alongside appropriate regulatory measures,
b.summary: ICOMOS正式建议条目；已按同事版主题体系完成逐条分类。

c.source_en: c) Ensuring a temporary moratorium on new construction within the planned buffer zones and the visually sensitive areas until the enlarged buffer zones and their regulations are approved, and the master plan is integrated with provisions for viewsheds,
c.summary: ICOMOS正式建议条目；已按同事版主题体系完成逐条分类。

d.source_en: d) Reinforcing the regulatory regimes for conservation and reconstruction zones included in the 2024 m aster plan by implementing stricter, clearer protection regimes and integrating Heritage Impact Assessment mechanisms into the master plan, based on the Guidance and Toolkit for Impact Assessments in a World Heritage Context,
d.summary: ICOMOS正式建议条目；已按同事版主题体系完成逐条分类。

e.source_en: e) Suspending the implementation of approved projects that, for their location, size and design, might have a negative impact on the Outstanding Universal Value of the serial property until Heritage Impact Assessments are carried out and measures to remove, reduce or mitigate potential negative impacts are incorporated into the implementation of such projects,
e.summary: ICOMOS正式建议条目；已按同事版主题体系完成逐条分类。

g.source_en: g) Developing an ad hoc risk management framework to be integrated into the management system,
g.summary: ICOMOS正式建议条目；已按同事版主题体系完成逐条分类。

j.summary: ICOMOS正式建议条目；已按同事版主题体系完成逐条分类。

payload.brief_zh: ICOMOS推荐OUV声明中的Brief synthesis原文已抽取；中文页面可在此基础上进行工作译文和审校。
payload.brief_en:
The Aalto Works comprise thirteen modern buildings and building ensembles designed between 1928 and 1988 by Alvar Aalto in collaboration with his first spouse Aino Marsio-Aalto and second spouse Elissa Aalto, as well as the employees of the Aalto studio (1923-1994) and Artek (founded 1935), their affiliated interior design and furnishings company. Located in both urban and rural areas of Finland, the serial property bears witness to the application of global principles of the 20th-century Modern Movement, as a response to new societal and universal needs. 

Strongly rooted in local building traditions, the Aalto Works are characterised by a comprehensive and individualistic design approach driven by an empathetic, site-specific, and human-centred vision, where each project is unique and responds to specific programmatic requirements. The serial property showcases the Aaltos’ experiments in construction technology, architectural aesthetics, spatial sequences, lighting, and the use of both natural and industrial materials. Each building is carefully integrated into its surroundings. This synthetic design approach also encompassed interior design, merging industrial elements with local traditional materials. Most of the buildings still feature their original collections of furniture and lighting designed by the Aalto studio.

payload.summary_en: Based on the nomination dossier and the additional information, and according to the State Party, the key attributes (“primary attributes” in the State Party’s terminology) of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value are as follows: 1. Elements that contribute to the modernisation of society, 2. The use of urban planning, architecture and design in creating an identity for the new nation, 3. A mutually beneficial interaction with the site context and surrounding natural or urban environment, 4. A synthesis of science, art, design and architecture, 5. Designed interiors as an integral part of the Aalto Works, 6. Collaborative design work, 7. An innovative approach and international impact on modern design. ICOMOS notes that the nomination is built around seven identified key attributes characterising the Aalto Works related to the proposed justification for criterion (ii), each of which is present in one or two of the nominated component parts. Then, for each nominated component part, the State Party has identified an additional group of secondary attributes – technical attributes in the terms of legislative protection in Finland. ICOMOS considers that the proposed key attributes do not convey the distinctive qualities of the nominated property in a clear and comprehensible way. In addition, any given attribute of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value must be reflected not only in one or two component parts, but in each of the nominated component parts. This issue was addressed in the ICOMOS interim report. In the additional information of February 2026, the State Party provided more detailed description of the proposed attributes, underlying the specific connections between the thirteen component parts and their contribution to each of the seven key attributes, as well as the way each of the attributes is present in each of the component parts. The State Party also highlighted the distinctiveness of each of the component parts and their qualities. ICOMOS considers that the proposed key attributes are too general, noting conceptual overlap and cross-cutting relationships. Consequently, ICOMOS recommends refining them and suggests combining the attributes 1 and 2 (“contribution to the modernisation of society” and “the use of urban planning, architecture and design in creating an identity for the new nation”). Furthermore, ICOMOS considers that the reference to the creation of an identity for the new nation cannot be considered as an argument that…

1.name: 完整性章节涉及支撑OUV的组成要素、属性完整性、系列组成关系及脆弱性。

2.name: 真实性章节涉及属性的信息来源、物质与非物质真实性、使用功能、环境背景及相关风险。

mentions.source_en: Integrity is a measure of the wholeness and intactness of the attributes that convey the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. For a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole and how each component part contributes to that value. The integrity of the Aalto Works is based on the influential structures, features and spaces created by the Aalto studio that best illustrate the integration of a new international architectural language with traditional local ideas during the modernisation of society in the 20th century by using sensitivity and a human-centred approach to architecture and design. Overall, the component parts are intact and retain their relationship with their associated landscapes and settings. Most retain interiors with significant fittings and furniture. Many sustain their original use, or sympathetic functions. The Paimio Sanatorium (component part 002) is the most vulnerable of the nominated component parts in terms of sustaining an appropriate use and needing significant conservation interventions, though there are mechanisms in place to mitigate potential losses. ICOMOS considers that the integrity of the nominated series as a whole as well as the integrity of each of the component parts have been demonstrated.
mentions.summary: 完整性章节涉及支撑OUV的组成要素、属性完整性、系列组成关系及脆弱性。

entries_en.integrity: Integrity is a measure of the wholeness and intactness of the attributes that convey the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. For a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole and how each component part contributes to that value. The integrity of the Aalto Works is based on the influential structures, features and spaces created by the Aalto studio that best illustrate the integration of a new international architectural language with traditional local ideas during the modernisation of society in the 20th century by using sensitivity and a human-centred approach to architecture and design. Overall, the component parts are intact and retain their relationship with their associated landscapes and settings. Most retain interiors with significant fittings and furniture. Many sustain their original use, or sympathetic functions. The Paimio Sanatorium (component part 002) is the most vulnerable of the nominated component parts in terms of sustaining an appropriate use and needing significant conservation interventions, though there are mechanisms in place to mitigate potential losses. ICOMOS considers that the integrity of the nominated series as a whole as well as the integrity of each of the component parts have been demonstrated.
entries_en.authenticity: The authenticity of the nominated property is based on the form and design, materials and substance, use and function, location and setting as well as spirit and feeling, articulated through a series of thirteen component parts. Authenticity depends on the degree of credibility and truthfulness of information sources on the value of the nominated property. These sources of information must be considered and judged primarily within the cultural contexts to which they belong. ICOMOS considers that all the nominated component parts have preserved their forms and designs. Careful conservation and management of each component part have ensured that the authenticity of these attributes is sustained. In some cases, previous work that had an impact on design authenticity has been reversed in recent conservation projects.
entries_en.boundaries: The estimated population within the nominated property totals 384 people residing in four of the thirteen component parts. There are 4,615 people living within the proposed buffer zones (2023-2024 figures). The boundaries of the component parts are clearly identified and delineated. Most refer to the historical layout, adopting the current legal or administrative divisions, and follow the boundaries of the plots where buildings and groups of buildings are located. Most of the proposed boundaries are consistent with national heritage or other protective designations of the land register.

entries.integrity: 依据Integrity章节是否明确认为条件已满足/已证明初判。

entries_en.conservation: Conservation is characterised as sensitive, consistent and methodologically rigorous, supported by robust documentation and experienced stewardship.

entries.protection_property: Legal protection and current management frameworks are described as well-established and effective.
entries.protection_buffer_zone: Development pressures around proposed buffer zones and changes in ownership/function require continued coordination and planning attention.
entries.conservation: Conservation is characterised as sensitive, consistent and methodologically rigorous, supported by robust documentation and experienced stewardship.
entries.management: Management frameworks are effective, but the proposed coordination mechanism requires further participatory development and implementation.
entries.threats_addressed: Climate change, development pressures and ownership/function changes are identified; comprehensive interpretation, presentation and visitor management are still needed.

a.source_en: a) Further refining the attributes to make them more operational for management purposes by identifying specific physical qualities, material fabric, tangible features, and relevant intangible aspects (such as processes, social arrangements, or cultural practices), as well as interactions between them,
a.summary: 进一步细化属性，使其更便于管理运用；明确具体物质品质、材料构造、有形特征、相关非物质层面及其相互关系。

b.source_en: b) Securing founds for maintenance and conservation interventions until a compatible use for the Paimio Sanatorium (component part 002) and all its features is found and established,
b.summary: 在 Paimio Sanatorium（组成部分 002）及其全部特征找到并确立相容用途前，确保维护和保护干预所需资金。

c.source_en: c) Adopting a maintenance and development strategy for secondary facilities, as well as for areas or buildings not considered as supporting the Outstanding Universal Value of the property, whether designed by the Aalto studio or not,
c.summary: 为次要设施，以及不被认为支撑遗产突出普遍价值的区域或建筑制定维护与发展策略，无论其是否由 Aalto 工作室设计。

d.source_en: d) Completing the formal designation process of buffer zones into planning documents as soon as possible, to ensure that they provide an effective added layer of protection to the property,
d.summary: 尽快完成将缓冲区正式纳入规划文件的程序，确保其为遗产提供有效的附加保护层。

e.source_en: e) Further developing through participatory processes, and putting into place management coordination structures and mechanisms for both individual component parts and the serial property as a whole,
e.summary: 通过参与式过程进一步发展并落实各组成部分及整个系列遗产的管理协调结构和机制。

f.source_en: f) Continuing to develop and update the Aalto Works Management Plan for the property as well as the individual plans for the component parts, and ensuring their implementation,
f.summary: 持续完善并更新遗产整体的 Aalto Works 管理计划及各组成部分的单项计划，并确保实施。

g.source_en: g) Further developing the monitoring framework to include clear monitoring indicators, including those supporting protection of interiors, and all current and potential factors affecting the property, such as, among others, development and tourism pressures,
g.summary: 进一步完善监测框架，纳入明确监测指标，包括支持室内空间保护的指标，以及发展、旅游压力等当前和潜在影响因素。

h.source_en: h) Developing and implementing a comprehensive interpretation and presentation strategy, including visitor management,
h.summary: 制定并实施综合性的阐释和展示策略，其中包括游客管理。

i.source_en: i) Ensuring that Heritage Impact Assessment is conducted before any development project within the property, its buffer zones and its immediate settings and that its procedures are consistent with the Guidance and Toolkit for Impact Assessments in a World Heritage Context.
i.summary: 确保在遗产、缓冲区及其直接环境内开展任何发展项目之前进行遗产影响评估，并使程序符合《世界遗产背景下影响评估指南与工具包》。

payload.brief_zh: ICOMOS推荐OUV声明中的Brief synthesis原文已抽取；中文页面可在此基础上进行工作译文和审校。
payload.brief_en:
On 6 June 1944, Operation Neptune, the first phase of Operation Overlord, conceived to open a new Western European Front during the Second World War against the forces of the Third Reich, was launched from beaches on the Normandy coast that have come to be known as the Beaches of the D-Day Landings. 

Along with the military action and human sacrifice on multiple fronts in Eastern Europe, Northern Africa and the Pacific, the Normandy Landings was one of the decisive moments in the Second World War, when after years of planning, seventeen allied nations collaborated in military, logistical, political, and moral resources as part of a concerted plan to overcome a regime of conquest and genocide. 

The serial property, comprising six component parts, commemorates the way years of planning and alliancebuilding culminated in collective, multinational, logistical and military efforts that marked the beginning of the end of the Second World War in Western Europe, ultimately contributing to the toppling of a totalitarian regime and paving the way for reconstruction, reconciliation, and a renewed commitment to collective security. 

The Beaches of the D-Day Landings also embody, symbolise, and evoke memories of the immense human sacrifice made to begin to break the entrenched authoritarian regime of the Third Reich that occupied most of Europe in the mid-20th century. 

The landing operations, launched on 6 June 1944, took place on five beaches (from west to east): Utah Beach, Omaha Beach, Gold Beach, Juno Beach and Sword Beach, as well as at Pointe du Hoc, the sixth amphibious assault site, constituting altogether the component parts of the serial property. Each site still bears the traces of the event. 

The serial property contains many tangible attributes: the beaches themselves, the five landing sites of 6 June 1944, and an amphibious assault site; remains of the most remarkable structures illustrating the logistical efforts made to ensure the success of Operation Neptune, particularly the artificial harbour known as Mulberry B; remains of the defensive system or “Atlantic Wall” that the Allies faced; traces left by the confrontation in the landscape, deliberately preserved as a reminder of the event; memorial sites created since 1944; memorial monuments, and finally, the American Military Cemetery at Colleville-sur-Mer, built in the immediate vicinity of Omaha Beach. They also contain intangible attributes, memories of the event to which they are intrinsically linked, having acquired strong symbolic significance. 

In paying tribute to the victims of the confrontation, the Beaches of the D-Day Landings have also become a place of reconciliation between former belligerents.

payload.summary_en: According to the State Party, based on the nomination dossier and the additional information, the key attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value can be grouped as follows: the remains of the defensive fortifications known as the Atlantic Wall, such as batteries and their structures and military equipment when still present, the radar station, bridges; the traces of the logistical arrangements for the offensive, most notably the structure of the artificial harbour known as Mulberry B; memorial elements, particularly the military cemeteries; underwater remains; and other traces left by the conflict within the landscape.

payload.summary_en: ICOMOS considers that Other key sites that may be nominated as part of other associations linked to the memory of this global conflict should have been included in the comparative analysis, which should also have recognised their complementary nature. ICOMOS considers that, despite its shortcomings, the comparative analysis justifies consideration of this property for the World Heritage List.
payload.summary: 比较分析章节已抽取原文和主要结论；比较对象采用正则初步识别，需在页面中人工复核名录状态和比较意义。

entries.integrity: 依据Integrity章节是否明确认为条件已满足/已证明初判。
entries.authenticity: 依据Authenticity章节是否明确认为条件已满足/已证明初判。
entries.integrity_authenticity_conclusion: 待抽取 ICOMOS 相应段落的结论。
entries.boundaries: 依据Boundaries章节对遗产区/缓冲区边界是否充分、是否需修订的表述初判。

entries_en.integrity: Following the modifications to the boundaries of the nominated property proposed by the State Party, ICOMOS considers that the integrity of the nominated series as a whole, as well as the integrity of each of the component parts have been demonstrated. However, given the ongoing natural and anthropogenic dynamics, including tourism development and the production of renewable energy, it will be essential to closely monitor the condition of the attributes, to strictly enforce the protection measures, and to implement paragraph 118bis of the Operational Guidelines for the Implementation of the World Heritage Convention.
entries_en.authenticity:
ICOMOS considers that the revised nominated property, with its attributes, can be seen as credibly reflecting its association with the D-Day Landings, as the initial phase of Operation Overlord, which aimed to free Western Europe from the Third Reich during the Second World War which, as part of a sequence of interconnected events, eventually led to the end of the war. However, the attributes of the nominated property cannot be considered credible sources of information to support the association between the nominated property and the foundation of the United Nations in 1945.

ICOMOS considers that the authenticity of the nominated series as a whole, as well as the authenticity of each of the component parts, has been demonstrated on the basis of the revised justification for inscription.
entries_en.boundaries:
ICOMOS considers that the proposed modifications to the boundaries are justified as they encompass the most significant physical attributes that bear witness to the event, as well as certain key memorials and cemeteries that reflect the link with the D-Day Landings and their memorialisation. The maritime part of the buffer zone preserves the submerged remains of structures used in the preparation for the D-Day Landings, whilst the terrestrial part of the buffer zone includes both historical remains bearing witness to the defensive system and to Operation Neptune, as well as cemeteries and memorials.
ICOMOS considers that the revised boundaries are adequate to ensure the inclusion of the attributes that convey the global significance of the event. The extensive buffer zone provides the necessary added layer of protection to preserve the spirit of the place and the landscape features that make the associations perceivable and credible.

entries.protection_property: Legal protection is adequate overall but does not always stem from heritage-specific provisions; ICOMOS recommends extending heritage protection to component parts not currently covered.
entries.protection_buffer_zone: Some expanded buffer-zone areas are governed mainly by spatial/urban planning, so protection mechanisms within the buffer zone need strengthening.
entries.conservation: Material evidence survives and documentation is substantial, but coastal erosion, corrosion, cliff instability and uneven survival require continued conservation attention.
entries.management: Governance is complex and requires strong coordination across instruments and stakeholders; interpretation also needs a coherent OUV-based strategy.
entries.threats_addressed: Coastal dynamics, renewable-energy projects, urban/tourism development and wind-farm impacts remain major issues requiring vigilance and strengthened planning controls.

entries_en.conservation: Material evidence survives and documentation is substantial, but coastal erosion, corrosion, cliff instability and uneven survival require continued conservation attention.
entries_en.management:
In summary, ICOMOS considers that the nominated serial property benefits from adequate legal protection, although such protection does not always stem from heritage-specific provisions, and that some parts of the expanded buffer zone are primarily governed by spatial and urban planning frameworks. It is therefore recommended that the protection regime be strengthened, in particular by extending heritage protection to component parts not currently covered by these mechanisms and by reinforcing protection mechanisms within the buffer zone. Clear objectives for safeguarding attributes supporting the justification for inscription and their landscape setting, should be promptly integrated into the management system and relevant planning instruments. 

The governance of this extensive and densely populated area is inherently complex, requiring strong coordination across multiple instruments and stakeholders; however, municipalities' commitment to the buffer zone is a positive indication as to the future effectiveness of this aspect. Vigilance over development is essential, particularly regarding renewable energy projects, including the offshore wind farm. According to ICOMOS, this should be dismantled once its concession expires and the site should be restored to its original condition. 

A coherent interpretation strategy based on the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property remains necessary and should be fully developed. Continued dialogue with stakeholders, including those expressing dissenting views, and sustained awareness-raising efforts are essential to ensure consensus and that promotional initiatives do not detract from the values and significance of the nominated property.

e.summary: ICOMOS正式建议条目；已按同事版主题体系完成逐条分类。

f.source_en: f) Developing a fully- fledged interpretation strategy based on the Outstanding Universal Value to ensure a consistent and unified approach across the serial property,
f.summary: ICOMOS正式建议条目；已按同事版主题体系完成逐条分类。

g.source_en: g) Developing strategies to manage increasing visitor pressure and prevent damage to attributes and the sense of place,
g.summary: ICOMOS正式建议条目；已按同事版主题体系完成逐条分类。

h.source_en: h) Maintaining ongoing dialogue with all stakeholders and strengthening awareness - raising efforts among local actors and communities,
h.summary: ICOMOS正式建议条目；已按同事版主题体系完成逐条分类。

i.source_en: i) Ensuring that the offshore wind farm under construction in the buffer zone is dismantled at the end of its concession (2057) and that the site is restored to its original condition,
i.summary: ICOMOS正式建议条目；已按同事版主题体系完成逐条分类。

j.source_en: j) Ensuring that the procedures for Heritage Impact Assessment are consistent with the Guidance and Toolkit for Impact Assessments in a World Heritage Context, and that Heritage Impact Assessment is conducted before any development project within the serial property, its buffer zone and its immediate setting, with a particular focus on renewable energy projects in the buffer zone and wider setting,

k.source_en: k) Informing the World Heritage Centre of the intention to undertake or authorise all major projects which may affect the Outstanding Universal Value of the serial property, in line with paragraph 172 of Operational Guidelines for the Implementation of the World Heritage Convention.

payload.summary_en: Based on the nomination dossier, and according to the State Party, the key attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value can be grouped as follows: the Castle of Carcassonne and its ramparts as a central stronghold of military and political power; the newly created defensive system spread on the territory of the seneschalty of Carcassonne; a coherent construction programme following the principles of the 13th-century Capetian military architecture; watchmen fortresses built on limestone ridges; strategic position for defence purposes; ostentatious monumentality; architectural rationality and military efficiency; vistas and panoramas; and the surrounding landscape unoccupied by civilian residences. ICOMOS considers that the State Party developed the justification for the proposed Outstanding Universal Value around two strands. The first one is related to the consolidation of power by the kings of France in the Languedoc region, and the creation of the seneschalty of Carcassonne, of which the nominated serial property is considered a tangible manifestation. The second strand is centred around the qualities of the Capetian military architecture and their adaptation to the distinctive topographical setting. The Castle of Carcassonne and its ramparts (component part 01) is slightly different in this sense, not being a hill/mountain fort but forming an important part of the defence system, as it served as its administrative centre. In the additional information of October 2025, the State Party explained that from the twenty-two fortresses under direct administration of the seneschal of Carcassonne, it selected those that served military and administrative functions, rather than played a symbolic role. The historical analysis of the constitution of the fortified system allows to identify three phases in the history of consolidation of the territory and link them to three groups of fortresses: (1) fortresses to the east, in the territory long-time pacified, which were annexed in the first phase; (2) fortresses in the south and those that directly protected Carcassonne, linked to the resistance of the populations against the Church and the king of France, as well as the border conflicts with the Kingdom of Aragon; and (3) fortresses later added to the system. The watchmen fortresses selected for the nominated serial property belong to the second group. Stylistic characteristics, architectural similarities, and integration with and visual dominance over…

mentions.source_en: The integrity of the nominated serial property is based on the inclusion of sufficient number of structural elements of the construction programme implemented by the Capetian Crown to control the conquered region of Languedoc in the aftermath of the Albigensian Crusade, and the related landscape aspects that played a role in the spread and consolidation of the royal control over this territory to fully convey the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. As a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole, and how each component part contributes to that value. Integrity is furthermore a measure of the intactness of the attributes. The nominated component parts represent partially the fortification system created for the seneschalty of Carcassonne, which included fortresses of different types, built at different stages of the military campaigns. Moreover, the conquest of Languedoc involved, besides construction of fortresses on the conquered territory, the establishment of “new towns”, which were strategically placed and helped resettle the population to ensure loyalty to the French Crown. In that sense, ICOMOS considers that the nominated series cannot be said to include enough necessary attributes to express the proposed Outstanding Universal Value fully.

entries_en.integrity: ICOMOS considers that the integrity of the nominated series as a whole, as well as the integrity of each of the component parts have been demonstrated in relation to a narrative focused on the architectural aspects of the nominated property.
entries_en.authenticity: ICOMOS considers that the authenticity of the nominated series as a whole, as well as the authenticity of each of the component parts have been demonstrated in relation to a narrative focused on the architectural aspects of the nominated property.
entries_en.integrity_authenticity_conclusion: In conclusion, ICOMOS considers that the conditions of integrity and authenticity of the nominated series as a whole and of the individual component parts have been met, but only in relation to a narrative centred on the political significance of the standardised Capetian military architecture and its diffusion through adaptation.
entries_en.boundaries: ICOMOS considers that the methodology applied to define the boundaries of the component parts of the nominated serial property and the buffer zones as well as the wider landscape setting is logical.

entries.protection_property: Legal protection is appropriate but should be strengthened through local urban planning tools for all municipalities.
entries.protection_buffer_zone: Buffer-zone/rural landscape management needs continued strengthening through local planning, fire prevention and landscape-management measures.
entries.conservation: Regular consolidation and restoration are ongoing, but several ruins remain unstable and environmental agents/fire/landslides require management.
entries.management: The proposed future management system is well developed, but effectiveness depends on the capacity of the AMPM managing body.
entries.threats_addressed: Environmental factors, fire risk, tourism at Carcassonne and rural development pressures are identified and addressed, but tourism and interpretation strategies are still needed.

entries_en.conservation: ICOMOS considers that a conservation plan needs to be developed for the nominated serial property as a whole, and individual conservation plans integrated with it. Baseline documentation should include information on the landscape attributes of the nominated property.
entries_en.management: In summary, ICOMOS considers that the legal protection of the nominated serial property is appropriate but should be strengthened by equipping all municipalities with relevant local urban planning tools (PLU or PLUi). The proposed future management system is well developed. Its effectiveness will depend on the capacity of the AMPM as the managing body. A comprehensive tourism strategy should be prepared and paired with an interpretation strategy for the nominated series as a whole.

payload.brief_en: The condominio theatre represents a major evolution in the Italian-style theatre model around the mid-18th and late 19th centuries, marking the transition from being a place for aristocratic entertainment to becoming a cultural institution driven by an emerging bourgeoisie. Based on a unique partnership between public bodies and groups of wealthy citizens, known as società condominiali, this economic model is founded on collective ownership and the sharing of costs. This management structure enabled the wide spread of theatres, particularly in small and medium-sized towns in Italy. By making the financers of theatre projects partners in their design, the condominio model turned these theatres into spaces for architectural experimentation and as 240 economic driving forces for these small and mediumsized towns. The condominio model thus fostered the development of specific architectural features manifesting themselves in precise adjustments to social spaces (entrance halls, foyer, ridotto), the standardisation of the tiered boxes, the optimisation of sightlines, the development of separate circulation systems, transforming the theatre into a tool of prestige precisely tailored for its users. This model fostered the emergence of spaces for social life where culture belonged to an emerging bourgeoisie. In addition to their artistic purpose, these theatres have also played a role as centres of political awareness, transforming small and medium-sized towns into dynamic social and cultural centres; a function they still retain today.
payload.brief_zh: ICOMOS推荐OUV声明中的Brief synthesis原文已抽取；中文页面可在此基础上进行工作译文和审校。

payload.summary_en: Based on the nomination dossier and the additional information, and according to the State Party, the key attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value are: a specific financing method used for the construction of these theatres, based on the patronage of the società condominiali, a partnership between public bodies and individuals from the emerging bourgeoisie; the presence of an auditorium (U-shaped, bell-shaped, horseshoeshaped, truncated oval shape or elliptical) with boxes, spaces reserved for the public (including reception areas such as ridotti and entrance halls) and stage machinery; a high density and interrelatedness of large-capacity Italian-style theatres, forming a regional cultural system dedicated to live performance; the harmonious incorporation of the theatres into their historical urban setting through rigorous planning; exchanges between architects, artists and theatre craftspeople; and the continuity of use of these theatres in accordance with their original purpose as venues for performances, meetings and gatherings. ICOMOS considers that some of these attributes are not sufficiently specific or relevant to determining the significance of condominio theatres.

mentions.source_en: Integrity is a measure of the completeness of the nominated property, its intactness and the absence of threats to its attributes. As a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole, and how each component part contributes to that value. Not all the theatres of the nominated serial property have retained all of their original layout, decor and original furniture. The fully or partially preserved stage equipment and machinery are often no longer in service. Some theatres have also suffered significant damage due to earthquakes, the Second World War, fires or other incidents. The damaged or destroyed parts of these theatres have been restored or rebuilt, while in some cases they are awaiting restoration work. The entrances to the theatres have generally been modified and adapted to make them accessible for all audiences. The additional information provided by the State Party in November 2025, in particular aerial views accompanied by photographs of the buildings located within the nominated property

payload.summary_en: ICOMOS considers that the international comparative analysis covers a very broad geographical and historical scope, with some comparisons that are not necessarily relevant in the specific context of the development of these theatres. ICOMOS considers that, despite certain weaknesses and gaps, the comparative analysis justifies consideration of the inscription of a reduced serial property comprising component parts 02, 03, 04, 05, 06, 07, 09, 12, 16, 17 on the World Heritage List.

entries_en.integrity: ICOMOS considers that the integrity of the nominated series as a whole, reduced to component parts 02, 03, 04, 05, 06, 07, 09, 12, 16, 17, as well as the integrity of each of these component parts have been demonstrated.
entries_en.authenticity: ICOMOS considers that the authenticity of the nominated series as whole, reduced to component parts 02, 03, 04, 05, 06, 07, 09, 12, 16, 17, and the authenticity of each of the component parts have been demonstrated.
entries_en.integrity_authenticity_conclusion: In conclusion, ICOMOS considers that the conditions of integrity and authenticity of the reduced nominated series as a whole, comprising component parts 02, 03, 04, 05, 06, 07, 09, 12, 16, 17, and of each of these component parts, are met.
entries_en.boundaries: ICOMOS considers that this rationale is appropriate, and that the revisions made to the boundaries of the component parts included in the proposed reduced series and their buffer zones are relevant. The State Party would need to submit a minor boundary modification request to adjust the boundaries of Teatro Gentile da Fabriano (component part 03) according to this approach. This is the theatre that ICOMOS recommends including in the reduced nominated series, in place of Teatro della Fortuna (component part 14).

entries.protection_property: ICOMOS considers the nominated series to benefit from an appropriate level of legal protection and regional/local mechanisms safeguarding the attributes.
entries.protection_buffer_zone: Regional and local protection mechanisms are considered capable of safeguarding the attributes; no major buffer-zone deficiency is retained in the final conclusion.
entries.conservation: Most auditoriums and key spaces are in very good or excellent condition, but some damage, technical systems and maintenance/restoration issues require continued attention.
entries.management: A management framework exists, but the effectiveness of the new management body still depends on operational procedures and sustained long-term resources.
entries.threats_addressed: Risks such as heating systems, wear, functional adaptations and resource sustainability are identified but require continued management and monitoring.

entries_en.conservation: ICOMOS considers that the conservation and monitoring of the nominated serial property are ensured by a suitable mechanism. In terms of documentation, the use of Heritage Building Information Model (HBIM) and detailed digital inventories ensures a detailed baseline for any future work. Conservation is managed by the Superintendences of the MiC, thus ensuring methodological consistency of any interventions. A system of continuous monitoring, based on quantitative and qualitative indicators, enables to assess the state of conservation and the effectiveness of the management plan. ICOMOS considers that it would be advisable that the monitoring system is adapted for easy integration of its outcomes into the Periodic Reporting questionnaire.
entries_en.management:
In summary, ICOMOS considers that the nominated serial property benefits from an appropriate level of legal protection, as well as regional and local protection mechanisms that safeguard the attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value.
The effectiveness of the management body created as part of the agreement between the regions involved in the nomination and the MiC will be demonstrated by the development of operational procedures and the long-term availability of resources allocated to the body responsible for coordinating and implementing the management plan. The latter provides a framework capable of ensuring the conservation of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value, provided the funding will be sustained beyond the first period from 2025 to 2028.

payload.summary_en: Based on the nomination dossier, and according to the State Party, the key attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value can be grouped as follows: unity and conceptual principles underlying a coherent 17th century system of bulwarked border towns along the Raia; innovation and complexity shown in the adaptive designs of the fortresses, advanced artillery integration and largescale construction under limited resources; and the precocious rehearsal of Vauban’s “pre-carré”, seen in its dense double-line frontier fortification and adoption of advances in European defensive concepts. The nominated property is presented as a series comprising three component parts, each of which, the State Party argues, embodies the proposed Outstanding Universal Value and its associated attributes. Regarding the serial approach and the selection of these three fortresses from the 143 forming the Raia, the State Party clarified in November 2025 that the choice was guided by a unifying concept, aiming to incorporate the most representative Portuguese bulwarked fortresses along the border. In February 2026, it further specified that this nomination does not advocate for an open-ended strategy for the inclusion of additional bulwarked fortresses, and that these fortresses, together with Évora and Elvas, constitute an exemplary representation of the cultural heritage under consideration. The State Party also highlighted that the three selected fortresses are part of the only sixteen fortified ensembles located on the Portuguese side of the Raia that are considered to be in a very good or good overall condition. The three component parts are all located within contiguous major hydrographic basins. They also represent three different morphological settings: plateau/plain for Almeida, mountain for Marvão and riverside for Valença. However, despite the detailed historical information provided, ICOMOS considers that the nomination dossier does not clearly demonstrate how the fortresses, either as a general system or in relation to the three selected component parts, function together within the Raia. As a result, the overall structure and functioning of the defensive system remain insufficiently articulated. This raises concerns as to whether the three component parts, taken together, can adequately represent the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. It is not demonstrated how this initial series justifies the proposed Outstanding Universal Value, nor how potential future…

payload.summary_en: ICOMOS does not consider that the comparative analysis justifies consideration of this property for the World Heritage List at this stage, as it remains insufficiently developed in relation to the wider system of bastioned fortresses along the Raia and lacks a convincing demonstration of the distinctiveness and representativity of the nominated component parts in both regional and global contexts.

entries_en.integrity:
ICOMOS considers that, individually, the three nominated component parts retain a high degree of integrity, with their fortifications and key elements largely preserved, though each shows localised impacts from historical events, urban development, or landscape modifications. 

Since the criteria for justifying the proposed Outstanding Universal Value have not been demonstrated at this stage, attributes of Outstanding Universal Value cannot be confirmed and integrity, as defined by the Operational Guidelines for the Implementation of the World Heritage Convention, is not demonstrated at this stage.
entries_en.authenticity: Since the criteria for justifying the proposed Outstanding Universal Value have not been demonstrated at this stage, attributes of Outstanding Universal Value cannot be confirmed and authenticity, as defined by the Operational Guidelines for the Implementation of the World Heritage Convention, is not demonstrated at this stage.
entries_en.integrity_authenticity_conclusion: In conclusion, ICOMOS considers that the conditions of integrity and authenticity of the individual component parts have generally been met but that the integrity and authenticity of the nominated series as a whole have not been demonstrated, due to concerns over the selection of component parts and the inability of the nominated series to fully represent the diversity, typologies, and historical complexity of the Raia.

entries.protection_property: The nominated property has a robust legal protection framework and generally appropriate planning tools, but arrangements remain not fully operational.
entries.protection_buffer_zone: Planning tools exist, but further coordination and World Heritage-specific impact-assessment methodologies are needed for the serial property and its settings.
entries.conservation: The deferral recommendation calls for documenting attributes and conservation state of any selected component parts, indicating current conservation evidence is not yet sufficient for the revised serial approach.
entries.management: The effectiveness of the management system is uncertain because it depends on formal establishment and operational capacity of AFAR and lacks some key stakeholders.
entries.threats_addressed: HIA, risk management, interpretation and visitor management still require strengthening; arrangements are not yet fully coordinated for long-term management.

entries_en.conservation: ICOMOS considers that the nominated fortresses are well documented and that ongoing conservation, restoration, and adaptive reuse measures, supported by a clear management plan, contribute to their safeguarding. Monitoring coordinated by AFAR is intended to ensure systematic assessment of authenticity, integrity, and territorial impacts, but its effectiveness will depend on the effective establishment and functioning of this structure.
entries_en.management: In summary, ICOMOS considers that the nominated property benefits from a robust legal protection framework and generally appropriate planning tools at national and municipal levels. However, the effectiveness of the management system remains uncertain at this stage, as it depends on the formal establishment and operational capacity of AFAR, and is weakened by the absence of key stakeholders such as ESTAMO within its structure. While a comprehensive management plan and monitoring mechanisms are in place, further work is needed to strengthen coordination, adopt World Heritage-specific impact assessment methodologies, develop a risk management strategy, and improve interpretation and visitor management. Overall, the protection and management arrangements provide a basis for conservation but are not yet fully operational or sufficiently coordinated to ensure the effective long-term management of the nominated serial property as a whole.

payload.brief_zh: 本项目未给出推荐OUV的Brief synthesis，通常因推荐为Referral/Deferral/Non-inscription或边界调整。
payload.brief_en:
Brief description

Located in Galicia in northern Spain, Ribeira Sacra is a large waterscape. It is bounded by the deep river canyons at the confluence of the Miño and Sil rivers and is characterised by steep slopes and rugged terrain. The property, nominated as a cultural landscape, has been shaped by the forces of water, and by the management of water for more than 1,500 years of continuous occupation and adaptation. Distinctive adaptations include the pattern of micro-plots called cavaduras that date back to the Middle Ages when distinct units marked by watercourses known as parishes were established; and steep terraces known as socalcos that support a mix of crops, chestnuts and vineyards. The early importance of monastic traditions is demonstrated by many cave sanctuaries, churches, abbeys and monasteries. Evidence of water management technologies span the periods of human occupation and include archaeological sites, drainage systems associated with the terraces, fountains, canals, weirs, crossings, bridges, water mills and industrial power stations. Local traditions of “water culture” include myths and legends, toponymy, and the legacies of eremitic and monastic practices.

payload.summary_en: According to the State Party, the nominated property is proposed as an interconnected set of attributes centred on water, rather than a list of individual attributes. These are described as follows in additional information provided in February 2026: the river canyons and valleys sculpted by water and the numerous watercourses; the water and energy heritage sustained by watercourses [including water mills, power stations and dams dating from the Middle Ages to the mid-20th century]; the enduring legacy of traditional terraced farming systems on the steep slopes of river canyons, as a feat of hydraulic engineering; the unique land-use and land-tenure (smallholdings) structure, marked and defined by the river system and watercourses; the eremitic and monastic heritage that shaped the distinctive cultural features landscape and water culture since its origins; and the survival of a rich intangible heritage associated with water culture.

entries_en.integrity: The integrity of the nominated cultural landscape is based on the state of conservation and the way major pressures are managed, as well as the intactness of the material evidence. The State Party has drawn the boundaries in order to include an area of sufficient scale to represent the features of the waterscape, and points to the very large number of watercourses, terraced slopes, and microplots. According to the State Party, there are no major threats impacting on the nominated property. No issues have been identified with the boundaries of the nominated cultural landscape. However, since the criterion for justifying the proposed Outstanding Universal Value has not been demonstrated, attributes of Outstanding Universal Value cannot be identified and integrity, as defined by the Operational Guidelines for the Implementation of the World Heritage Convention, is not demonstrated. ICOMOS considers that the integrity of the nominated property has not been demonstrated.
entries_en.authenticity: ICOMOS considers that since the criterion for justifying the proposed Outstanding Universal Value has not been demonstrated, attributes of Outstanding Universal Value cannot be identified and authenticity, as defined by the Operational Guidelines for the Implementation of the World Heritage Convention, is not demonstrated.
entries_en.integrity_authenticity_conclusion: In conclusion, ICOMOS considers that the conditions of integrity and authenticity of the nominated property have not been met.
entries_en.boundaries: In conclusion, ICOMOS considers that the proposed boundaries and buffer zone are adequate and are aligned with the legal protection and management system.

entries.protection_property: ICOMOS considers the legal protection adequate; the non-inscription recommendation relates to OUV rather than protection failure.
entries.protection_buffer_zone: The management and planning arrangements are considered adequate, with no major buffer-zone failure driving the recommendation.
entries.management: In summary, ICOMOS considers that the legal protection and management system are adequate, although formal processes for Heritage Impact Assessment should be established. The management plan and sustainable tourism plan provide relevant strategies and actions for the conservation, safeguarding and presentation of the nominated property.
entries.threats_addressed: Threats are addressed through relevant strategies, but formal HIA should be established for future impact control.

entries_en.conservation: ICOMOS considers that that the documentation, conservation measures, and monitoring of the nominated cultural landscape are adequate.

payload.summary_en: Based on the nomination dossier, and according to the State Party, the key attributes of the potential Outstanding Universal Value are: the remains of the fortification system, including ramparts, towers and gates; the domestic, religious and administrative structures within the fortified enclosure; the hydraulic installations consisting of rock-cut cisterns and water channels; the necropolises with rock-cut tombs; the quarries associated with construction activities; the underground rock-cut spaces, including the structure identified as a Mithraeum; and features associated with historic circulation routes. ICOMOS notes that the name of the nominated property, “Zerzevan Castle and Mithraeum”, reflects the interpretative framework adopted by the State Party. This terminology was discussed during the evaluation process, with ICOMOS expressing its concern that the functional and typological identification of certain elements, particularly the underground rock-cut space interpreted as a Mithraeum, remains subject to differing interpretations based on the information currently available. ICOMOS considers that the way in which attributes are interpreted has a direct impact on the potential Outstanding Universal Value.

entries_en.authenticity: Since the criteria for justifying the potential Outstanding Universal Value have not been demonstrated, attributes of Outstanding Universal Value cannot be identified and authenticity, as defined by the Operational Guidelines for the Implementation of the World Heritage Convention, is not demonstrated.
entries_en.integrity_authenticity_conclusion: In conclusion, ICOMOS considers that the conditions of integrity and authenticity of the nominated property have not been met.
entries_en.boundaries: ICOMOS considers that, while the nominated area covers the main concentration of archaeological remains, the exclusion of certain documented attributes from the nominated property affects the wholeness of the nominated property. The rationale for the current delineation is not sufficiently articulated in relation to the attributes put forward by the State Party. ICOMOS further considers that while the buffer zone provides an added layer of protection for the nominated property, the presence of visually intrusive elements limits its effectiveness in fully supporting the potential Outstanding Universal Value.

entries.protection_property: The property benefits from legal protection, an approved management plan, established institutional responsibilities and active conservation/documentation programmes.
entries.protection_buffer_zone: Further strengthening is needed to control infrastructure impacts and visitor pressures in and around the site.
entries.conservation: Active conservation and documentation programmes exist, but stronger integration and control of infrastructure/visitor impacts are needed.
entries.management: Management exists but needs clearer coordination, decision-making arrangements and a more fully integrated system.
entries.threats_addressed: Visitor pressure, infrastructure impacts and community participation issues are recognised but require stronger control and integration.

entries_en.conservation: ICOMOS considers that the information provided on conservation measures and monitoring demonstrates that substantial documentation, condition assessment and intervention planning have been undertaken for a number of attributes of the nominated property. At the same time, further work is needed to ensure more comprehensive baseline documentation, a more fully coordinated conservation framework across all attributes, and a monitoring system more clearly focused on conservation effectiveness and site vulnerabilities.
entries_en.management: In summary, ICOMOS considers that the nominated property benefits from legal protection, an approved management plan, established institutional responsibilities, active conservation and documentation programmes and a growing visitor and outreach framework. Visitor access, interpretation and promotion measures have been developed, and local communities derive some benefits through employment and public activities associated with the site. At the same time, further strengthening is needed to ensure a more fully integrated management system, clearer coordination and decision-making arrangements, more effective control of visitor pressures and infrastructure impacts, and more inclusive participation of local communities in management processes.

payload.brief_en: The set of housing estates in the Berlin Modern Style provides outstanding testimony to the implementation of housing policies during the period 1910-1933 and especially during the Weimar Republic, when the city of Berlin was characterized by its political, social, cultural and technical progressiveness. The housing estates reflect, with the highest degree of quality, the combination of urbanism, architecture, garden design and aesthetic research typical of early 20th century modernism, as well as the application of new hygienic and social standards. Some of the most prominent leading architects of German modernism were involved in the design and construction of the properties; they developed innovative urban, building and flat-roof typologies, technical solutions and aesthetic achievements.

payload.summary_en:
Based on the nomination dossier, and according to the State Party, while the nominated extension shares the common attributes of the World Heritage property, it also contributes to the whole series with the following attributes: the hybrid urban typology that combines the garden city elements and apartment buildings through the integration of the buildings into a wooded landscape; the fast connection to the city centre via the modernist underground station; the shopping arcades within the station conceived as a commercial, supply and community centre; the exclusive use of flat roofs; the extensive use of colour throughout the buildings and building elements; and the sculptural design of the façades as reflected in the windows and jamb storeys.

... ICOMOS concurs with the identified attributes proposed by the State Party for the nominated extension, and considers that they complement those of the World Heritage series.

mentions.summary:
Integrity
Waldsiedlung Zehlendorf significantly enhances the integrity of the World Heritage serial property through attributes such as the integration of green spaces and a forest with housing, a bespoke modernist transportation infrastructure combined with a community and commercial centre, the exclusive use of flat roofing throughout the estate, and a specific façade colour scheme.

payload.summary_en: ICOMOS considers that the methodology adopted for the comparative analysis is sound, and that the conclusion of the State Party is justified. ICOMOS considers that the comparative analysis justifies consideration of the nominated extension for the World Heritage List, as a component part to the Berlin Modernism Housing Estates.

entries.authenticity:
涉及局部重建：A few buildings were damaged or destroyed during the Second World War. They were repaired or rebuilt in the 1950s to habitable condition, but little attention was paid to their authenticity. However, the number of such cases is limited. In addition, some building elements and interior layouts of the residential units were changed from the original designs. The cinema in the shopping arcades at the railway station was replaced by a grocery store in the 1960s. An escalator and a lift were installed, which are not part of the original design. These past alterations have affected the authenticity of the nominated extension.

对历史不当干预的调整：
Since the 1970s, restorations have been undertaken with a particular focus on the façades, the plaster and its colouring, and building elements such as doors and windows. These activities have followed guidelines available at that time to ensure these works and adaptations to the buildings were in line with conservation standards for historic monuments. However, these did not prevent deviations from or alterations to the original designs. Since then, these past alterations have been assessed and, in some cases, partly removed. Thanks to regular efforts to mitigate this issue, the authenticity of the nominated extension has been improving. To preserve the authenticity of the nominated extension in the future, recent Guidelines for row houses, apartment buildings, outdoor spaces and streets have been developed and put in place, in addition the existing legal framework and conservation plans.

entries_en.integrity:
The nominated extension is of adequate size, encompassing the complete historical urban area of Waldsiedlung Zehlendorf, including all residential and functional buildings, green spaces, gardens, public areas and streets. All the factors affecting the conditions of the nominated extension are under control. Waldsiedlung Zehlendorf significantly enhances the integrity of the World Heritage serial property through attributes such as the integration of green spaces and a forest with housing, a bespoke modernist transportation infrastructure combined with a community and commercial centre, the exclusive use of flat roofing throughout the estate, and a specific façade colour scheme. 

ICOMOS considers that the integrity of the nominated extension has been demonstrated.
entries_en.authenticity: ICOMOS considers that, overall, the authenticity of the nominated extension is adequate, and has been enhanced by the restoration work carried out after the 1970s.
entries_en.integrity_authenticity_conclusion: In conclusion, ICOMOS considers that the conditions of integrity and authenticity of the nominated extension have been met, and reinforce those of the already-inscribed property.
entries_en.boundaries: ICOMOS considers that the boundaries of the nominated extension are adequate and that the proposed buffer zone provides an effective added layer of protection to Waldsiedlung Zehlendorf.

entries.protection_property: ICOMOS considers legal protection of the nominated extension effective.
entries.protection_buffer_zone: Protection and management of the extension and its setting are adequate, with no outstanding buffer-zone concern in the final evaluation.
entries.conservation: Restoration and conservation are well documented and implemented, with an adequate conservation plan and monitoring baseline.
entries.management: ICOMOS considers the management system adequate; visitor management is satisfactory and communities are involved in decision-making.
entries.threats_addressed: Fire, climate and renewable-energy risks are identified and addressed through protection concepts and regulations.

entries_en.conservation: ICOMOS considers that the level of documentation is satisfactory. The conservation measures in place are adequate. ICOMOS considers that while the monitoring mechanism is adequate overall, more indicators should be developed for monitoring the state of conservation of the nominated extension. Furthermore, community involvement in the monitoring process should be encouraged.
entries_en.management: In summary, ICOMOS considers that the legal protection of the nominated extension is effective, and that its management system is adequate. Visitor management is satisfactory, and local communities have been involved in decision-making processes.

payload.brief_en:
Manaus, founded in 1669 as an isolated fort on the Rio Negro, underwent a spectacular transformation in the 19th century, thanks to the rise of rubber. From a simple village, by around 1900 it had become a prosperous town with 50,000 inhabitants, nicknamed the "Paris of the Tropics". Fortunes amassed from rubber exploitation financed Haussmann-style urban modernisation. 

The Teatro Amazonas, with its eclectic Neo-Renaissance style, is a prime symbol of this ambition. The extensive use of European materials (Glasgow iron, Carrara marble, Murano chandeliers, French furniture) reflects a desire to position Manaus within the international circuits of luxury and culture. The interior, decorated by European and Brazilian artists, reinforces this cosmopolitan aesthetic. 

The Largo de São Sebastião, with its paving evoking the "meeting of the waters" and its monument celebrating the opening of the ports, showcases the economic power of the city and serves as a social showcase for the rubber elites.

payload.summary_en: Based on the nomination dossier and the additional information, and according to the State Party, the key attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value can be grouped as follows: the form, design and materials of the architecture, which combine European eclecticism, late 19th century Industrial Revolution technologies and materials imported from Europe with Amazonian artistic expression, techniques and materials; predominance in the urban landscape and adaptation to the environment, where the theatres and associated public spaces are symbolic of the socio-political and cultural transformations of the “golden age of rubber” and their role in the structuring and urban development of the cities; and the multiple uses and functions of the property, which range from the performing arts to the preservation of intangible cultural heritage, such as the Boi-Bumbá of the State of Amazonas and the Carimbó of the State of Pará, thus acting as a catalyst for artistic education and economic development. In its interim report, ICOMOS requested additional information on the historical narrative presented by the State Party and the values it embodies. In the additional information of February 2026, the State Party clarified that although the Rubber Cycle was relatively short, it generated unprecedented concentrations of wealth, creating the conditions for ambitious projects that transformed the urban landscape, cultural life and identity of the region. Belém, founded in 1616, was already the administrative and trading centre of the Amazon. With the export of rubber, it strengthened its position within the international economic networks. The Theatro da Paz, in Neo-classical style, symbolises this cosmopolitan openness. Manaus developed as a centre for the collection and export of latex, and as the capital of the new province of Amazonas from 1850. The eclectic-style Teatro Amazonas embodies the aim of making Manaus a cultural capital in the heart of the rainforest. Belém and Manaus are both rivals and interdependent. According to the State Party, the two theatres function as complementary nodes on the same artistic circuit and embody the architectural translation of an ephemeral extractive economy, a modernisation in the heart of the rainforest and a selective appropriation of European models. 288 ICOMOS considers the justification proposed by the State Party for the nominated serial property to be unconvincing. mentions.source_en: Integrity According to the State Party, the integrity of the nominated serial property is based on the combination of European eclecticism, industrial metal structures and Amazonian materials. It is also based on the capacity of the theatres to symbolise the "rubber boom" and to shape the urban landscape. Finally, the integrity of the nominated serial property also lies in the continuity of artistic functions combined with the participation of traditional cultural expressions and the creation of economic opportunities for the local population. As a serial nomination, integrity is also a measure of whether the component parts individually and collectively contain all the attributes necessary to express in a substantial way the proposed Outstanding Universal Value of the nominated property as a whole, and how each component part contributes to that value. Integrity is furthermore a measure of the intactness of the attributes. ICOMOS considers that the justification proposed by the State Party for the nominated serial property is not demonstrated.

entries_en.boundaries: There are no permanent populations within the perimeter of the property. The population of the buffer zones could not be estimated by the State Party. The nominated theatres are the public property of the States of Pará and Amazonas, while the associated public spaces are the property of the cities of Belém and Manaus. The boundaries of component part 001, the Theatro da Paz and Praça da República, are bounded to the north by Rua Osvaldo Cruz, to the west by Avenida Presidente Vargas/Rua Arcipreste Manoel Teodoro, to the south by Rua Gama Abreu and Avenida Nossa Senhora de Nazaré, and to the east by Avenida Assis de Vasconcelos. Its buffer zone is delimited to the west by 292 the historic centre of the city, which includes the districts of Cidade Velha and Campina, and to the east by the first sectors of the expansion of the city along the main axis formed of wide, tree-lined avenues, the “boulevards”, such as Avenida Nossa Senhora de Nazaré.

payload.brief_zh: 本项目未给出推荐OUV的Brief synthesis，通常因推荐为Referral/Deferral/Non-inscription或边界调整。
payload.brief_en:
Brief description

Mount Olympus, the highest mountain in Greece (2,918 metres above sea level), rises on the border between Macedonia and Thessaly, within the Regional Units of Pieria and Larissa. Its microclimate, resulting from its proximity to the sea and abrupt elevation, produces a remarkable diversity in terrain, climate, and vegetation. The imposing form and cloud-shrouded peaks of the massif, often accompanied by storms, have long inspired awe and reverence. In this landscape the ancient Greeks situated the abode of their Twelve Olympian Gods, with Zeus as their sovereign, as well as the Muses and Graces. According to the ancient Greek poet Hesiod, Zeus triumphed over Cronus and the Titans here, establishing his dominion over gods, demigods, and mortals. On the lower slopes of Mount Olympus, the city of Dion was established in the 5th century BCE with temples and sanctuaries orientated towards Mount Olympus, the archaeological remains of which exemplify the cult of the Olympian Gods. Through the writings of Hesiod and Homer, Mount Olympus became a central point of reference for Greek mythology throughout the Greek and Roman worlds, rendering Mount Olympus an enduring emblem of Greek civilisation.

payload.summary_en: Based on the nomination dossier and the additional information, and according to the State Party, the key attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value are the geological features and geomorphological phenomena (“geosites”) to which mythological narratives are repeatedly anchored. Further attributes are the traces left by people serving and worshipping these gods on and around the mountain. These traces include, for example, the Archaeological site of Dion (component part 2) and the St. Antonios peak (part of component part 1), both of which also form part of this narrative. More physical evidence of the worship of the gods will probably be identified through future systematic archaeological surveys in the higher mountain regions. An associated intangible attribute is the impact the myth of Mount Olympus still has today as the abode of the gods, and Mount Olympus as a topos expressed in works of art, literature and the use of the name Olympus. Originally, the State Party had not defined specific tangible aspects of the mountain as cultural attributes of the proposed Outstanding Universal Value. In response to a request for clarification by ICOMOS, the State Party submitted additional information in November 2025 and reiterated that the intangible cultural values are linked to all the natural attributes of the mountain itself rather than to any particular spot or viewshed of the mountain. In other words, the mountain is valued as a dominant single entity within which a multiplicity of peaks, valleys and other natural features are associated with specific aspects of the life of the ancient Greek gods. In the additional information of February 2026, the State Party defined twenty geosites, several of which are linked explicitly to a cultural narrative focused on the connection between the mountain and the myth. They serve as examples that help to clarify the connection between the myth and the mountain. However, according to the State Party, the value rests not in each individual location, but in the combination of these sites and vistas that together form the scene or stage for the performance of the Olympian Gods. Other geosites are viewpoints that allow one to observe the awe-inspiring peaks of the mountain. As part of the additional information of November 2025, the State Party proposed to strengthen the identification 314 of tangible attributes associated with criterion (vi) by including the archaeological remains of temples and sanctuaries at…
